### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-29T04:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/early-periods-shortened-cycles-explain.32205/post-366082>

All opsins are linked to Vitamin A. Vitamin A is the bomb that causes the destruction. Vitamin A = ROR and RXR receptors.

In my decentralized thesis, the transition metals chelated by melanin, namely, **Copper (Cu), Iron (Fe), Manganese (Mn), and Molybdenum (Mo)** are not just atomic bystanders; they are the functional "hardware" that physically change our mitochondrial biology in specific ways and this allows Vitamin A to properly interact with the Retinoic Acid Receptor (RAR) and Retinoid X Receptor (RXR) to translate mitochondrial light into genetic action.

These metals interact with the RAR/RXR pathway through the following mechanisms. the ones that are big for your lady are Fe and Mn when it comes to her cycle issues.

To support hormone function, Cytochrome P450 (**CYP**) enzymes in humans rely on specific metal ions to catalyze the complex synthesis and breakdown of sex steroids.

-   **Iron (Fe):** This is the **most critical**metal ion for CYP function. It has to be in the Fe+2 = state not the +3 state.
    -   **Catalytic Center:** Every CYP enzyme contains a **heme-iron center**. The iron atom cycles between oxidation states (**Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺**) to activate oxygen, allowing the enzyme to chemically modify hormones. _**Only the +2 state carries oxygen.**_
    -   **Hormone Synthesis:** Iron-dependent CYPs (like **CYP11A1**) initiate the conversion of cholesterol into pregnenolone, the "mother hormone" for all sex steroids. Read the Hormone 101 and 102 blogs on pregenenolone syndrome.
    -   **Electron Transfer:** In mitochondria, CYPs receive electrons from **iron-sulfur proteins** (like [adrenodoxin](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3538421/)) to power their reactions.
-   **Zinc (Zn):** While not the central metal in the heme itself, zinc is a vital **structural and catalytic cofactor** for many enzymes that work alongside CYPs.
    -   **Enzyme Stability:** Zinc helps stabilize the tertiary structure of proteins. Studies have shown that **Zinc(II) ions** can directly modulate the structure and activity of **CYP3A4**, a key enzyme for clearing excess estrogen.
    -   Did you know melanin acts as a significant **reservoir and chelator** for zinc (**Zn²⁺**) ions in the human body too? Now you can see why nnEMF that destroys melanin plays are role in her fucked up cycles.
    -   Because the binding affinity is only "moderate," melanin does not just lock zinc away; it can **store and release** it as needed by the cell. This makes pigmented tissues (like the eyes and skin) major storage sites for zinc. What happens when blue light or nnEMF destroy that melanin? You got a period shortening and a major fertility problem don't you?
-   **Manganese (Mn) and Selenium (Se):** These essential metals are involved in the broader regulatory network of the **[hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0147651325013077)**, which controls the menstrual cycle. Manganese acts as a cofactor for enzymes involved in antioxidant defense, protecting CYP proteins from oxidative damage during hormone metabolism. Se is taken care of by my Epi-paleo Rx.

**Manganese (Mn): The RXR Stress Buffer**

Manganese interacts primarily through **RXR-mediated neuroprotection**. So when you got blu elight liberating Vitamin A Mn-SOD inside your mitochondrial matrix still allow you to make proper UPEs to have a kid. When you lose that ability, you cannot. That is where your girl is now. Now you can see how Vitamin issues play a huge role in CYP biology.

**Mitochondrial Protection:** RXRα/PPARγ heterodimers (which require the same RXR partner as RAR) directly upregulate **Mn-SOD (SOD2)** in your mitochondrial matrix to reduce mitochondrial stress and make COHERENT UPEs (not chaotic ones like she is making) that are associated with coherence and proper ROS/RNS signaling.

**Information Integrity:** Since Mn-SOD is the only enzyme that can neutralize matrix superoxide without the "Fenton fire" of iron, its regulation by RXR ensures that the UPE signal remains coherent. Manganese isn't just a cofactor; it is the "output" of a healthy RXR signal that protects the system from its own metabolic "noise." MELANIN CONTROL THE QUANTUM AMOUNT OF Mn needed in this system.

### Jacks — 2026-01-31T19:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/accessing-patreon-blogs-and-content.32209/post-366172>

The Patreon blogs are the same as the Blogs on the Optimal Klubs website - just housed on different websites. You have to subscribe to Patreon to access them on that website at a cost of $5 or so a month - but no need to subscribe if you are a Gold Member as all the Blogs can be found when logged into optimalklubs.com under the first tab listed and listed as Blogs. **Cysteine's Evolutionary Journey: From Ancient Thiol to Life's Master Regulator : MELANIN**

In the Archean era, cysteine began as a simple thiol molecule. During the Great Oxidation Event (GOE), rising oxygen levels transformed it into a key sensor for nutrients and light. This sensing ability drove the evolution of the family of melanins, pigments structured around cysteine, that enabled cells to recycle phosphorus through the pentose phosphate pathway (PPP) and generate NADPH.

With abundant NADPH, cells could activate TORC1, enabling decentralized control over growth and apoptosis (programmed cell death).Early cells used the PPP to neutralize reactive oxygen species (ROS). This process fostered the evolution of melanin and ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs), culminating in cells generating "UPE light from nothing", a biophotonic spark that fueled the Cambrian explosion and life's rapid diversification in the fossil record.

The overarching story is how Life's decentralization occured with precision from LUCA's primitive biochemistry to eukaryotic light-sensing optics, all hinges on cysteine's quantum switch embedded in melanins. This switch, acting as a protective "shield" (via pathways like Nrf2), can be disrupted by non-native electromagnetic fields (nnEMF), but restored through UV-A exposure, cold therapy, and breathwork—rebuilding biological "wealth in time" against centralized chaos. Read and be ready to be stunned. It is a story Darwin could not tell.

Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels

14.  Complete Vitamin D panel

16.  Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
17.  No panel is complete without an ASI and salivary melatonin level. ?

### Rudolf — 2026-04-04T17:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32220/post-370252>

**Progress** with my girlfriends chronic intestine problems (leaky gut):
I got her now to eat fish. No shellfish so far, but predetor sea fish with more firm meat. (I increased my seafood intake big time, including oysters, caviar, mussels and shrimps).
We sleep on magneticos. She is dreaming more and sometimes complaining about it. I think I sleep better. We are more strict with our blue light blocking glasses after sunset, we rarely use blue light LEDs anymore at home. We are out a lot for morning sun light. We are grounding (I'm more rigorous than she does) each day. I got _TOLOS earth runne_rs and ordered some for her, too. I also got some cool tan t-shits.
I Progressed _CT_ to 10 mins + some additional "bathtub diving" in the end (mostly do CT indoor in the morning so far with some epsom salt in the water). The shivering is still intense and it starts about 2mins after I got out. She does face dips and cold showering. She does not seem to be more cold adapted yet. Although we have cooler room temp in the sleeping room now without her freezing.

She still shows signs of _may be some **demyelination**_: paresthesia / tingling of the tongue, parts of the face and tip of the nose (probably trigeminus), feet and sometimes hands.

We reduced nnEMF as much as possible (in a city flat) and plan to move spring next year to the countryside (even thought about moving further south).
I ordered _Preventa 45 DDW_ and we drink it daily, I mix it with normal mountain spring water. I use a 80ppm concentration for her (I only use tiny amounts myself).
She was sent to do an MRI Selink by her gastroenterologist, there were no signs of inflammation on the intestine walls, there were two strange findings: **Very low location of the gallbladder** and **very low location of the colon** - may be normal anatomic variant. The radiologist does not to seem very confident about it... I never encountered sth similar in my patients (dogs, cats, ...).
Since start of the diet (5 weeks ago), _calprotectin_ values got better. She is _positive_ for **_candida antibodies_** and also _positive_ **_fecal candida culture_**. We did not start anything else about it. We do the low to none carbohydrates, so may be candida gets away - if it is a real diagnosis (not sure).
We plan to do the extended blood work up suggested in the Eli-Paelo-Rx, still looking for a doc who does everything. I'm still fine, despite getting vaccinated twice. Hope it stays like this..
By the way - I recognized a reduced incidence (to none) of those strange clouds over Germany. There is more sun or natural clouds. Anyone else noticed this since the start of the year?
If anyone has some tips, suggestions, I would appreciate them very much.
And thank you everyone for your kind input here in the forum!

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-05T02:16:48+0300

> [flavin0 said:](/goto/post?id=366977)
>
> I downloaded that bitcoin document examining discrete vs. continuous time. It reminds me of how theologians call out a distinction between two words for "time" in Greek: _chronos_ and _kairos_. _Chronos_ is the notion of chronological time that scientists sort of take for granted, while kairos refers to something totally different. I am not a theologian but it seems like _kairos_ is about events in some grand schedule that probably serve a highly specific purpose, or _telos._
>
> I am currently exploring Dr. Fosbury's claim of the G-class star that is our Sun. Apparently when hydrogen picks up another electron to become H- it has a consequence in the amount of infrared that arrives here on the surface of the Earth. I guess it is a little murky what's going on up there in the Sun considering Dr. Kruse seems to believe in a lattice vs simple thermal model of how the Sun, and even Dr. Fosbury admits there is a lot of non-equilibrium dynamics up there anyways. But I think at this point in my understanding it is becoming clearer that the electromagnetic profile of the solar irradiance has a truly unique role in shaping life on Earth, hence there is no alternative to sunlight.
>
> If even God created on a daily schedule (on the first day, it was light, second day I think atmosphere, etc) I think it would be wise for us to follow suit.

This idea bridges Information Theory and Thermodynamics, so I like it. You’ve hit on the distinction between the Tick (Chronos) and the Phase (Kairos).
In the Bitcoin document, discrete time is the "Block Height", it’s a sequence of events that creates a "before" and "after." I called it Newtonian time = time is absolute. That is Chronos. Continuous time is the "Network Propagation", the fluid, non-equilibrium flow of energy. That is Kairos. This is Einsteinian Time = time is relative

**1\. Chronos vs. Kairos: The Biological Layers**
Chronos (The Base Layer): This is the DNA/RNA. It is the "discrete" parts list. It sits there like a cold Bitcoin wallet, waiting for an instruction. It is the chronological record of our ancestors, but it has no "torque" on its own.
Kairos (The Operating System): This is Circadian Biology. It is the "Grand Schedule" or telos. It is the timing of when a gene is expressed. Kairos is the "Right Moment", which is the specific window where the Sun’s IR-A thins the water lattice enough for a proton to hop. Without Kairos, the DNA (Chronos) is just a "seized" library.

**2\. The H−Ion: The Sun’s "Infrared Valve"**
Dr. Fosbury’s work on the Negative Hydrogen Ion (H−) really ties back to Robataille work on the electric sun model. Robataille work is the "smoking gun" for why the Sun is an "Infinite Ensemble" and a light bulb is "Quantized Poverty."
The Physics: In the Sun’s photosphere, a neutral Hydrogen atom picks up a second electron to become H−. This ion is extremely "fragile." When it breaks apart, it releases a massive, continuous spectrum of Infrared (IR-A).
The Consequence: This H− transition is what creates the "Goldilocks" IR-A profile that arrives on Earth. It is the specific frequency that thins the metabolic water lattice (the 160-dielectric).
The Electric Sun: If the Sun is a Lattice (the Electric Sun model) rather than a simple fusion ball, then it is a Coherent Oscillator. It isn't just "burning"; it is broadcasting a timing signal (Kairos) to the Earth's water lattice. That is why I favor the electric sun model.

**3\. The Solar Irradiance as the "Unique Master"**
TINA—there is no alternative to sunlight. never will be either but I am not so sure Fosbury or his buddy Zimmerman think this way.
Artificial Light (The Countable Set): It lacks the H− transition. It has no "Infrared Valve." It provides the Chronos (you can see the clock) but it has zero Kairos (it can't trigger the "Right Moment" for the bicarb loop or the 4th ventricle vortex).
Sunlight (The Uncountable Set): Because of the non-equilibrium dynamics of H−, the Sun provides the "Non-Equilibrium Torque" needed to move the 150 ppm deuterium silt. It is the only light source that can "un-weld" the DNA base layer.

**My Decentralized Mechanic's Diagnosis:**
Modernity has tried to replace Kairos with Chronos. We have clocks everywhere, but no "timing." We live in "Discrete Time" (digital screens/nnEMF) while our biology is designed for the "Continuous Lattice" of the Sun.

### LHorn25 — 2026-02-04T00:04:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-366326>

My name is Lianne and I am 43 years old, struggled with infertility for many years but have made peace with that. The the good news was it was the catalyst that started my healing journey. I always considered myself pretty healthy, so it was shocking to me when I could not get pregnant. Had 3 ovarian cysts removed over 20 years but was repeatedly told it would not impact my fertility. I did not meet my husband till I was 37 so advanced age played a role I am sure. I was vegetarian/vegan for a long time and finally stopped once my hair started falling out. Have been eating meat since 2021 and feel a million times better than before.

I am an acupuncturist with my own practice for 14 years and finding it tougher and tougher to get patients better. Plus treating under blue light- now it makes sense. Trying to change the environment I practice in but it's challenging. I am half way through my acupuncture doctorate program when about 2 months ago came across Jack Kruse. I literally have been studying his work more than my schoolwork! Honestly this program is not what I thought and very much in line with centralized medicine. I won't bore you guys with the details. I tell all my patients about Jack's work and try to get them to go outside to view sunrise/set to start.

I am proudly out there in zero degrees looking to the east now every single morning. People think i'm nuts. Just bought blue blockers, Iris software, went to my local spring today for the first time to get quality water, and bought a bunch of red bulbs. I eat salmon, eggs and lamb burgers for breakfast and am mostly keto. Tried cold showers and don't think i'm ready because when my cycle came I had issues so maybe face dunking to start. Some questions I have and would appreciate any guidance:

1\. I am in upstate NY at the 42nd latitude, how can I ground in the mornings with zeros degrees and snow on the ground?

2\. I know CT is rec esp for us who live in the North but i found it messed up my menstrual cycle and oxidized bleeding to almost black and low back pain and cramping. In Chinese Medicine we tell patients to avoid cold bc it stagnates the blood... so CT is not something CM would promote and i am trying to wrap my head around it. Since I had such a negative experience, now how to tell my patients to start doing this?! I believe it works but I just need to reconcile the how as it goes against such ancient medicine where nature was the basis.

3\. Infrared sauna blanket-yes or no? got a Brookstone blanket for Xmas but not sure if it is a good idea- also confused about CT and sauna and when to use which. Or should i use it and then go outside with less clothes? Or then do cold shower?

4\. Vitamin D lamp or tanning bed, or reptile lamp? Can you explain when to use red light therapy? I have stopped supplementing w Vit D but not sure how to optimize my D levels in the winter. Thank you so much for making it till the end

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-04T04:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-366340>

1- How to ground in the cold/snow (with clothes/shoes on) . . . And/or when walking and you do not have grounding shoes. (Crystal not required)
2- Not sure Jack’s take on this but I have heard many people have great success with hot/cold therapy. Think Scandinavia, Sauna or Steam followed by cold plunge or a roll in the snow. Hot stimulates blood flow then a brief cold “shock” actually does the same without the heat causing inflammation.
3- Almost every plugged in device (red light whatever) I have tested had HIGH EMF electric field.
4- ALL artificial light is a poor substitute for the big ball in the sky . . . TINA (there is no alternative).
That said remember sun light is 42% (+/-) infra red AND AT THE SAME TIME 5% (+/-) UV.
I live at 42º on the west coast and am looking into moving SOUTH . . . El Salvador, at least for the winter months, I hope to visit back there soon.

VIP - Reduce your nnEMF . . . Buy meters, (if you don’t have) and take control of your space, yes your work space but especially your sleep area at night. . . [Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://Www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

I persisted.

His farm visit was legendary.

Now he lives the Rx.

In my "QUILT" framework, the **RPE-SCN-Leptin axis** functions as a light-driven "clock-speed" regulator for the human metabolic engine, using **melanopsin (OPN4)** as the primary sensor to time-stamp energy production and storage. **_This makes all mammalian cells costly in time over energy._** Because human cells are "costly in time," any disruption to the photonic timestamp in the SCN (like chronic artificial blue light) creates a **timing error** that the body interprets as a metabolic failure. When the SCN fails your POMC mechanisms on chr 2 fail. YOU GET PALE

When the SCN fails your body fails to properly regenerate the **POMC/melanocortin** systems on Chromosome 2. As your skin becomes less atrophic you dump mass in sweat and your exocrine gut and your brain becomes human again from its atavistic chimp like state tied to shitty thinking.

DLO loves seafood and porkbelly. All things I encouraged for him even though his plates of food scared people at my events. Why did I let him run wild?

**The DHA is the Evolutionary Bottleneck that blocks broken brains from becoming human again.**
My assertion that DHA is the "single rate-limiting step" to a big brain is supported by the unique biochemistry of neural membranes. DHA is found more commonly on the RPE-SCN-leptin pathways than any other = makes the SCN more accurate and faster. This makes you an expert in entropy management. DLO would argue with me about seafood and I just told him EAT IT.

I performed brain surgery without a scalpel on DLO for a decade before he caught on. What neurosurgical procedure did I do on DLO?

**The RPE-SCN and "4D" Action:** The interaction between the Retinal Pigment Epithelium (RPE) and the **SCN** creates a feedback loop that governs time-based biology:

**Melanopsin** detects light intensity and transmits that "time" data to the SCN (the body's master clock).
This creates a "4D" temporal framework where the spatial layout (built by HOX genes) is synced to environmental light cycles (via OPN4) to regulate metabolism, sleep, and hormonal output. While HOXD genes provide the structural "hardware," OPN4 acts as the "software" that uses environmental light to calibrate that hardware for survival in space and time.

**Comparison of Roles**

| Feature | HOXD Cluster (2q31.1) | Melanopsin (OPN4) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Primary Role** | Spatial positioning (Where things go) | Irradiance detection (What time it is) |
| **CNS Focus** | Hindbrain, spinal cord segmentation | ipRGCs, SCN, and hypothalamic regulation |
| **Development** | Sets rhombomere boundaries & neural tracts | Influences axon guidance & chiasm migration |
| **Target Pathway** | Antero-posterior axis signaling | Retinohypothalamic tract (RHT) |

^^^^^THAT WAS THE Rx I did.

What happened?

If you been reading what I am putting out you should begin to get some intuition about why atrophic skin and the mitochondrial matrix LINK is deeply supported by the "Skin-Brain-Endocrine" axis: AS DLO GOT BROWN AND STAYED THIS WAY, HIS BRAIN CAME BACK ONLINE. HE BECAME HIS OWN ASIAN TIGER.

Skin as a Solar Panel: The skin is a major site of POMC expression = TAN. Reduced POMC leads to thinning (atrophy = no sweat or tan) and reduced melanin. Melanin is not just a pigment; it is an energy transducer that protects the mitochondrial matrix from oxidative "overheating." Why? it controls the exhaust mass that needs to be ejected.
How did the surgery work? UV light from the sun transcribes melanin from POMC while also making gamma MSH to help hydrate melanin in the BRAIN.

Melanin then absorbs tons of light for you.

What else does UV light do?
UV light also makes Nitric oxide and Vitamin D.

What do both of them do?
They inhibit energy production in the matrix. NO inhibits CCO. What does Vitamin D do to the VDR on the IMM? slows ETC. Nature built you to never overheat in the sun, yet you didn't both to look carefully enough at the wiring diagram. NIR light make CCO DDW. eccrine and exocrine glands begin to eject massive amounts of atoms you do not need and make you a primate brain thinker.

Why?

This science explains to you there is NO SET POINT for solar exposure except of you have ATROPHIC skin lacking melanin, which most of you do. What else does POMC also do? It ALSO releases beta-endorphin when you are in UV light which makes us addicted to the sun exposure so these question never come up. If you do not seek the sun it means by definition you
have not gotten enough sun to cleave POMC to make beta endorphin.

The real reason you overheat in the sun is likely a defect in your exhaust pipe = eccrine or exocrine system.

### stuart31 — 2026-02-23T05:25:53+0200

> [LHorn25 said:](/goto/post?id=366326)

In Chinese Medicine we tell patients to avoid cold bc it stagnates the blood... so CT is not something CM would promote and i am trying to wrap my head around it. Since I had such a negative experience, now how to tell my patients to start doing this?! I believe it works but I just need to reconcile the how as it goes against such ancient medicine where nature was the basis.

Hi, Leanne! I’m also a TCM Acupuncturist and pretty new to Dr Kruse’s work.
That is definitely an issue at the heart of TCM as how in order to be healthy, avoiding cold esp in the winter is important. I am also very curious about that! I believe a lot of what we do works for different reasons than what we might think it does.

From the cold thermogenesis series, cold adaptation opens metabolic pathways that eutherian mammals (human ancestors) evolved to thrive in their changing environment. This directly contradicts TCM principles! However, the ideas of cold being GOOD for us go back to how life evolved in the oceans at near freezing temperatures so it is hard-wired into how our biology works and central to most forms of life. The written stories from Chinese Medicine go back ~2000 years so nowhere near the scale of millions of years ago where complex life came from.

Early health decline has been an issue in Chinese Medicine from the very start. From the Nei Jing Su Wen (oldest written recordings on Chinese Medicine) the text opens with the emperor asking his chief physician how come people in the “old days” used to live to 100 years with little to no decline but people nowadays live to 50 and already show signs of aging? At the time of writing during the Warring States period it was an agricultural society = were able to eat high carbohydrates out of season = circadian mismatch. So by definition were not following nature (or in other words their own rules).

As far as recommending cold to clients. That is something I have not done in practice yet. I mostly focus on telling them to get more AM sun, grounding, eliminating artificial light at night and mitigating nnEMF particularly A/C fields in the bedroom as most people I treat are not sleeping. I would be careful recommending CT to patients as people can really hurt themselves. Even Jack says to get cleared by your doctor for things like heart conditions before doing CT. Maybe best to start with recommending face dunks or walking outside in the cold?

I have heard other female mitos having issues with CT as well like what you describe. Before starting it’s important to test for maternal haplotype so people know they are able to uncouple mitochondria in the first place, increase DHA intake beforehand to improve omega 3-6 ratios in tissues and should start **slowly**. CT should truthfully be started after the Leptin Rx. It’s all in the blogs. Beyond that I honestly do not know why females would have complications with doing CT from Jack's perspective.

### [The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol](https://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

What is the next step in the evolution of the Leptin Rx? The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol should be added gradually to the Leptin Rx rest protocol. This blog post is additive to the Leptin Rx, and is an evolution extension of it for those who need it. I hope you all realize that not everyone will

 jackkruse.com

### LHorn25 — 2026-02-23T16:16:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367455>

> [stuart31 said:](/goto/post?id=367433)
>
> Hi, Leanne! I’m also a TCM Acupuncturist and pretty new to Dr Kruse’s work.
> That is definitely an issue at the heart of TCM as how in order to be healthy, avoiding cold esp in the winter is important. I am also very curious about that! I believe a lot of what we do works for different reasons than what we might think it does.
>
> From the cold thermogenesis series, cold adaptation opens metabolic pathways that eutherian mammals (human ancestors) evolved to thrive in their changing environment. This directly contradicts TCM principles! However, the ideas of cold being GOOD for us go back to how life evolved in the oceans at near freezing temperatures so it is hard-wired into how our biology works and central to most forms of life. The written stories from Chinese Medicine go back ~2000 years so nowhere near the scale of millions of years ago where complex life came from.
>
> Early health decline has been an issue in Chinese Medicine from the very start. From the Nei Jing Su Wen (oldest written recordings on Chinese Medicine) the text opens with the emperor asking his chief physician how come people in the “old days” used to live to 100 years with little to no decline but people nowadays live to 50 and already show signs of aging? At the time of writing during the Warring States period it was an agricultural society = were able to eat high carbohydrates out of season = circadian mismatch. So by definition were not following nature (or in other words their own rules).
>
> As far as recommending cold to clients. That is something I have not done in practice yet. I mostly focus on telling them to get more AM sun, grounding, eliminating artificial light at night and mitigating nnEMF particularly A/C fields in the bedroom as most people I treat are not sleeping. I would be careful recommending CT to patients as people can really hurt themselves. Even Jack says to get cleared by your doctor for things like heart conditions before doing CT. Maybe best to start with recommending face dunks or walking outside in the cold?
>
> I have heard other female mitos having issues with CT as well like what you describe. Before starting it’s important to test for maternal haplotype so people know they are able to uncouple mitochondria in the first place, increase DHA intake beforehand to improve omega 3-6 ratios in tissues and should start **slowly**. CT should truthfully be started after the Leptin Rx. It’s all in the blogs. Beyond that I honestly do not know why females would have complications with doing CT from Jack's perspective.
>
>
> ### [The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol](https://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)
>
> What is the next step in the evolution of the Leptin Rx? The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol should be added gradually to the Leptin Rx rest protocol. This blog post is additive to the Leptin Rx, and is an evolution extension of it for those who need it. I hope you all realize that not everyone will
>
>  jackkruse.com

Stuart, thanks so much for your thoughtful reply. I have been recommending AM light, seafood, blue blockers, Iris software and grounding (cautiously because many wires in the area are buried under ground) and so far have just 1-2 patients taking my suggestions seriously. This helps me understand why Jack gets so frustrated with even his members, haha, because people seem to want the most convenient fix. This is how we have been conditioned and it is a hard program to breeak. I find the few who go all in do reap the biggest rewards.

Where are you located? Are you finding it is becoming more and more difficult to help patients fully recover from issues? I have been in practice for 14 years and I can't be getting "worse" as a practitioner as i am always trying to learn more and more, but in the last few years I am seeing that patients' results from tx do not seem to hold as long. Could also be that as I gain more knowledge, I have been taking on more difficult cases and things like Long Covid did not exist before 2020. Wonder your thoughts on this?
As for the Nei Jing... I did have the thought after I posted, that it seemed they were already living out of harmony with nature a few thousand years ago. It got me thinking that perhaps the "cold" that infiltrates the body that TCM describes is because we are not adapting to our environment the way we are designed to. That living in warm shelters even back then-plus agriculture, etc has been our demise. But it certainly will be one I keep pondering. i keep saying I am going to read the Nei jing cover to cover to gain more clarity- i am staring at the book right now... BUT between catching up on all Jack's blogs, the forum and all the webinars as I just became a gold member... seems there's not enough time in the day

### stuart31 — 2026-02-24T17:09:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367574>

I’m in Saskatoon, Canada aka way too far north. I’m in my second year of practice so don’t have the experience you do. I’m still figuring things out so am finding my results are getting more consistent just with finding my bearings..Jack’s work, Mae Wan Ho and Becker’s books have been helpful in understanding what it is I’m actually doing.

For me, patients are generally in 3 categories. Those that don’t get better, those that get better right away and those that see results temporarily..That being said, here in Canada during winter people are mostly grounded 0% of the time, pale as can be, constantly exposed to nnEMF. It seems in clinical practice there’s so many factors that play into people’s improvement that it’s difficult to say what is truly helping. The most problematic variable is people’s environment so for me that variable has to change to know what treatments actually help people heal/reach optimal best.

Ideally we need to treat people who are:
\- cold adapted
\- well melanated
\- not living in a blue lit, microwaved environment
\- grounded

In my limited experience on more complex cases, mitigating the harm from centralized medicine is a huge factor. People are often way overmedicated. I just saw a patient who recovered from severe urticaria who has diabetes, heart problems, hypothyroid. Guess what? The hives were a side effect from her heart meds. She had also previously had an anaphylactic reaction to a CCB she was on. Often SSRIs, statins and hypertension meds are actually causing the complaints people come to see me for!

As for Chinese history, have you heard of the tomb of Xin Zhui from the Han dynasty? "The best preserved mummy in history", was a 2000 year old body of a noblewoman. It was so well preserved she had intact skin and internal organs. They did an autopsy and found blood clots that caused her to have a heart attack that killed her. She had liver disease, gallstones, high blood pressure, arteriosclersis and a degenerated lumbar disc that likely caused her severe pain.

### [Meet The Most Well-Preserved Mummy In Human History](https://allthatsinteresting.com/xin-zhui-lady-dai)

How the Lady of Dai remained so shockingly intact for 2,000 years.

 allthatsinteresting.com

Being disconnected from nature goes back a long time. Just look at how much clothing they wore

I have not heard about the tomb of Xin Zhui but this is really eye opening. Makes so much sense. I read Becker's book a couple years ago while studying under Dr. Jeremy Steiner (he is big in the acu world for effects of electro-acupuncture, not sure if you've heard of him?) some how that led me down the rabbit hole and i found Dr. Jack. When he mentioned The Body Electric book a light bulb went off for me! I think we have a lot to offer patients and I think EA is really beneficial. My issue with Jeremy is he is always trying to sell something and it got to be too much "markety" for me so I'm no longer part of his private group- but have learned a lot. Some of his teachings conflict with Jack (like PEMF devices, for example) but other views are exactly in line and it is wild!

### stuart31 — 2026-02-27T17:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member.32224/post-367739>

Wow I love that you brought Steiner up! I heard about him just recently from [@amocchino1](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/41548/) , another Acupuncturist on here. Jeremy's work does seem to line up with Jack's a lot. I did a bit more digging and it seems he learned a lot of what he's teaching from Jerry Tennant who was big into PEMF biomodulator devices. You can find a lot of Jerry's talks on Youtube and he's written a few books as well. Their business models seems very similar. Get you into the funnel and sell you more and more stuff.

They offer a lot of big promises as well but do seem to get into the realm of pseudoscience so left me skeptical.

What Jerry and subsequently Jeremy say about filling up the acu channels with electrons does seem to make sense but feels too much like a one stop solution. If we truly could use EA to exogenously restore "voltage" in channels akin to the photoelectric effect from sun exposure, eating abundant seafood and grounding, there's a few problems with that.

\- we would need to have hydrated collagen networks and water proton cables in the first place to not overpower the existing "wiring"
or
\- if we have high heteroplasmy

in both cases the treatment would cause massive amounts of inflammation rather than having the desired effects.

I did make a post in the healthcare practitioners forum asking about these concerns over electroacupuncture vs standard. In the last Q&A Jack referred to TENS devices being good for short term use but long term can be problematic. I'd assume the same would hold true for EA. The power even from microcurrent devices is just so much greater than endogenous currents. Becker also talks a bit about concerns with EA in his book Cross Currents. What acu can do though is stimulate UPE's and photorepair as mentioned here:

### [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1AuQyQJwHo/)

 www.facebook.com

Maybe Jack can jump in here if I'm talking out of my ass.

Interestingly a lot of research on acupuncture does use electric stimulation so for sure it has benefits. Jack's recent FB post above references acu studes using EA to increase brown adipose tissue (increasing melanin in fat) and stem cells in blood circulation which blew me away.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-27T19:01:56+0200

The claims of adding electrons to a acu circuit is pure nonsense.

# Thread: Decentralized perspective on wisdom teeth?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fouadfo1 — 2026-02-04T04:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366338>

Hello everyone, so I have what my dentist says are impacted wisdom teeth on my lower jaw. I have been in off and on pain for the last 2 years trying to remain patient because I do not want to remove them with the hopes that with time they will perhaps ‘settle in’.

However, during this time frame, I noticed that my tinnitus actually coincided with the jaw pain I started to experience 2 years ago. The tinnitus has only increased in intensity, and on my ‘bad days’ when my jaw is tense and painful, the tinnitus seems to be particularly intense.

I’m wondering what the best solution here would be, I’m concerned that removing my wisdom teeth would result in further complications.

Any ideas? Thanks

### Shannon Eavenson — 2026-02-04T05:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366344>

Is it better to try to expand your jaw with special devices or exercises? I have held back on getting my daughters pulled. My son took out only one side because one tooth was growing sideways but he kept the other side top and bottom two years ago age 23. I don't know I just know DR> Jack was talking about how it would be better to keep the wisdom teeth.

### S Hudson — 2026-02-04T20:57:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-366368>

Not enough information to be of assistance I'm afraid. I'd need radiographs, intra-oral photos and a full clinical history of the symptoms as and when they presented.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-06T17:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralized-perspective-on-wisdom-teeth.32225/post-373221>

How is your Wednesday going? Just came off the beach for AM sunrise and spent 3 hours there. Now I will begin resisting my guild. Decentralized reistance is worth doing in a dystopian fiat world like centralized medicine.

New blog out this AM will blow your mind. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-87-mayan-155959027](https://www.patreon.com/posts/cpc-87-mayan-155959027)

What was the original "Neuralink" device the Mayans used to fight the last magnetic excursion?

With politicians and other non-medical individuals dictating to the wise in decentralized medicine and dentistry what we are allowed to prescribe to the ill, we clinicians, have been put in a position such that our ability to uphold the Hippocratic oath is under attack. Never before has our role as decentrlaized thinkers and MDs been so important because never before have we become complicit in causing so much harm in this Rockefeller dynasty of corruption.

# Thread: No more Blue Blockers in my house!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-blue-blockers-in-my-house.32234/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TommyM — 2026-02-06T19:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-more-blue-blockers-in-my-house.32234/post-366445>

A few months ago I converted all of my lights, ceiling lights, bulbs, etc., to Incandescent bulbs from Healthlighting.com. Everyone loves them. I wore blue blockers in the house while using my laptop, phone, or when the TV was on, but I hate wearing blue blockers in my house, and they leak from the sides so I did a little research. It turns out that the theatrical/photography industry makes colored gel films for spotlights and lighting effects. I found a company called ROSCO and they publish the light filtering/blocking specs of their various gel films. After some experimentation I narrowed my selection to the two films below.

I use the Roscolux Medium Amber #20 on my two 65" TV's. I trimmed the film and it clings to the screen naturally through static electric bond. I use tiny amounts of scotch tape at the top. Ideally my goal is to eliminate TV altogether, but this works great and it's easy on the eyes, skin, and brain.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

For my laptop and desk monitor I use Roscolux #21 Golden Amber Gel Film. For the laptop, I cut the film to size and had it laminated at the local UPS store, and use sticky back magnetic tape for attachment. For my large monitor, the film sticks naturally. The real life image below, appears darker than it actually is when backlit by my screens.

For my iPhones I use Roscolux Medium Amber #20. I cut the film and place it over my glass screen and use a two part phone case with a built in screen protector.

The Rosco Roscolux Gel Films are available in various sizes:
20" X 24" - $11.00 -$15.00
48" X 25' Roll - $170

Available at
[Stagelightingstore.com](https://www.stagelightingstore.com/Rosco-E-Colour-020-Medium-Amber-48-x-5-Roll?srsltid=AfmBOoqG8TKJGfWgX66-OYNOZwMuUmoHXqNyhbO2vZvZ6MXTWUFH7Q5L3uo)
[BHPhotovideo.com](https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/163249-REG/Rosco_102300204825_E_Colour_020_Medium_Amber.html)
Fullcompass.com

I suggest you purchase two 20"X 24" rolls of each color and see what works for you.

No more Blue Blockers in the house anymore. Hope this help on your journey to becoming a Mitochondriac

Thanks Dr. Jack for pointing the way!

> [TommyM said:](/goto/post?id=366445)
>
> A few months ago I converted all of my lights, ceiling lights, bulbs, etc., to Incandescent bulbs from Healthlighting.com. Everyone loves them. I wore blue blockers in the house while using my laptop, phone, or when the TV was on, but I hate wearing blue blockers in my house, and they leak from the sides so I did a little research. It turns out that the theatrical/photography industry makes colored gel films for spotlights and lighting effects. I found a company called ROSCO and they publish the light filtering/blocking specs of their various gel films. After some experimentation I narrowed my selection to the two films below.
>
> I use the Roscolux Medium Amber #20 on my two 65" TV's. I trimmed the film and it clings to the screen naturally through static electric bond. I use tiny amounts of scotch tape at the top. Ideally my goal is to eliminate TV altogether, but this works great and it's easy on the eyes, skin, and brain.
> [View attachment 33524](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/33524/) [View attachment 33534](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/33534/)
>
>
> For my laptop and desk monitor I use Roscolux #21 Golden Amber Gel Film. For the laptop, I cut the film to size and had it laminated at the local UPS store, and use sticky back magnetic tape for attachment. For my large monitor, the film sticks naturally. The real life image below, appears darker than it actually is when backlit by my screens.
>
> [View attachment 33528](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/33528/) [View attachment 33532](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/33532/)
>
>
> For my iPhones I use Roscolux Medium Amber #20. I cut the film and place it over my glass screen and use a two part phone case with a built in screen protector.
>
> [View attachment 33535](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/33535/)
>
> The Rosco Roscolux Gel Films are available in various sizes:
> 20" X 24" - $11.00 -$15.00
> 48" X 25' Roll - $170
>
> Available at

> [BHPhotovideo.com](https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/163249-REG/Rosco_102300204825_E_Colour_020_Medium_Amber.html)
> Fullcompass.com
>
> I suggest you purchase two 20"X 24" rolls of each color and see what works for you.
>
> No more Blue Blockers in the house anymore. Hope this help on your journey to becoming a Mitochondriac
>

# Thread: Starlink Solution from China
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starlink-solution-from-china.32238/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2026-02-08T20:35:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starlink-solution-from-china.32238/post-366543>

**A new, compact, high-power microwave weapon, the TPG1000Cs, has been developed at a Shanghai Nuclear Technology Institute, which could become one of the most serious threats to the Starlink satellite network.**

The device can deliver 20 gigawatts of energy for up to a full minute, the South China Morning Post reported, cited by [Portfolio](https://www.portfolio.hu/global/20260205/bekovetkezett-az-amitol-rettegtek-az-egyesult-allamokban-pillanatok-alatt-semlegesithetik-amerika-legnagyobb-erosseget-816196).

The TPG1000Cs, the world’s first compact driver for high-power microwave weapons, has been created at the Northwest Institute of Nuclear Technology in Shanghai. The device can deliver 20 gigawatts of power for up to one minute.

At just four meters long and weighing just five tons, the device is small enough to be mounted on trucks, warships, airplanes, or even satellites. Some Chinese experts estimate that a ground-based microwave weapon with a power of over 1 gigawatt could be capable of seriously disrupting or even damaging satellites in low Earth orbit, such as Starlink, being used in the Russian-Ukrainian war.

Previously known similar systems could operate continuously for no more than three seconds and were much larger. The Russian Sinus-7 drive, for example, was operational for about a second, delivered about 100 pulses per shot, and weighed up to 10 tons.

China has repeatedly signaled that Starlink poses a serious threat to its national security. Chinese military researchers are currently developing new “Starlink killer” weapons, including high-powered microwave systems and lasers, that could be used to relatively cheaply combat large constellations of low-orbit satellites if necessary.

SpaceX has lowered the orbital altitude of its Starlink satellites to reduce the risk of collisions. But that makes them much more vulnerable to attacks from ground-based directed energy weapons. If China eventually deploys the TPG1000Cs in space, the invisible strikes could be even more devastating.

### Paul Fieber — 2026-02-11T00:37:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise.32243/post-366663>

Hi

I live in western Oregon at the 43rd latitude in a valley surrounded by mountains. Right now our sunrise is at 7:30AM, because of our location we do not see the actual sun for about 20-30 minutes, before it crests the mountain. Is this to late to get all the benefits of the first sunrise each morning? We also have a very wet winter ( Dec.- Feb) with little sun in the AM. I would appreciate if someone could suggest a light that would mimic the sunrise to cover UVA and red light. Is it OK to use a red light indoors after sunset?

It is not a "fat" problem; it is a **localized dielectric failure** of the periorbital neuroectodermal system. It represents the body’s desperate attempt to build a **"Lead Shield"** around the eye because the melanin-water-light stoichiometry in that zip code has been obliterated by blue light stress and nnEMF.

# Thread: Trees and leafy friends blocking  5g
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul T008 — 2026-02-11T16:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366697>

Does any one know why the trees and hedges block 5g signals ,
I know trees reflect Infar red light, from the the leaf itself in scatter pattern from the sun
but also trees are grounded from the root system guessing picking up electrons from the earth.
but then there is it the Chlorophyll within the leaf itself i thinking this may block the signal of 5g
is 5g a blue wavelength maybe that the chlorophyll absorbs this.
has any one any ideas which of any of the above or other thoughts to why trees and hedges block 5g nature
the council in the UK have a habit of felling the trees to improve the 5 g signal

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-12T01:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366729>

Trees, hedges, and foliage contain a high amount of water in their leaves, branches, and trunks. Water molecules absorb radio waves, especially at higher frequencies, converting the signal energy into heat and reducing its strength. This is why wet foliage (e.g., after rain) exacerbates the issue.

The dense structure of leaves, needles (in evergreens), and branches scatters the waves in multiple directions, diffusing the signal and preventing it from maintaining a direct path to the receiver. This is more pronounced in leafy seasons; for example, studies show signal loss can drop by several decibels in winter when deciduous trees lose their leaves.

Research from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) measured average signal losses of around 35.3 dB through evergreens and 22-27 dB through leafy deciduous trees, which can reduce signal power by factors of 1,000 or more in dense areas like forests.

This makes mmWave 5G less reliable in wooded or suburban areas with heavy vegetation, often requiring more base stations or fallback to lower-frequency bands for coverage.

Lower-frequency 5G bands (sub-6 GHz) are less affected by trees and can penetrate better, similar to 4G, but they don't offer the same ultra-high speeds.

### Paul T008 — 2026-02-12T10:56:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366734>

As a Gardner will have to encourage the tribe/customers to plant hedges and trees around there font garden to mess 5g electric and gas smart meter signals will be reduced . many thanks for the information

### Anthony Smith — 2026-02-12T18:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trees-and-leafy-friends-blocking-5g.32246/post-366750>

Not sure but I believe electric and gas “smart” meters are sub 1Ghz so have no problem penetrating foliage or walls.

5G is a technology not a set frequency so lower frequency 5G as Jack said, can penetrate (AT&T uses 3-4GHz) where higher frequencies cannot. 20 GHz+ 5G can also be blocked by glass. Check the frequencies being used in your area.

# Thread: Saturn's Return - Mellica Schmidt - Canada
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MellicaSchmidt — 2026-02-14T22:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-366895>

My name is Mellica Schmidt. I'm 28, from India originally but I've been living in Vancouver, BC Canada for almost 20 years now. **I've put in bold what's most medically relevant for short reading.** In future posts I might cut to the chase with medical info but I'm finding it really helpful to treat this as a proper journal.

I always like bad news first so here goes:

My two biggest mistakes in life are:
**1\. Took birth control for about nine years (pills for most of it, 2-3 years of the Mirena IUD)
2\. Agreed to 2x Moderna jabs**

I've never been one to shy away from consequences and I know what I get will be no less than I deserve, so I try and take these on the chin best I can. When I was introduced to Jack's work eight months ago (I had to do the math because I thought it had been a couple years) the only thing I had to think about was trying to learn what "cytochrome c oxidase" meant (still working on its intricacies), and now it's, "how do we leave Canada, where do want to go, is it too late, where can we afford to go..."etc etc etc.

Medically, I'm facing potential **infertility**.
**AMH levels came back low (9.7)
AFC showed my right ovary's count was 9** (low-average I was told). My **left ovary's count was 4 (low)** and I was floored with the discovery of a **7cm cyst** in there. All I was told was not to get it removed because I'd likely lose eggs during the surgery. **Going for a blood test to help diagnose what kind of cyst it is**. They also want a sonohysterogram to check if my tubes are blocked etc. I know the ultrasound/SIS are radiating my body but I decided it was worth the risk to find out what's going on. I think after I get answers I'll abandon the clinic, since they will likely push IUI/IVF which I don't consider an option. **Birth control ruined my cycle but over the past three years I've been slowly recovering** through the only supplement I still reliably take from my naturopath. It's a mix of chasteberry, ginkgo berry leaf, and vit B6 to help my body make its own progesterone again.

I think **nnEMFS over the years are probably one of the biggest contributers to lack of fecundity**. I've been working as a camera technician for 7-8 years, being bombarded with radiowaves, and subject to really poor light and circadian rhythm (often in dark studios and shooting overnight) for 10-14 hours a day.
I told my boss today that after this next gig, which ends next week, **I'm leaving the industry.** I have no idea what I'll do for income after that. I fantasize (and I know I'm not the first) about opening a retreat focussed on all the things light, water, and magnetism, even though I have neither the capital nor the expertise...yet.

**We also live two blocks from a substation.

Living at the 54th latitude when I was born at the 33rd** is also a big issue. My Vit D was 142nmol/L end of 2024 but I think back then I was taking Vit D oil, I'm getting it tested again soon. **I do own a Sperti D and use a hybrid tanning bed.** The increase in my mood after tanning is nothing short of miraculous, but I can't afford to go as frequently as I'd like. One of the saddest days of the year for me is the summer solstice because the days afterwards get short so rapidly despite the warm temperatures that linger till September.

Other things of note-
\-I might have **tinnitus,** my ears ring when I go to bed and when I wake up in the morning. During the daytime it's not really noticeable. I know there was a recent blog about this that I'm hoping I can get to soon.
\-Food is fine in general but I have **very strong cravings for sugar**, certain processed foods and sometimes carbs.
\-If it isn't obvious by now I'm a highly stressed individual, by nature, lack of enough UV all my life, and by current circumstances. **My cortisol in a DUTCH test last year showed my waking level at 87.7ng/mg**, a whole 37 points higher than the high range, and it **crashes in the early afternoon**. I try meditating and I use an acupressure mat everyday which helps, but I'm pretty sure I was probably constantly in a sympathetic state from around age 12 to a few years ago, for multiple reasons which I'm sure hasn't helped anything.

\-Other potentially relevant facts from my DUTCH test:
**Testosterone 2.33ng/mg - low
Dopamine markers low- HVA 3.2 ug/mg
Norepinephrine/Epinephrine marker low- VMA 2.0 ug/mg**
Aside from these and the cortisol issue everything else was in range.

That's probably enough of the bad. Now the good.

My husband is the best thing in my life. He's 36, a blond-haired blue-eyed Canadian with German/British ancestry, and the only rock in my life. He's my circle of six. He is my good news. Even just writing this much about him makes me more optimistic.

**I do get out at sunrise everyday** (sometimes my work interferes but even then I at least try to ge 2-5min). At home, even though we're near a substation and in a densely populated city, in lieu of a backyard we have a south-facing rooftop patio which has been a lifesaver for sun exposure.

**I sleep really well**, I rarely have trouble falling asleep and sleep through the night (usually get up to pee like two hours before sunrise but that's it). The only time this is disrupted is if I'm not careful about controlling my blue light exposure then sometimes I get up in the middle of the night for a while. Stress will do the same too.

I started painting with watercolors. My hobbies are generally just reading and learning about quantum biology these days so I needed something different.

I'm taking Alexis Cowan's QuantumU course which has been really fun and challenging.

I have taken steps to change our house to be more circadian friendly, with adding some red bulbs, swapping LEDs etc. Thankfully sunrise has come back to right before 7am so we're entering the sweet spot before it gets bright at 4:30. Next will be hardwiring wifi.

We generally eat healthy, I found a new fish market yesterday to up our DHA intake. I eat caviar like a supplement. I've been forcing breakfast first thing when I get up in the morning. I'm a really good cook- I make our bone broth from scratch and because I refuse to completely cut out bread from my life I bought a mill to make my own flour from local glyphosate-free grains. I love making sourdough.

Despite all the bad, I have a lot to be grateful for- something I forget often and easily. My body is at least intact (I know somebody who recently lost a leg in an accident). My brain functions. I'm not on the Epstein list. It'll be spring soon. My family is relatively okay. I can afford to spend money on a case of DDW.

**One question I haven't sorted yet is I'm not sure if I'm leptin resistant.** I'm around 120lbs (5'4), I've been an early riser since childhood but as I mentioned I do have cravings. My instinct tells me the cravings have to do with dopamine issues and aforementioned stress. When I get in with my naturopath next I might just get it tested. Aside from that, if I were to diagnose myself I'd say **I think I just desperately need more UV light.

THE PLAN GOING FORWARD, AS IT STANDS
\-**After this job ends, go full steam with all the relevant protocols (lacking in cold plunging right now, need more DHA in diet, maybe the leptin rx but not sure)
\-**Sort out bitcoin.** I only own xxxxx bitcoin right now- and not even own because I don't have it in a wallet yet (hoping to sort that ASAP but I really don't understand it as well as I should so currently it's just another thing to be overwhelmed by).
\-Find a new job, preferably remote so I can control my environment.
**\-Go south, preferably El Salvador.** Trying to sort out the best way to do this financially but I'm hoping at least a couple months ASAP (likely April-June), then maybe a longer stay over the winter. I'm nervous about it though because I'm not a traveller and this will be my first solo trip and even though I've heard El Salvador's safe, being a woman alone in a country I don't know is going to be scary especially since I'll be looking at lower-cost housing. I've seen Jeremy Franco mentioned here so maybe I will get a silver membership to chat with him (I think silver is the requirement). Regardless, hoping while I'm there to scope out potential real estate to buy for our eventual exit from Canada. Maybe it's too late, maybe it's not, but we gotta try.

For those who don't know, Saturn's return refers to the phenomenon of great transition people experience when Saturn returns to the position it was in when they were born. Usually the effect can be felt for a few years, from around age 27-30, then again at around 60. I've never been much for astrology but when I learned about it recently I thought, "That's pretty fucking bang on."

I'm not sure if I've begun writing to seek advice, admit guilt, or provide myself clarity, but it's been cathartic, at least- thanks for reading. Any comments are welcome and appreciated. Especially looking to connect with other Canadians that are trying to leave too.

### LoriLee — 2026-02-15T05:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-366910>

Contact India for housing in El Salvador. +1 (813) 833-8750. Ive been 3 times without incident. Once you go, you'll never want to leave. I've never once felt in danger. Check out the Bitcoin hardware store in El zonte for Bitcoin help. They have a YouTube channel. Message them. They can help you remotely too. Ask for Ronny. I have his number if needed.

### MellicaSchmidt — 2026-05-04T18:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-373010>

Post #2

I've been in Atami for about a month now. Really enjoying the beach and the people (but not the mosquitos, I guess I'm leaking too much UV). Here's a breakdown of what's been happening:

**MELANIN:** I'm significantly darker than when I got here, and it's starting to stick. The first couple weeks I noticed if I missed a day of conscious tanning, or if it was cloudy that I lost my melanin very quickly- I must have been really needing it. I don't think I've reached my maximum yet but I should be able to before I leave. One thing of note is I wasn't as good with handling the heat as I was the UV, especially on my legs. Somebody pointed out this may have to do with my heme proteins, so that's been added on the list to research. I have noticed heat tolerance increasing, and I make a point of trying to sweat as much as I can before I hop in the plunge.

**GROUNDING:** I haven't been at the beach grounding as long as I'd like to over the last week or so, so I'm going to be more conscious about it. I currently average at 2-3 hours a day overall. I want it to be at least twice that. I'm not exercising right now but I'm sore after surf lessons which I believe indicates I need more grounding too.

**DRINKING WATER:** Not satiated easily from drinking water (this issue has existed for a while), I think just an indication that my mitochondria isn't making enough and I'm dehydrated from the inside.

**CYST DE-FRAG** (maybe): I notice that when I'm in the sun (grounding or not) I feel a "heartbeat" in my left abdominal area. I think it's trying to shake deuterium loose. Sometimes it's alarmingly strong. I take it as a good sign, and after a good sun day it stays after I move to shade.

**HEAVY BLOOD**: A TCM I saw here said my blood is heavily in stasis, which I realized a couple days later probably meant it's "maple syrup". He also said my kidneys and spleen are "low" which I took as "sagging from deuterium overload". So I'm reading up on the de-frag protocols and trying to apply what I can. Definitely doing a lot of humming while I'm lying on the beach in the tide.

Here's the defrag/deuterium dump list I've come up with so far (edited as I learn):

\-jumping jacks grounded
\-walking
\-chest/body pounding
\-sweating
\-tens machine (maybe a massage gun as well, since de-frag seems to supercede nnEMF exposure I think, so long as grounding is available)
\-humming
\-tuning forks
\-40hz music/sound
\-saline/DDW IV (maybe I can find somebody to do this for me at home) with ingested DDW afterwards
\-DDW ingestion in general
\-mastic gum (I sometimes grind my teeth at night, is that an attempt at defragging?)
\-wearing jade, specifically letting it charge in the sun and touching to various acupuncture points in body, specifically fingertips
\-changing my fillings with something more piezoelectric (modern version of jade inlays) this is more long term, need to read about it more
\-in relation to jade, green + red light, i.e sitting under trees outside should help
\-psilocybin mushrooms
\-MCT rich foods/ MCT oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides)
\-max UV and grounding possible
\-deuterium depleted/high DHA diet etc.
\-TMS (transcranial magnetic stimulation) machine, not applicable to me unless my brain goes crazy.

Also wondering if vasodilation via jamaica tea is helpful at all, not as a de-fragging element but just to help allow better blood flow.

My homework for this is the "understanding vortices" and the vagal exhaust blog. (Probably de-frag thread again too). After understanding that women will offload their deuterium to their children, it's basically going to be my life's mission to de-frag as much as I can. Maybe I'll go as far as getting a thermogram before we decide to try for conception again.

**APPETITE:** As soon as I arrived my appetite was diminished. I dismissed it thinking it would only be for a couple days maybe because I was unused to the heat, but it's generally persisted. I end up fasting for 12-14 hours a day. (Breakfast first thing, last meal around 3 or 4). My cravings for caffeine and sugar were also extremely diminished almost immediately. This has changed significantly over the past week, and I've been waking up hungry in the middle of the night even though I feel satiated during the day. Cravings are back a bit too. (This last week might all be cycle related.)

**STRESS:** Generally low (how could it not be in the sun?) but definitely comes when I start to think about what I'm going to do for a job when I get home, and our future re: moving down here potentially.

**CT:** I've been cold plunging fairly consistently. I started at 55F and worked my way to around 20min, and would use it to cool myself so I could stay in the sun longer, so maybe a total of 40min plunging around 4 days a week, ish. Then I moved to 53F for around 10min, total 30min a day or so spread over maybe 2 hours, about a week ago. I feel great when I'm in the plunge.

**CYCLE LATE:** My cycle was due about a month into my visit, and I was 4 days late which is highly unusual. From the time it was supposed to start I had the aforementioned erratic appetite and bowel movements, basically it felt like my system went haywire. I'm unsure if I was late because of the intermittent fasting or the cold plunging, or something else entirely.

**TENDONITIS**: Generally better, noticed difficulty with my arms and hands in the cold plunge but was working towards being able to submerge my hands for longer than a minute.

**TINNITUS**: Not completely gone but a lot less noticeable than at home (In both places, I only hear it when I'm about to sleep).

It's been feeling simultaneously fast and slow paced, my time here, but it's still hard to believe I've been here a month already. I'm halfway through my trip now and am curious what this next month brings. What's surprised me most so far is how fulfilling it can be to just bake in the sun with no podcasts, no music no nothing, except maybe some humming. I'm not sure I've ever been so content with just feeling the sun on my bare skin, nor has my mind been able to stay quiet the way it does here.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-04T20:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturns-return-mellica-schmidt-canada.32259/post-373034>

> [MellicaSchmidt said:](/goto/post?id=366895)
>
> My name is Mellica Schmidt. I'm 28, from India originally but I've been living in Vancouver, BC Canada for almost 20 years now. **I've put in bold what's most medically relevant for short reading.** In future posts I might cut to the chase with medical info but I'm finding it really helpful to treat this as a proper journal.
>
> I always like bad news first so here goes:
>
> My two biggest mistakes in life are:
> **1\. Took birth control for about nine years (pills for most of it, 2-3 years of the Mirena IUD)
> 2\. Agreed to 2x Moderna jabs**
>
> I've never been one to shy away from consequences and I know what I get will be no less than I deserve, so I try and take these on the chin best I can. When I was introduced to Jack's work eight months ago (I had to do the math because I thought it had been a couple years) the only thing I had to think about was trying to learn what "cytochrome c oxidase" meant (still working on its intricacies), and now it's, "how do we leave Canada, where do want to go, is it too late, where can we afford to go..."etc etc etc.
>
> Medically, I'm facing potential **infertility**.
> **AMH levels came back low (9.7)
> AFC showed my right ovary's count was 9** (low-average I was told). My **left ovary's count was 4 (low)** and I was floored with the discovery of a **7cm cyst** in there. All I was told was not to get it removed because I'd likely lose eggs during the surgery. **Going for a blood test to help diagnose what kind of cyst it is**. They also want a sonohysterogram to check if my tubes are blocked etc. I know the ultrasound/SIS are radiating my body but I decided it was worth the risk to find out what's going on. I think after I get answers I'll abandon the clinic, since they will likely push IUI/IVF which I don't consider an option. **Birth control ruined my cycle but over the past three years I've been slowly recovering** through the only supplement I still reliably take from my naturopath. It's a mix of chasteberry, ginkgo berry leaf, and vit B6 to help my body make its own progesterone again.
>
> I think **nnEMFS over the years are probably one of the biggest contributers to lack of fecundity**. I've been working as a camera technician for 7-8 years, being bombarded with radiowaves, and subject to really poor light and circadian rhythm (often in dark studios and shooting overnight) for 10-14 hours a day.
> I told my boss today that after this next gig, which ends next week, **I'm leaving the industry.** I have no idea what I'll do for income after that. I fantasize (and I know I'm not the first) about opening a retreat focussed on all the things light, water, and magnetism, even though I have neither the capital nor the expertise...yet.
>
> **We also live two blocks from a substation.
>
> Living at the 54th latitude when I was born at the 33rd** is also a big issue. My Vit D was 142nmol/L end of 2024 but I think back then I was taking Vit D oil, I'm getting it tested again soon. **I do own a Sperti D and use a hybrid tanning bed.** The increase in my mood after tanning is nothing short of miraculous, but I can't afford to go as frequently as I'd like. One of the saddest days of the year for me is the summer solstice because the days afterwards get short so rapidly despite the warm temperatures that linger till September.
>
> Other things of note-
> \-I might have **tinnitus,** my ears ring when I go to bed and when I wake up in the morning. During the daytime it's not really noticeable. I know there was a recent blog about this that I'm hoping I can get to soon.
> \-Food is fine in general but I have **very strong cravings for sugar**, certain processed foods and sometimes carbs.
> \-If it isn't obvious by now I'm a highly stressed individual, by nature, lack of enough UV all my life, and by current circumstances. **My cortisol in a DUTCH test last year showed my waking level at 87.7ng/mg**, a whole 37 points higher than the high range, and it **crashes in the early afternoon**. I try meditating and I use an acupressure mat everyday which helps, but I'm pretty sure I was probably constantly in a sympathetic state from around age 12 to a few years ago, for multiple reasons which I'm sure hasn't helped anything.
>
> \-Other potentially relevant facts from my DUTCH test:
> **Testosterone 2.33ng/mg - low
> Dopamine markers low- HVA 3.2 ug/mg
> Norepinephrine/Epinephrine marker low- VMA 2.0 ug/mg**
> Aside from these and the cortisol issue everything else was in range.
>
> That's probably enough of the bad. Now the good.
>
> My husband is the best thing in my life. He's 36, a blond-haired blue-eyed Canadian with German/British ancestry, and the only rock in my life. He's my circle of six. He is my good news. Even just writing this much about him makes me more optimistic.
>
> **I do get out at sunrise everyday** (sometimes my work interferes but even then I at least try to ge 2-5min). At home, even though we're near a substation and in a densely populated city, in lieu of a backyard we have a south-facing rooftop patio which has been a lifesaver for sun exposure.
>
> **I sleep really well**, I rarely have trouble falling asleep and sleep through the night (usually get up to pee like two hours before sunrise but that's it). The only time this is disrupted is if I'm not careful about controlling my blue light exposure then sometimes I get up in the middle of the night for a while. Stress will do the same too.
>
> I started painting with watercolors. My hobbies are generally just reading and learning about quantum biology these days so I needed something different.
>
> I'm taking Alexis Cowan's QuantumU course which has been really fun and challenging.
>
> I have taken steps to change our house to be more circadian friendly, with adding some red bulbs, swapping LEDs etc. Thankfully sunrise has come back to right before 7am so we're entering the sweet spot before it gets bright at 4:30. Next will be hardwiring wifi.
>
> We generally eat healthy, I found a new fish market yesterday to up our DHA intake. I eat caviar like a supplement. I've been forcing breakfast first thing when I get up in the morning. I'm a really good cook- I make our bone broth from scratch and because I refuse to completely cut out bread from my life I bought a mill to make my own flour from local glyphosate-free grains. I love making sourdough.
>
> Despite all the bad, I have a lot to be grateful for- something I forget often and easily. My body is at least intact (I know somebody who recently lost a leg in an accident). My brain functions. I'm not on the Epstein list. It'll be spring soon. My family is relatively okay. I can afford to spend money on a case of DDW.
>
> **One question I haven't sorted yet is I'm not sure if I'm leptin resistant.** I'm around 120lbs (5'4), I've been an early riser since childhood but as I mentioned I do have cravings. My instinct tells me the cravings have to do with dopamine issues and aforementioned stress. When I get in with my naturopath next I might just get it tested. Aside from that, if I were to diagnose myself I'd say **I think I just desperately need more UV light.
>
> THE PLAN GOING FORWARD, AS IT STANDS
> \-**After this job ends, go full steam with all the relevant protocols (lacking in cold plunging right now, need more DHA in diet, maybe the leptin rx but not sure)
> \-**Sort out bitcoin.** I only own xxxxx bitcoin right now- and not even own because I don't have it in a wallet yet (hoping to sort that ASAP but I really don't understand it as well as I should so currently it's just another thing to be overwhelmed by).
> \-Find a new job, preferably remote so I can control my environment.
> **\-Go south, preferably El Salvador.** Trying to sort out the best way to do this financially but I'm hoping at least a couple months ASAP (likely April-June), then maybe a longer stay over the winter. I'm nervous about it though because I'm not a traveller and this will be my first solo trip and even though I've heard El Salvador's safe, being a woman alone in a country I don't know is going to be scary especially since I'll be looking at lower-cost housing. I've seen Jeremy Franco mentioned here so maybe I will get a silver membership to chat with him (I think silver is the requirement). Regardless, hoping while I'm there to scope out potential real estate to buy for our eventual exit from Canada. Maybe it's too late, maybe it's not, but we gotta try.
>
> For those who don't know, Saturn's return refers to the phenomenon of great transition people experience when Saturn returns to the position it was in when they were born. Usually the effect can be felt for a few years, from around age 27-30, then again at around 60. I've never been much for astrology but when I learned about it recently I thought, "That's pretty fucking bang on."
>
> I'm not sure if I've begun writing to seek advice, admit guilt, or provide myself clarity, but it's been cathartic, at least- thanks for reading. Any comments are welcome and appreciated. Especially looking to connect with other Canadians that are trying to leave too.

Never put your Bitcoin in a post EVER.

I took care of it.
