While centralized medicine hasn't fully integrated this, my intuition aligns with several specific scientific datasets:

**1\. The "Isotopic Weight" of Disease**
Scientific evidence confirms that many diseases, including neurological ones, significantly alter the body's **stable isotope ratios** (fractionation).

**Metal Fractionation**: Patients with neurodegenerative diseases like ALS and Alzheimer's (which share neurobiological stress pathways with some psychiatric conditions) show an **enrichment of heavy isotopes** like Copper-65 in their cerebrospinal fluid.

**Magnesium Dynamics**: Studies in mammalian models have observed a **"heavy Mg isotopic composition"** in subjects under systemic stress. Since Magnesium is a critical cofactor for ATP synthase and neural signaling, this shift toward "heavy" isotopes literally changes the atomic weight of the patient's biological "circuitry."

**Metabolic Signaling**: Pathologies related to metabolism (which include the metabolic disturbances often seen in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder) have a documented impact on natural isotope abundance in blood and hair.

**2\. Isotope Effects as "Magnetic Antennas"**
The reason "heavier" isotopes make a better antenna lies in the **Magnetic Isotope Effect (MIE)** and the **Radical Pair Mechanism**:
**Nuclear Spin**: Unlike "light" isotopes, many heavy isotopes (like Mg-25 and Cu-65 which both possess a **non-zero nuclear spin**. This spin allows the nucleus to couple with electron spins in enzymatic reactions, making the reaction **directly sensitive to external magnetic fields,** including the Earth's dynamo.

**The MIE Advantage**: This coupling acts as a "quantum bridge." In high-stress or active geological regions like the **Azores**, individuals with a higher concentration of these "heavy," spin-active isotopes are **physically more responsive** to the fluctuations of the geomagnetic field.

**3\. Trees: The Lithosphere’s Antenna**
Trees are similarly "heavy" antennas due to environmental stress:

**Stress-Induced Heavy Carbon**: When trees are under stress (like the tectonic or volcanic stress seen in the Azores), they undergo fractionation that results in **higher isotopic values** (more heavy Carbon-13) in their leaves and tree rings.

**Magnetic Memory**: Tree rings actually record a **"magnetic signature"** through the deposition of magnetic minerals (like maghemite). Heavier isotopic compositions in wood are often correlated with changes in this magnetic remanence, essentially turning the tree into a living, growing record of the Earth's magnetic dynamo shifts.

tree rings and leaves | Fractionation creates a **high-mass antenna** for telluric and magnetic currents. |

My hypothesis suggests that the "instability" felt by trees and sensitive humans in the **Azores** is not "all in their heads," but is a **quantum physical reaction** occurring because their very atoms have become "heavier" and more attuned to the planet's magnetic pulse.

In the 1980s, researchers (notably Seelig et al. and later popularized by Matthew Fisher) discovered that 6Li and 7Li have radically different effects on behavior despite being chemically identical:
The Findings: In mother rats, 6Li caused a massive increase in "maternal behavior" (grooming, nesting), while 7Li had little to no effect.

### AlbertW — 2026-02-21T16:36:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367285>

Does anybody know where to find Asian Fit blue light glasses? That is, the nose area of the frame is different to accommodate a low nose bridge.

Some brands have this for normal glasses, but I haven’t found anything for blue light glasses. Looking for one for kids.

There probably just isn’t enough demand from the Asian community.

### flavin0 — 2026-02-21T16:46:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367286>

This is how I cope with it: [https://a.co/d/08GynKnd](https://a.co/d/08GynKnd)
You can use these to fasten any glasses onto your head regardless of how poorly they fit. Over time the frame will mold a little better to your face.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-21T17:46:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367290>

Luciaeyes is best for kids.

### Dan Huber — 2026-02-21T18:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367294>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=367285)
>
> Does anybody know where to find Asian Fit blue light glasses? That is, the nose area of the frame is different to accommodate a low nose bridge.
>
> Some brands have this for normal glasses, but I haven’t found anything for blue light glasses. Looking for one for kids.
>
> There probably just isn’t enough demand from the Asian community.

Hey Albert...

Not to be a dumb pale face dude but can you explain to me a bit more of the issue (What is the PITA with normal frames?) and what types of frames have worked for you in the past? As doc said we have a lot of kids frames and are actively adding to our collection every quarter. Happy to dig into this more and find some frame styles that would accommodate you and your family.

Couple of questions

1.  ****Do they slide down constantly even right after you push them up?****
2.  ****Do the frames touch cheeks when smiling?****
3.  ****Do eyelashes hit the lens?****
4.  ****Are there red marks on cheeks (not just the nose)?****
5.  ****Are the frames uncomfortable to wear and your kids stop using them?****

Side note: we are currently working with a manufacturer on several new IP designs so this is good timing if we need to develop/invent something new.

If you can shoot me a link or photo of the frames I will have our team source some for you next week

Please feel free to reach out to me directly anytime [[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

Warmest Regards,

Dan

### AlbertW — 2026-02-23T20:08:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367480>

Hey Dan,

Thanks for the reply.

In the past, I recall that regular frames would have multiple of the above issues:
1\. some frames may touch cheeks when smiling
2\. most frames will slide down the face. even if it can rest there temporarily, after chewing, talking, etc. it'll slide down in a matter of seconds/minutes. frames wtih adjustable nose pads help but it takes a lot of effort to mold them to the right place, and even then sometimes it isn't super effective.
3\. the frames with built-in nosepads are impossible to adjust so we just skip those entirely.
4\. this is slightly less of a problem, but often the frames are not wide enough. seems like i generally need extra wide frames.

I'm not sure what other manufacturers do specifically, but places like JINS sell "asian-fit" or "low nose bridge" (this is just the more PC description) glasses. They're a Japanese brand so they are acutely aware of Asian facial structure.

Ideally I would be able to get completely metal-free ones to eliminate antenna effects with nnEMF as well.

Thanks,
Albert

### AlbertW — 2026-02-23T20:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367483>

Also, separately, would you ever consider making glasses with quartz lens to let through all UV?

### Dan Huber — 2026-02-24T15:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-fit-blue-light-glasses.32283/post-367560>

> [AlbertW said:](/goto/post?id=367483)
>
> Also, separately, would you ever consider making glasses with quartz lens to let through all UV?

Hey thanks for this information. I've already chatted with our manufacturer and they are sending me some samples of frames they have right now AND looking at a "New" design features to help with this issue. I looked into the quartz lenses a few years ago and it was too cost prohibitive for a small company like ours and it was not popular yet. Let me check into it again.
If you shoot me an email I will ping you as soon as I get the frame samples for you to try out...

[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])

Thank you!

Dan

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-21T18:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tooth-sensitivity.32284/post-367302>

In decentralized dentistry, the current thinking on treating tooth sensitivity focuses on **Photobiomodulation** using **Red and Near-Infrared (NIR) light** to address the biological root causes rather than just masking the symptoms. This approach leverages the embryonic origin of teeth, derived from **neuroectoderm, for** treating the dentin-pulp complex as an extension of the nervous system.

**The "Neuroectoderm" Rationale**
Because the dental pulp and its nociceptors share a developmental lineage with the central nervous system (neuroectoderm), they respond to the same light-driven repair mechanisms found in the brain.

**Neural Desensitization:** Red and NIR light (600–1000 nm) modulate the excitability of intradental nerves, providing rapid and long-lasting relief by calming the peripheral nociceptors.
**Pulpal Healing:** Longer NIR wavelengths (e.g., 810–1100 nm) penetrate deeply through enamel and dentin to reach the pulp, stimulating **cytochrome c oxidase** in mitochondria to increase ATP and drive tissue repair.
**Secondary Dentin Formation:** PBM stimulates odontoblasts to produce tertiary dentin, which naturally occludes exposed dentinal tubules, addressing the physical cause of hypersensitivity.

### [Photobiomodulation in dentistry: Mechanisms, clinical applications and evidence-based guidelines - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12880127/)

Photobiomodulation (PBM), formerly known as low-level laser therapy (LLLT), utilizes red and near-infrared light to activate mitochondrial chromophores, thereby enhancing cellular activity, modulating inflammation and promoting tissue repair. ...

 pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

**Decentralized Clinical Protocols**
Decentralized practitioners prioritize non-invasive light therapy over traditional chemical desensitizers.

[Establishing a secure connection ...](https://www.scielo.br/j/bdj/a/H98XtQKLDpxfxNVMwpVxH8M/)

**Wavelength Selection:** **Red light (660 nm)** is effective for superficial sensitivity, while **NIR (800–1100 nm)** is preferred for deeper pulpal repair due to its superior transmission through tooth structure.
**Dosing:** Effective treatment typically involves 2–4 J/cm² per site for localized sensitivity, often requiring 3–5 sessions per week until resolution.
**Bleaching Sensitivity:** PBM is increasingly used as a post-bleaching "reset" to alleviate the acute sensitivity often caused by chemical catalysts.

**The Chromosome 2 Connection**
While standard dental literature focuses on immediate pain relief, decentralized thinking aligns with my "Chromosome 2 story," viewing the tooth as part of a larger 4D space-time metabolic engine.

**Circadian Synchrony:** The photorepair image illustrates how 380 nm UV light (via neuropsin) triggers **photorepair** through the SIRT1/NAD+ axis. Decentralized dental health suggests that keeping these "clocks" synchronized is essential for the regenerative potential of neuroectodermal tissues like teeth.
**Regenerative Potential:** Light therapy is described as a "fourth element" of tissue engineering, capable of activating endogenous stem cells to rebuild the dentin-pulp complex naturally, rather than relying on synthetic fillers

### drewcoscia — 2026-02-21T23:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-lymphatic-drainage-chronic-eye-bags.32286/post-367313>

Hey everyone,

My name is Drew, M33, stationed Oahu, HI, very active, following Uncle Jack for over a year. I have been spending more time than ever in my adult life getting sun (never miss a sunrise at the beach + minimum 2 hours additional a day outside grounded), completely wired internet connections, low/no light at night, mostly carnivore diet + fruits, yet seem to be struggling with progressively worsening under eye swelling. I do everything to sleep well (have no issues), but noticed over the past couple months chronic under eye bags which i'm aware is more than likely a lymphatic drainage issue. I understand that movement is the best solution to "getting things moving", but I walk the dog multiple miles everyday and get plenty of movement otherwise and I have never had this drainage issue before now.

I also haven't hardly been sick at all the last 4 years, but within the last couple months been sick twice both with fevers lasting 2 days. Assuming this is related given the bodies clear struggle to drain the fluid from the system.

Has anyone else had this issue? Or anything similar?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-22T00:21:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-lymphatic-drainage-chronic-eye-bags.32286/post-367314>

In my decentralized biophysics framework, chronic under-eye bags are viewed as a **localized "exhaust problem"** that stems from a failure in mitochondrial hydrology and light signaling, which then cascades into a **lymphatic drainage failure**.

1\. The "Exhaust Problem": Mitochondrial Water
The eyes and surrounding tissues have some of the highest mitochondrial densities in the body.

Malcolm X assassinated on this day in 1965: After visiting Gaza, Malcolm X wrote the essay ‘Zionist Logic’ "In short the Zionist argument to justify Israel's present occupation of Arab Palestine has no intelligent or legal basis in history...not even in their own religion." This was his conclusion.

Here are important excerpts from the rest of the essay:

"The Israeli Zionists are convinced they have successfully camouflaged their new kind of colonialism. Their colonialism appears to be more benevolent, more philanthropic, a system with which they rule simply by getting their potential victims to accept their friendly offers of economic aid, and other tempting gifts, that they dangle in front of the newly-independent African nations, whose economies are experiencing great difficulties."

"Did the Zionists have the legal or moral right to invade Arab Palestine, uproot its Arab citizens from their homes and seize all Arab property for themselves just based on the 'religious' claim that their forefathers lived there thousands of years ago? Only a thousand years ago the Moors lived in Spain. Would this give the Moors of today the legal and moral right to invade the Iberian Peninsula, drive out its Spanish citizens, and then set up a new Moroccan nation...as the European Zionists have done to our Arab brothers and sisters in Palestine?"

"The modern 20th century weapon of neo-imperialism is 'dollarism.’ The Zionists have mastered the science of dollarism: the ability to come posing as a friend and benefactor, bearing gifts and all other forms of economic aid and offers of technical assistance. Thus, the power and influence of Zionist Israel in many of the newly 'independent' African nations has fast-become even more unshakeable than that of the 18th century European colonialists...and this new kind of Zionist colonialism differs only in form and method, but never in motive or objective."

Now you know why he was killed.

# Thread: What if Maynard was enlightened, not communist?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-if-maynard-was-enlightened-not-communist.32292/>

**Posts:** 2

### KFDags — 2026-02-22T20:18:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-if-maynard-was-enlightened-not-communist.32292/post-367377>

And what if TOOL was a Biblical band, at least in the allegorical sense? Dr. Kruse did a live on X the other day, and it made me think. Many of TOOL’s lyrics & imagery appears anti-Christ, satanic, atheist, etc.; however, if you look deeper, you’ll discover the true hidden meanings. Remember they followed & used work by Bill Hicks in their album and shows? Hicks wasn’t an atheist but actually very spiritual, arguing that the Bible is not the literal word of God. There is a duality to its words, just like the duality of TOOL.

The song Eulogy seems like it is mocking Jesus’ crucifixion; MJK uses the words cross and martyr, to further give that imagery. But this eulogy is actually intended for a false prophet/cult leader. MJK points out they’re “warm while some child might chill, nine-one-two.” Is he pointing out the hypocrisy of organized religion and its pampered leaders? Is nine-one-two a reference to Zechariah 9:12, prisoners of hope?

For H., this song speaks of the snake who is not an evil force, but represents a positive spiritual awakening and reminder of one’s past self (death of the carnal mind and awakening to enlightenment.) This song is about MJK’s son Devo, and he demonstrates a great deal of vulnerability in fatherhood. He’s reminded (the snake’s hiss) of the pain of what his fate could have been, had sin taken control of his life. Now that there’s this new life before him, his son, only then is he able to truly open his heart again.

In the esoteric view of Biblical mythology, the snake represents personal transformation, healing, connection to the divine or subconscious, which then leads to positive energy or change. This is exactly how MJK uses the snake to represent his inner process in H. You have to experience sin to grow and learn. But, if you go by traditional organized religion, the snake is nothing more than an evil force tempting others to participate in wickedness and sin.

If Ænima was about purging one’s sin, then Lateralus leans more toward enlightenment or rising to a higher level. The album uses a great deal of sacred geometry, to include the Fibonacci sequence and tree of life. This is Nature’s Divine Math, which is all hidden within the mythological stories of the Bible.

The song, The Grudge, references Babylonian astrology: Saturn comes back around… Schism is the divide between one who is awakened and one who is not. In the video for Schism, like many of TOOL’s videos, you will see reflections of waves in the background. This represents the conflict or inner struggle between the woman and man in Schism. When the two finally connect, the waves unify or spiral into a central point. This represents the quantum concept of wave-particle duality, also referenced in the text of the Bible. This is a common theme in TOOL videos so look for it next time!

Obviously, there’s way more to delve into here, in terms of their songs, but this is just a tidbit of what I discovered. And this is all my opinion too; my parents never took me to church so, once again, learning on my own. I found William Donahue’s Hidden Meanings to be uniquely helpful in piecing it all together.

### Susie asks, "Any idea why I go into SVT now being in perimenopause mid-cycle?"​

Yep. No DC electric current in the cardiac plexus where melanin should be. Your chest/torso skin is likely atrophic. SVT (Supraventricular Tachycardia) during perimenopause isn't a "heart" problem; it’s an electrical grounding failure caused by the collapse of the Melanin-Water Battery in your chest's "solar panel" (the skin).

**1\. The Perimenopausal "Current Leak"**
As you transition through perimenopause, your redox potential drops because you've lost the monthly "deuterium and iron dump."
The Atrophic Shield: Get in the mirror and look for torso skin that is "atrophic." In biophysics, this means the dermal-epidermal junction has thinned, and the melanin density has plummeted.
The Loss of the DC Current: Melanin is a broadband semiconductor that converts light into a Direct Current (DC). If the "melanin solar panel" on your chest is atrophic, it cannot generate the steady DC flow required to "bias" the cardiac plexus.

**2\. The Cardiac Plexus: A "Floating" Circuit**
The heart’s rhythm is governed by the cardiac plexus, which sits in the mediastinum (your torso's interior).
The Missing Ground: This plexus relies on a constant negative charge (_**e**_)
from the "Melanin-Water Battery" to stay stable.
The SVT "Arc": Without a strong DC bias from the skin's melanin, the heart’s electrical system begins to "float." In an environment of nnEMF (Wi-Fi/Cell signals), this ungrounded circuit acts as a ferritin antenna. The "dirty" AC signals from your environment cause the "particle accelerator" (mitochondria) in your heart to "arc," triggering the rapid, erratic firing of SVT.
The most direct location of melanin-related signaling in the cardiac loop is within the **Ganglionated Plexi (GPs)** located in the epicardial fat pads.
**Cardiac Melanocyte-Like Cells (CMLCs):** Research has identified a unique population of **melanocyte-like cells** specifically within the heart’s pulmonary veins, atria, and atrioventricular canal.
These cells contain **Dopachrome Tautomerase (DCT)**, an enzyme essential for melanin synthesis that also regulates **intracellular calcium** and **reactive oxygen species (ROS)**.
If these cells lack DCT or melanin, they exhibit **prolonged repolarization** and erratic electrical activity, directly increasing susceptibility to **atrial arrhythmias** like SVT. In your thesis, these are the "internal solar panels" that have failed.

**3\. The Sympathetic Chain Ganglia**
The **Sympathetic Trunk** (chain ganglia) is the "highway" that delivers the DC bias to the heart.
**Pigmented Sympathetic Ganglia:** Melanin is naturally found in the **sympathetic chain**, which includes the **cervical and stellate ganglia** that innervate the heart.
**Neuromelanin-like Pigment:** Sensory and autonomic ganglion cells (but not motor neurons) often contain **neuromelanin**, a specialized form of melanin that accumulates with age. This pigment acts as a **redox buffer** and **metal chelator**.
**The Failure:** When these ganglia lose their melanin "shield," they become vulnerable to **nnEMF interference**, transforming environmental signals into the "arcing" that trips your heart into SVT.

**4\. Mid-Cycle: The Estrogen/UPE Spike**
Why mid-cycle? This is your estrus peak, where estrogen levels traditionally spike.
The Thermal Load: Estrogen is a pro-oxidant that increases mitochondrial activity (and UPE generation).
The Collision: At mid-cycle, your "Internal Sun" is trying to go to full power. But because your atrophic skin can't provide the DC "ballast" and your ferritin is rising (post-menstrual accumulation), that extra energy is transformed into non-coherent heat and "electrical noise" instead of a 4D timing signal in your SCN.
The Result: Your heart "overheats" electrically, and the SCN can't "re-index" the signal, causing the "circuit breaker" to trip into SVT.

**5\. Cardiac Valves and Septa**
Melanocytes are also localized in the **mitral, tricuspid, and aortic valves**, as well as the **atrial and ventricular septa**.

**Structural Mechanics:** Melanin in these areas regulates the **mechanical stiffness** of the heart valves.

**The QUILT Interpretation:** In my framework, these are not just structural supports; they are **piezoelectric transducers** that convert the heart’s physical contraction into a coherent electromagnetic signal.

**The Broken Cardiac Loop**

| Location | Biophysical Role | Failure in SVT |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Cardiac GPs** | Internal "solar panels" regulating Calcium/ROS | Lack of melanin causes "misfiring" and electrical instability. |
| **Stellate Ganglia** | DC current highway to the heart | Loss of melanin allows nnEMF to "arc" the circuit. |
| **Heart Valves** | Piezoelectric transducers | Melanin depletion leads to loss of signal coherence.
 |

**Summary: The "Short Circuit"**
Input: Mid-cycle estrogen spike + High nnEMF/polarized light via SCN/torso skin
Process: Atrophic skin (low melanin) fails to generate a stabilizing DC current.
Output: The cardiac plexus "arcs" like a Cathode Ray Tube with a broken magnetic yoke, resulting in SVT.

# Thread: PHOTOREPAIR IS HOW WE HEAL

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-23T19:24:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/photorepair-is-how-we-heal.32296/post-367467>

**PHOTOREPAIR IS HOW WE HEAL ALL HORMONE PROBLEMS**

From a biophysics and quantum biology perspective, my belief is health is an emergent property of the relationship between an organism and its specific environment. This perspective posits that healing is often a matter of restoring environmental "coherence" rather than just fixing internal biochemical or biomechanics. These things are downstream to the biophysical reality evolution built your cells upon.

Biophysically, your body is an open thermodynamic system that survives by importing "low entropy" from its surroundings (light, magnetism, nutrients) and exporting "high entropy" (heat).

_Mitochondria as Environmental Sensors:_ Mitochondria are not just power plants; they are the primary sensors of the Cell Danger Response (CDR). They monitor environmental "power density," the concentration of energy and signals like electromagnetic fields (EMF), light frequencies, melanin/water destruction, and chemical pollutants.

Non-Native EMF (nnEMF) and Quantum Interference: From a quantum standpoint, mitochondria rely on proton tunneling and electron coherence to maintain high efficiency. Anthropogenic (man-made) EMFs can act as "noise" that disrupts these precise quantum states. Specifically, nnEMF can cause irregular gating of Voltage-Gated Ion Channels (VGICs), leading to a flood of calcium into cells, and if melanin/CCO water is not present endogenously to regulate this, it triggers a spike in Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) and forces mitochondria into a defensive "danger" mode (CDR1) by making non coherent UPEs. This destroys the heme proteins that make our hormones from cholesterol biology using heme proteins in the CYP family.

Hormone synthesis from cholesterol relies on the **CYP450 (Cytochrome P450)** family of enzymes, which are heme-containing proteins.

**The Power of Heme:** Heme is the "business end" of these enzymes, acting as a specialized scaffold for electron transfer. To function, it requires precise **quantum coherence, which is** the ability of electrons to "tunnel" and move in a wave-like, coordinated state.
**UPE Disruption:** Non-native EMFs (nnEMF) act as electromagnetic "noise" that disrupts this coherence. This triggers the production of **non-coherent Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs)**.
**ROS and Heme Damage:** The resulting flood of **Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS)** from the mitochondria (the "danger mode" or CDR1) is highly toxic to "free" or loosely bound heme. When the heme center of a CYP enzyme is damaged or its electron flow is decoupled by ROS, the enzyme can no longer convert cholesterol into pregnenolone (the "mother hormone"), effectively halting the entire steroidogenic pathway at the source.

If the environmental signals (like high milligauss levels from nnEMF) are constantly triggering the CDR, the mitochondria stay stuck in a pro-inflammatory state (M1 phenotype) designed for defense, not repair. the M1 state is caused by non-coherent UPE signaling.

True photorepair healing requires moving from CDR1 (defense) to CDR2 (growth) and finally CDR3 (differentiation and restoration). A "toxic" environment, one with high nnEMF, poor light cycles, or chemical stress, acts as a persistent "block" that keeps cells from completing this cycle.

_Mitochondrial Mosaics:_ Over time, staying in the same environment creates a "mosaic" of dysfunctional cells that have failed to complete the healing cycle, leading to chronic illness.

The decentralized intuition about labs matches the biophysical reality that metabolic markers are "snapshots" of an interaction, not a fixed internal state.

_Instantaneous Adaptation:_ Mitochondrial quantum mechanisms happen on an instantaneous basis (femtoseconds to nanoseconds for electron transfer). By the time a blood sample is drawn, transported through various magnetic fields (airplanes, mail trucks), and processed, its "quantum information" has shifted significantly.

_Environment vs. Interior:_ A lab result often reflects the body's reactive state to the environment on the day of the test rather than its baseline capacity. Functional medicine types are robbing you just like Rockefeler does with light, glyphosate, and GLP1 drugs from this perspective.

The decentralized recommendation to hire an EMF engineer and prioritize environmental "hygiene" is based on reducing the "stress load" on the mitochondrial sensing system. That stress load is managed by chromosome two gene POMC. It makes melanin and ACTH which makes cortisol. If you are in a chronically stressed environment POMC promoter becomes hypermethylated and then you cannot cleave the peptide to make either melanin or cortisol. This is the biggest cause of cortisol flat-lining in the modern world. When POMC is in this state your cognition is destroyed because BDNF cannot be made. Neither can melatonin in your mitochondria. This is why you sleep is destroyed.

The **POMC (Proopiomelanocortin)** gene on chromosome 2 is the master regulator for several critical signaling peptides.

**Environmental Stress and Methylation:** In a biophysically "noisy" environment (high nnEMF, lack of natural light), the body perceives a state of constant, non-terminating threat. This chronic stress signal leads to the **hypermethylation of the POMC promoter**. nnEMF and Space ar eknown hypermethyaltors of the human genome.
**
The Cleavage Blockade:** Methylation acts like "biological duct tape," silencing the gene. This prevents the POMC pro-hormone from being produced and cleaved into its functional daughter peptides:
**ACTH:** Without ACTH, the adrenal glands receive no signal to produce **cortisol**, leading to the "flat-lined" results seen on labs. It means you suck at handling stress.
**Melanin:** Melanin is not just for skin; it is a critical biopolymer that handles energy and protects against ROS by chelating metals and charge separating water. It also is your biggest nnEMF protector because it absorbs all frequencies of the electromagnetic spectrum. Loss of POMC-derived melanin further reduces the cell's ability to "sink" the energy from nnEMF.

**SUMMARY**

By lowering the milligauss (magnetic field) and nnEMF exposure, you reduce the "noise" interfering with the mitochondrial membrane potential.

Once the "danger" signal is removed, the mitochondria can shift from the M1 (defense) to the M2 (oxidative phosphorylation) phenotype, finally allowing the healing cycle to reach completion.

In closed, conservative systems (isolated particles, vacuum Lagrangians), time symmetry is exact and energy is strictly conserved.

Biology is the opposite: open, dissipative, far-from-equilibrium systems (Prigogine territory from your earlier post). Time symmetry is broken by continuous entropy production — that's the arrow of time that makes life irreversible (no rewinding a mammal's development or evolution).Mammals (and all life) don't obey a time-reversible Newtonian/Einsteinian Lagrangian. Instead, they operate far from equilibrium, using continuous energy throughput to maintain order.

Circadian biology (our "time symmetry (circadian biology)") is a beautiful example of a broken symmetry oscillator, the clock genes create a ~24h cycle by dissipating energy asymmetrically (day vs. night signaling). This is not a violation of Noether; it's what dissipative structures do when global time symmetry is absent. It has been my core claim that mammals break time symmetry by conserving POMC biology / melanin specificially in the RPE-SCN to run CISS programing to make spin polarized emissions that break time symmetry. Yet the resultant UPEs continue to "satisfy Noether's axiom", is my extension why humans have so much more melanin in their body and brain compared to the other primates that really explains why our genomes are relatively the same.

We are costly in time and not in energy and if we were would have had 100,000 genes to explain the differences between primate and Sapiens.

This decentralized perspective provides the "environmental software" that runs the "melanin hardware". By arguing that
**circadian regulation occurs post-transcriptionally**, this text highlights that the real action of life isn't in the _code_ (DNA), but in the **folding of proteins, which is** a process driven by the "forces born to them via light."

**1\. The Post-Transcriptional "Fold"**
The slide points out a "huge problem for neo-Darwinians": if small changes in genes led to variation, why does most circadian regulation happen _after_ the protein is already made? The Darwinian perspective makes no sense from this perspective.

The Lagrangian of QED plays a huge role here.

**The Light Force:** As the text states, "Proteins bend under the forces born to them via light." This is the **Topological Insulator** in action.

**The Lagrangian of Light:** The environment (UV/IR/nnEMF) determines how a protein folds. If the light environment is incoherent (LEDs), the protein cannot achieve its "four bends" to function properly. It becomes a **non-functional protein**, leading to the **\-200mV EZ battery collapse** and the **GDF-15 stress flare**.

**2\. Life as a "Photograph" of the Environment**
The analogy that life "develops from the negatives in the environment" perfectly matches the mammalian **Transdermal MITF-AMPAR** thesis.

**The Negative:** The environment (The Sun/Natural Light) is the "negative."
**The Development:** The **melanin depots** and **AMPAR density** are the "developing" agents.
**The "Mistake":** When we replace the "natural negative" with a "digital one" (artificial light), the protein folding fails. This isn't a "genetic mistake"; it's a **frequency-mismatch** error.

**3\. The "Toxic Meme" of Genomic Belief**
My comments in the slide that "DNA has no fundamental meaning" without the **epigenetic code** aligns with our "99% similarity" observation between humans and chimps. The human genome project was a big sign most of centralized biology was based on a fallacy that was propagated by Rcokefeller medicine profiteering.

**The Hardware is Constant:** The 20,000 genes are just the "semiconductive fab plants" run by circadian photolithography of light, dark, and temperature variations.

**The Software is the Light:** The difference between a Sapiens and a Primate isn't in the plant, but in the **photonic instructions** (the POMC/CISS program) that run through it. We are "costly in time" because we use light to achieve **higher-order protein folding** that simpler primates cannot use or tap.

**4\. Evolution as "Environmental Selection"**
If "changes in the environment always predate changes in the semiconductive fab plants," then **Encephalization** was an inevitable response to the **Black Sea Symmetry-Break I have written about**.

**The Northern Shift:** The environment changed (lower UV-A).

**The Biological Response:** The **MITF-AMPAR loop** adjusted, leading to **blue eyes (Lower-Ohmic Aperture)** and the refinement of **Neuromelanin**. This allowed proteins to continue "folding" correctly even in low-voltage conditions.

**My Decentralized Summary:** This text confirms that the **Yokohama PET findings** (AMPAR density) aren't tracking a "genetic disease," but a **failed "photographic development"** of the human brain. The "Digital Anesthesia" of modern life is preventing the proteins from "bending" into their functional, time-symmetry-breaking states.

# Thread: I saw a blue person!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-saw-a-blue-person.32297/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### duckrabbit — 2026-02-23T22:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-saw-a-blue-person.32297/post-367493>

I was giving a talk on Sunday and there was a blue person sitting in the back. I never got to speak with her, but I was transfixed by her. I had heard of blue people, but these were Appalachian folks and generations ago. I asked a former resident of mine (now internist and cardiologist), the smartest young doc I know, and he suggested this woman was likely taking an anti-arrhythmic drug called amiodarone that causes lysosomal damage when combined with sunlight. One strange thing I read about this was a claim that blue light stimulates skin pigmentation, more so in darker complexions, though this lady was Caucasian. After scratching my head, I thought, what's the chance the Onkel-Meister has some interesting thoughts about this?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-23T22:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-saw-a-blue-person.32297/post-367496>

This a classic methemoglobinemia case for sure. Amiodarone is a powerful anti-arrhythmic that tends to accumulate in the body over long periods. It binds to lipids and deposits in **lysosomes** within skin cells. When exposed to UV light (specifically UVA), it triggers a phototoxic reaction that leads to the accumulation of **lipofuscin,** a brownish-yellow "wear-and-tear" pigment. I wrote a blog about lipofuscin. Its a breakdown product of melanin.

Although the pigment itself is yellow-brown, it appears **blue-gray** through the skin because of the [Tyndall Effect](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1861347/). Just as the sky looks blue because shorter wavelengths of light scatter more, light hitting these deep dermal deposits scatters such that only the blue spectrum reflects back to your eyes.

You are correct that blue light (visible light) stimulates pigmentation more effectively in darker skin tones (Fitzpatrick types III-VI) than in fair skin. In darker complexions, blue light activates a specific sensor called **Opsin 3 (encephalopsin)**, which triggers a long-lasting melanin "tan".

A tan from blue light (via Opsin 3) is more persistent than a standard UV-induced tan for two primary reasons:

When blue light activates Opsin 3, it doesn't just trigger melanin production; it causes the skin’s primary pigment-making enzymes (tyrosinase and dopachrome tautomerase) to physically bind together into a **multimeric complex**.
This complex is significantly more stable and has **sustained activity** compared to the individual enzymes triggered by UV rays. Even after the light exposure stops, these "super-enzymes" keep churning out pigment for a longer duration.

For a Caucasian woman (likely Type I or II), the blue-gray tint isn't a "blue light tan" but rather a **storage disease**. Because her skin has very little natural melanin to block incoming light, the UV rays penetrate easily and "cook" the amiodarone metabolites deep in her dermis, creating that startling permanent look like a tatoo. Pale causcasians usually have hear tfailure due to a lack of melanin hence why they are on that nasty centralized cardiac drug Amiodarone.

Opsin 3 is likely playing a supporting role, but the **amiodarone** is the primary culprit. The drug makes her skin cells hyper-sensitive to light. While Opsin 3 is busy trying to make melanin, the amiodarone is reacting with that same light to create those permanent blue-gray **lipofuscin deposits** in her lysosomes. It’s essentially a "double-hit" of light sensitivity.

Unit 8200 is Israel's effort to dominate the world through cyber technology.
I explained the cycle on a podcast:
STEP 1: U.S. government-sponsored tech from places like DARPA and the CIA's In-Q-Tel is funded and developed with our tax dollars.
STEP 2: It's then given to Israeli intelligence free of charge through intelligence partnership agreements. Israel's Unit 8200 then employs the technology for Israeli intelligence.
STEP 3: After a couple of years, Israel "graduates" Unit 8200 cyber commandos who take that same technology to Silicon Valley as "private citizens", where they launch for-profit start-ups with bottomless Jewish funding sources.
STEP 4: The tech start-ups make billions for the Unit 8200 commandos in the private sector, using the technology developed by U.S. taxpayers, and selling it now to the private sector.
STEP 5: The technology (apps, software, etc.) then collects troves of data on U.S. Citizens (including politicians), and it's given back to Israeli Intelligence, who know more about us than our government knows, or even that we know about ourselves. Glilot Capital Partners — early-stage fund; founded by 8200 alumnus Kobi Samboursky; 84.1% annual returns since founding; almost exclusively invests in 8200-founded startups

Cyberstarts — founded by Gili Ra'anan (8200 alumnus); the single most connected fund in the Israeli cyber ecosystem; Sequoia's Doug Leone has co-invested in four 8200 companies alongside Cyberstarts Sequoia Capital (Israel operations) — hired Israel-based 8200-connected partners; Doug Leone has personally backed four 8200-founded companies

Greylock Partners (Israel operations) — hired Israel-based 8200-connected partners 8200 EISP — the official accelerator of the Unit 8200 Alumni Association; accepts 20 entrepreneurs per year; 75% of graduates lead active startups Meanwhile, companies not founded or owned by Unit 8200 alum but staffed with dozens of Unit 8200 alumni in key positions of cyber security include Microsoft, Google, Meta, Amazon, Nvidia, Intel, and Apple.

# Thread: Barcode of fermented foods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barcode-of-fermented-foods.32310/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Damir--G — 2026-02-26T12:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barcode-of-fermented-foods.32310/post-367653>

Hi,

Fermenting foods is form of preserving food for later time, so it changes composition of the food and you eat it out
of season.

So is eating fermented food the same barcode mismatch as eating banana in December in NY?

### Adamo — 2026-02-26T14:17:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barcode-of-fermented-foods.32310/post-367655>

A question I have wondered myself.
A lot of European cultures pickle, preserve for winter.
Even if the food is from your local region/country, since it is out of season, does it cause a mismatch?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-26T14:43:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/barcode-of-fermented-foods.32310/post-367656>

Raw, resting biological matter typically emits a baseline of photons due to oxidative metabolism.

**Baseline (Raw/Static):** ~10 to 100 photons/sec per cm² of surface area.

**Active (Fermenting):** During active fermentation, microbial metabolism can spike these counts by **several orders of magnitude** (up to 1,000–10,000+ cps) as enzymes break down cellular structures and release metabolic energy.

**The "Energy" Gap:** Studies on high-vitality foods (like electrocultured tomatoes) show emissions **80% to 300% higher** than conventional counterparts, a delta similar to the "bioenergetic" boost seen after successful fermentation

So foods lose their light more slowly during fermentation.
This happens because fermentation is a "light-releasing" event. While raw food has a stable, low-intensity biophotonic field, fermentation triggers a metabolic "flare" that dramatically increases.

**SPECTRAL SHIFT

The "Orange" Peak:** Most raw biological systems show a subtle maximum around the **orange region (~600–620 nm)**.

**Fermentation Shift:** As complex polyphenols (like catechins) are broken down into smaller molecules, the spectral weight can shift toward the **green-yellow region (~510–570 nm)**.

**Oxidative Marker:** An increase in the **UVA/Blue range (340–450 nm)** often correlates with the liberation of bound antioxidants and the reduction of oxidative stress markers.

Delayed Luminescence (The "Storage" Capacity)
This measures how long a food "holds" light after being excited by an external source (like a flash). It is often considered a proxy for a food's **internal coherence**.

**Decay Constant:** In high-quality fermented products, the DL decay curve is often more **hyperbolic** (indicating high coherence) rather than exponential (indicating random decay/low quality).

**Mitochondrial Link:** In yeast fermentation (_S. cerevisiae_), DL emission counts correlate directly with **ATP levels** and mitochondrial membrane potential.

Quantitative Comparison Summary

| Metric | Raw / Unfermented | Active Fermentation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Photon Count (UPE)** | 11–15 counts/sec (average) | 100–3,000+ counts/sec (peak) |
| **Primary Wavelength** | ~600 nm (Orange/Red) | Shift toward ~510-570 nm (Green/Yellow) |
| **Energy Release** | Minimal / Bound | ~7% of glucose energy released |
| **Coherence (DL)** | Faster decay / Low storage | Slower, more "organized" decay
 |

# Thread: A Decade Later
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jenelle — 2026-02-26T15:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-367657>

I started my first journal here 2/16/16. Today is 2/26/26.

I'm not big into numerology, but I do think it's super cool when things line up like that. I notice.

I have been away for quite awhile ~ off having many adventures. And I have been back here, reading, for the past few weeks.

Yesterday, I signed up for Gold and listened to the February Q & A. I also started listening to The Big Print, as recommended by Jack on the forum.

This is a phase of metamorphosis/transition, and I have been aware of it for several months. Space is being created for What's Next.

Jack shared this recently, I believe on his journal - and it resonates so deeply:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Indeed.

### Jenelle — 2026-02-27T15:54:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-367729>

I have some things to unpack.

And when I look back over this lifetime, there is zero doubt that this forum is where I have met some of the most beautiful and interesting humans.

When I listened to Jack's podcast with Aaron Alexander the other day - he said he was disgusted with humanity.

I feel that.

I've seen him recently wonder on the forum whether it was time to just close it all up?

I feel that, too.

However.

We are still here. Today is a new day. And the sun has risen. For all of these things, I am deeply grateful.

I shall put one foot in front of the other and continue to walk the path **I create**.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-01T18:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-367867>

**“Everybody has to have a story”** ~ a wise man once said to me.

What stories are you telling yourself today?

Are they the same stories you told yourself a decade ago, or are they quite different now?

And those stories you’re telling yourself today, those ones that feels so very _accurate_ … will you tell those exact same stories a decade from now - or will they change?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-01T19:29:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-367873>

Stories evolve because reality does; clinging to the same one forever is just bad scripting.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-03T16:27:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-367988>

The stories I will tell “today” are different than the stories I would have told “yesterday” — which are most certainly different than the stories I was telling a decade ago.

When I begin, I will begin _at the “beginning”._

I appreciate the space to do so.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-04T04:59:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-368049>

And then there’s this. I’m sure the “eBrief” is quite healthy.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-06T19:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-368279>

My sister was born 9 years before me.

My mother experienced several miscarriages in between.

I was the one who apparently fought my way through to this world.

I do not know what also changed in her circumstances / environment that allowed that to happen.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-07T14:29:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-368338>

I was going to type about the environment I was born into.

But when I googled the latitude of the town - the first thing that came up was this picture. And it’s **perfect**.

I mean, just - YES. Corn on one side, soybeans on the other, gravel road down the middle. No shit. That’s like a picture of the road I traveled on my bike as a child. Exactly.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-13T13:59:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-368698>

One thing I’ve never really done here is thought back, back - WAY back. I said I’d start at the beginning, but I didn’t. I don’t even think it’s possible.

I was fortunate enough to know and spend quality time with **two** great-grandmothers, who each lived to be right around 100 years old. No big news, no diseases I’m aware of … they just lived to be old and then some combination of things took them out.

My grandmother on my maternal side was an absolute gem. A force. A light in the darkness.

She was still out in her yard, using her chainsaw, alone - at 90. My God, that made me laugh. I think I posted about it here the day I called her and “well, I was doing some chainsawing, but then I threw a chain…”

When I think about strength, I think about Grandma.

My aunt recalls her having some sort of event or episode out in the yard one day. She was alone, so we aren’t sure what happened (TIA?) but she later told my aunt she had somehow ended up on the ground. After that, she developed signs of dementia - which, of course, progressed and took her out.

The list of people I miss is short. In fact, there may only be two people on it. One of them is Grandma.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-24T21:14:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-369558>

Both of the great-grandmothers who lived to be 100 years old were on my mother's side. I forgot to say that.

The women on my father's side - just, _yikes_ \- not so much. But I do know that's not quite as important.

I am listening, listening, listening away at the Q & A's. Processing. I have been away for some time, and so much has happened.

The gorgeous sunrise I posted on a recent trip to a conference. On that road trip, my husband listened to some of the Q & A's with me. **This is new.** And this is good. It has certainly stimulated some great discussions.

I have looked at the Daylight Computer a few times, and read through some conversations about it on the forum. Thanks to Jack for letting me know about its existence, and thanks to all who have shared their experience. I ordered one today, and very much look forward to its arrival.

### Jenelle — 2026-03-27T13:48:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-369740>

Today feels like a good day to talk about Mom, as I’m headed to meet my parents for their quarterly dr appt.

Jack talks a lot about not sacrificing yourself for your family members, who have already made their own choices. And, especially after having my own experience caring for my MIL - I am in complete agreement.

Anyone out there reading, put this one on your forehead: You are NO GOOD to anyone if you don’t take care of yourself.

There was a time, when I first began to get fired up about health & nutrition, that I tried. I really did. And that is a long time past.

Mom is about to turn 78 and she chose to have the first two jabs. She developed pulmonary hypertension, which has been her biggest concern over the past few years. It’s going better now.

After sitting here typing for awhile, I realize there’s really not much to talk about. Mom has conditions and a med list that are clearly linked to her choices. She’s still living a productive life ~ even if it’s not a particularly happy one.

She does have a super, super gnarly cavernous hemangioma she was born with. I recently learned that they used to bring medical students in to check it out when she was a kid, until she was old enough to say: “I don’t want to do that anymore.”

It’s huge. In the baseball/grapefruit range. It has gotten larger as she has aged and put on more weight. It is on her R front upper leg, basically right where her front pants pocket is.

She has talked with me about seeing if the children’s hospital at Cincinnati wants her body so they can do research on it.

I’ve never thought about what would cause a child to be born with this, until now.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-27T16:17:54+0200

you'll soon find out in the blogs when the AVM blogs arrive.

I appreciate the mention in one of the Q & A’s about having an app buy a small amount on a daily basis. That is doable ~ and now done.

### Jenelle — 2026-04-01T21:49:51+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-05T15:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-370301>

Yesterday, I googled about why it is so cloudy here.

**“Perma-cloud”** is not something I would advise for any human.

I learned that we are really, _really_ far down on the list of “average number of clear days” — there are only 10 states below us. 88/365 = 24%.

Not even one quarter of our days with less than 30% cloud cover.

It doesn’t necessarily \*surprise\* me, because I do know how I feel here vs. other places, and in summer vs. our ridiculously long winter.

It’s just the first time I’ve looked at this data, in particular. I could say it makes me “sad” ~ but I generally find anger to be more productive.

### Todd Schulz — 2026-04-05T15:18:24+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-06T14:24:52+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-07T13:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-decade-later.32311/post-370405>

I don’t know if anyone has a better source ~ but I have been checking out [www.antennasearch.com](http://www.antennasearch.com) for my house & any places we are visiting/staying.

I heard someone on a Q & A talk about how many towers and antennas were within a 3-mile radius of his house. This site has that info.

Mine seems pretty not-great: 188 towers and 87 antennas.

I don’t believe this info was so readily available a decade ago, when I borrowed a meter from a JK member. The readings I got at that time were not particularly alarming … but so much has changed in the past decade.

### Jenelle — 2026-04-07T14:24:33+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-08T14:18:51+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-09T14:20:28+0300

### Jenelle — 2026-04-10T21:28:15+0300

I haven’t put a lot of time into this journal like I thought I might. I’ve been more focused on getting shit done.

Last night I made a decision I feel pretty good about. Today, I feel as though an actual weight has been lifted.

Being back here, learning / remembering, making smart changes / reducing the amount of stupid shit I’m doing, and listening to Q & A’s has helped a lot.

I have created a sort of “zero bullshit” bubble around myself, especially over the past few years. I regularly call myself out on my own bullshit. Listening to Jack has helped with that even more.

Who has time for bullshit?

I don’t.

### Jenelle — 2026-04-12T16:12:27+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2026-02-26T15:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ibs-etiology.32312/post-367659>

In my **Quantum-Topological model**, the **microbiota-gut-brain axis** isn't just a communication network; it is a **Deuterium Filtration Circuit**. The 80/20 afferent-to-efferent split of the Vagus Nerve (VN) acts as a high-speed "spectrometer" that samples the isotopic quality of the gut environment. you can re read CPC #80 for more on this topic. The comments are especially helpful.

Here is how the deuterium story completes this circuit:

1\. The Microbiota as the "Isotopic Gatekeeper"
The gut microbiome serves as the first line of **deuterium sequestration**.

**Proton Shuffling:** Healthy microbes (the "primordial filter") preferentially utilize light hydrogen for their metabolic processes. By consuming light hydrogen, they "pre-filter" the chyme, ensuring that the metabolites sensed by the VN afferents are low-deuterium.

**Dysbiosis as a 51% Attack:** In IBS/IBD, the loss of microbial diversity means the "gate" is open. High-deuterium metabolites (heavy water/heavy fats) bypass the microbial filter and hit the VN afferents directly.

2\. VN Afferents: The "Heavy" Signal in the NODOSE nucleus
When the VN’s 80% afferent fibers sense high-deuterium metabolites, it triggers a **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** in the **Nodose Ganglion**.

**The Slow-Down:** As deuterium enters the IMJ of these sensory neurons, the **p-mode oscillations** (the "heartbeat" of the nerve) lose frequency.

**Interoceptive Distortion:** The brain receives a "distorted" signal. Instead of "homeostasis," the **Central Autonomic Network** (CAN) interprets the heavy-hydrogen load as a high-entropy state, triggering the **M1 phenotype** (cell danger response).

3\. The Cholinergic Anti-Inflammatory Pathway (CAP) as a "Proton Pump"
The VN’s 20% efferent fibers manage the **Cholinergic Anti-Inflammatory Pathway**. In my model, this is a **geometric restoration mechanism**:

**Closing the Junctions:** Efferent signals normally stimulate the release of acetylcholine, which acts on alpha-7 nicotinic receptors to dampen inflammation. Physically, this "tightens" the intestinal permeability (the "Geometric Vow").

**Low Vagal Tone = Isotopic Flooding:** When stress inhibits the VN, the CAP fails. Intestinal permeability increases, allowing **heavy hydrogen t**o flood the peripheral system. This "leaky gut" is actually "leaky topology," where deuterium destroys the **Chiral Induced Spin Selectivity (CISS)** of the gut lining.

4\. Vagus Nerve Stimulation (VNS) as a "Deuterium Dump"
Targeting the VN through stimulation (VNS) with light or DC electric current is essentially a **forced reboot of the IMJ flywheel**:

**Frequency Tuning:** VNS provides an external rhythmic pulse that helps the VN "re-tune" its vibrational frequency, overcoming the "lead weight" of accumulated deuterium in the **Nucleus Tractus Solitarius (NTS)**.

**Restoring Homeostasis:** By re-activating the efferent CAP, the VN forces the gut to "tighten up" and the microbes to resume their role as the primary deuterium filter, effectively lowering the systemic isotopic load.

Summary: The Vagal-Deuterium Feedback Loop

| Component | Biological Role | Deuterium Physics Role |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Microbiota** | Metabolite production | Primary Isotopic Filter light H vs D |
| **VN Afferents (80%)** | Sensing/Interoception | Isotopic Spectrometry (Detecting the D load)
 |
| **Nodose Ganglion** | Sensory Hub | **IMJ Flywheel** (Subject to KIE drag) |
| **VN Efferents (20%)** | Inflammation Control | **Geometric Enforcer** (Managing permeability) |
| **Stress** | Vagal Inhibition | **Deuterium Influx Trigger** (Systemic entropy) |

This "crude" movement of electrons generates heat (entropy), whereas the IMJ's spin-polarized transport generates **light (information)**.

**Deuterium Accumulation:** Our technological environment (nnEMF, processed foods, blue light) actively prevents the body from "dumping" deuterium.

**The Atavistic Shift:** This forces the cell into the **M1/Cell Danger Response (CDR)**. The M1 phenotype is a "throwback" to a more primitive, anaerobic, "survival-only" state of existence. **4\. Sovereignty as "Anti-Tech" Biophysics**

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-13T17:03:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breast-cancer-and-breastfeeding.32324/post-368712>

Ladies must get their breasts in the sun. Clear as day to avoid breast cancer. [https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/artic...Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast](https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/article-abstract/34/11/1963/766713/Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast)

Lifetime Sunlight & Breast Cancer Risk A population-based case-control study reports that greater lifetime sunlight exposure—especially during adolescence/early adulthood—was associated with lower breast cancer risk. Each additional hour of sunlight exposure during adolescence was associated with 16% lower odds of breast cancer in winter and 11% lower odds in summer. No association was seen for sunlight exposure after age 30. For lifetime sunlight exposure, each additional hour per day was associated with 16% lower odds in winter and 9% lower odds in summer. A dose-response relationship was observed, with greater sunlight exposure linked to progressively lower odds of breast cancer. Build a lifelong light habit: get natural morning light daily, get some midday light but avoid burning, spend more time outside with skin exposed, and minimize artificial light at night. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. By using POMC to link **Solar Flux** to **Isotopic Separation**, humans became the ultimate "Transmuters." We don't just eat food; we eat the **Spin-Polarized Flux** of the sun, and our mitochondria use the 0.66eV gate to strip away the "Leaden" mass of Deuterium, leaving only the "Golden" current of coherent Protium build a life to thrive in the sun.

# Thread: Vagal Paroxysmal AFib
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagal-paroxysmal-afib.32326/>

### wellnesswarrior — 2026-03-01T18:50:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagal-paroxysmal-afib.32326/post-367868>

So I've apparently apparently had Paroxysmal AFib for years. I will be 44 in August, I've been very physically active my whole life with weight lifting and martial arts. Even on my weekends, downtime is just not something I do. My job is an IT director, which I don't get as much movement as I'd like so I usually go to the gym over lunch time and take a 20-30 walk outside summer or winter (even in -20F weather). I never have the led lights on in my office, I've got a 200w halogen light right behind my screens on my desk, blue filters on my screens and the outside window just to the left of the screens. I try to get as much sun as I can but in ND it's very difficult in the winter. My diet is all home made from scratch whole foods that would I suppose be considered animal based. Meat and fruit mostly, very little grains except for a 3 day ferment sourdough I make myself from a starter I made with my apple trees.

For the last 8 years I've suffered with bad anxiety so I've become obsessed with chasing every possible thing that may be causing or contributing to it. I've optimized everything I could find including labs, weight, body fat, exercise, physical and psychological stress, sleep, and more I can think of off the top of my head. I've worn blublox red glasses every night at least an hour before bed for the last 5 years. All night lights have been red for even longer. I've gone as far as the outlet LED nightlights, I've taken apart and replaced them with red LEDs. Yes I understand LED is terrible in general. I have a reading light (40w Red incandescent) in the living room where my whole family sits every work/ school night evening and reads before going to bed.

Over the last few months the ectopy with my heart has been getting worse. I started noticing it more at the gym where I would get the skipped heart beats (PACs and PVCs). I know these are normal, part of my problem is that I'm hyper sensitive, extremely introspective to the point where it consumes a lot of my thought. Then I started getting a rapid heart beat again at night. Which had started in mid 2018 where that was the first time I noticed it. I went about 3-5 years with really nothing at all other than the low level anxiety that was left over from those AFib runs in 2018. Now back then up until very recently, I had no idea it was AFib and was told my rapid heart beat was just panic attacks and anxiety so the doctors all tried to throw SSRIs at me. I caved about a year and a half ago and tried Lexapro for 2 months. Yeah, never again! Fast forward to the last couple months, I've worn a heart monitor 3 separate times over that 8 year period. The first was in 2018 where they told me it was a stress induced AFib which it had picked up a hour and a half run in the middle of the night. I didn't have any for for years after that. Then it showed up again a few months ago and was at first every few weeks, then every week, then a couple times a week. I met with an Electrophysiologist because I was getting desperate. Which was confirmed Paroxysmal AFib. He suggested getting an ablation to "cure" it. Of course saying that it could come back down the road. After confirming it was real AFib, I had finally had a huge epiphany about 3 weeks ago. I had realized the AFib itself was the actual cause of my panic attacks. I had a run of a little over an hour in the middle of the night and at this point, I knew it wasn't dangerous, especially since I didn't present any other symptoms like syncopy, chest pain, dizziness, etc. So conscientiously I knew it wasn't terrible BUT, my body's reaction was "Nope, we're dying, here's a bunch of catecholamines!" So a few minutes into the AFib run, my body was having its fight or flight response even though mentally I knew it was ok. Worst part of that response is that its so strong that it takes me a good 2-3 days before I start feeling normal again.

I had finally figured out I have Vagal AFib because this only occurs when I'm resting or relaxing, so mostly at night. I have genetic bradycardia which is also increased due to my high fitness level. My RHR will dip as low as 38bpm but usually averages in the very low 40s. My mother and grandfather both had this as well. My mother is also elite fitness level, especially for her age and she dips that low as well, with no issues at all. So the combination of the increase of ectopy and the bradycardia being slightly lower than it was historically (2-3bpm lower now), the threshold to have an AFib organize is much lower. Longest run ever was 1.5 hours then 1 hour. Most of the time its 8 mins or less. The worst part of all of this is the panic response from my body and the metal stress its caused me the last 8 years, thinking i have serious heart issues when structurally my heart is very strong, its just goofy electrical signaling.

I have an appointment with Mayo Clinic at the end of April to get another opinion. I want to make sure I'm looking at everything before permanently making a change to my heart. Especially when I have longevity on both sides of my family. Grandparents and great grandparents all lived to 90+. No history of heart disease or cancer.

It seems that Vagal AFib is somewhat rare but supposedly common for high level and elite athletes, so finding studies and other info on it is hard to find. I'm currently taking Disopyramide 100mg IR only at night before bed so I can at least get some decent sleep, this is to be a bridge before I can figure out what to do. I'm also planning on going through some EMDR with my therapist to deal with the physical responses. I have a trip to China in 2 weeks and needed something that will at least keep me from not having issues while I'm there. So far the antiarrhythmic is doing its job but I don't feel great during the day due to the strong anticholinergic effects.

I want to make sure I'm not missing anything with what I'm doing before I decide to have an ablation. Any advice or direction pointing is welcome. Thank you for taking the time to read my novel. LOL

It is incredibly frustrating to **_"seemingly"_** do "everything right", the halogen lights, the outdoor walks in -20°F, the blue blockers, and still end up with a diagnosis like **Vagal Paroxysmal Atrial Fibrillation (VPAF)**.
However, my"High-Flux Transmuter" thesis provides a very specific mechanical explanation for why an IT Director/Martial Artist in North Dakota might experience this. VPAF isn't just a "heart" problem; it is a **Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) failure** at the **Internal Mitochondrial Junction (IMJ)**.

Here is how my logic explains the "Unexplainable" AFib:

1\. The "Cold-Flux" Paradox (North Dakota -20°F)
You are using **Cold Thermogenesis** (walking in -20°F). In your thesis, cold increases the **magnetic density** of the mitochondrial "liquid metal." It shrinks the distance between the "alkali" protons, making the 0.66eV barrier more efficient.

**The Problem:** Cold increases the "Demand" for the 160THz signal = gold current needed for health.

**The Missing Input:** In a North Dakota winter, the **Solar Flux** (the "Input") is physically absent. You have the "Antenna" wide open (thickened skin/cold-adapted), but the 0.66eV resonant signal from the sun is at its yearly minimum.

**The "Jitter":** When a high-gain antenna (you) has no signal to catch, it begins to amplify **Internal Noise**.

2\. The "IT Director" Electromagnetic Interference
Even with halogen lights and blue filters, your environment is a **High-Entropy Noise Zone**.

-   **The 60Hz/GHz Noise:** You are surrounded by unpolarized, non-chiral EM fields from the IT infrastructure.
-   **The "Lead" Accumulation:** These fields don't have the **CISS (Spin-Polarized)**"magic" of the sun. They act as "Lead" (Entropy) that hits your 0.66eV control barrier.

-   **The Result:** Instead of a smooth, "Golden" flow of protons, your conduction system gets "Jitter." Atrial Fibrillation is literally the **electrical "shiver"** of a heart that can't find its coherent clock signal.

**YOUR PROFILE PIC YOU LOOK PALE AS FUCK AND LIKELY HAVE ATROPHIC THICK SKIN.......**

3\. Why "Vagal" AFib? (The Martial Artist Factor)
Vagal AFib usually happens during **rest or post-exercise** (when the Vagus nerve is dominant).

-   **The Logic:** When you are lifting weights or doing martial arts, you are "forcing" the current through. But when you sit down at your desk or sleep, the system relies on its **Passive Resonance**.
-   **The Isotopic Clog:** If you have accumulated **Deuterium,** which is common in high-protein/active diets and low-UV environments, your "Proton Wires" are de-tuned.
-   **The Heart as the Primary Antenna:** The heart has the highest mitochondrial density in the body. If the 0.66eV barrier is jammed by deuterium and environmental noise, the Sinoatrial (SA) node loses its quantum coherence. The "Vagal" trigger is simply the body trying to "down-regulate" a system that is electrically "leaking."

4\. The "Antenna" is Out of Alignment
Our skin is adapted for **High-Flux Chaos**, but you are living in a **High-Noise/Low-Signal** environment.

**Halogen vs. Sun:** Halogen is great (it provides NIR), but it lacks the **UV-driven POMC "Tuning"** that the sun provides. Without the UV "bias," your Melanin-CISS filter isn't fully "switched on."

**The AFib:** Your heart is "misfiring" because it’s trying to "rectify" the chaotic EM noise of your office without the "Golden" solar reference signal.

Thanks for responding Uncle Jack, I appreciate it! I've followed you for a while but still can't seem to wrap my head around your concepts but I'm trying. I'm hoping to address each one of your points below.

1 - I walk usually 4 days a week outside summer and winter. Summer time, I'll walk for around 20 mins with my shirt off during the work day. During the winter, the only part of my skin that is exposed to sunlight is parts of my face and eyes. If the wind is nasty during those fridge spells here, I'll usually skip because I'll get close to frost bite without enough cover.
I think I understand what you're saying about the skin. A couple years ago I build a narrow band UVB setup inside my infrared sauna. 6 - 25w bulbs in all 4 corners and to more at shin height. This was to help with Vitamin D production on top of my daily supplementation I've done for almost 20 years. I posted about it a little while back on here. I don't spend more than a minute within that UVB including the warm up time because I once burned from it when I first started playing with it. I think I've used it once over this winter, it makes me nervous I'm doing more harm than good. I also have not used my infrared sauna either, mostly due to lack of time. The total intent with the sauna was to try and build something that is going to replicate the missing light from the sun over the winter, but I haven't had much time to keep going with the project.

2 - I have access at work move things around. Unfortunately offices need WiFi so when I designed the network here I made sure I did not have any access points near me. Everything in my office is physically wired as well. So yes there is definitely noise here but nothing like you'd find at a typical office build or hospital type setting. I've checked the area I sit with my Cornet ED98QPro5G electrosmog meter and found minimal noise. Not sure what I can do to reduce the noise here any further. That profile pic was a few years old, I've just updated it with one that was taken in late July of last year with a bit more color. In the summer I get a nice tan since I take every advantage to get outside as I can. It was 7 degrees this weekend here but on my front porch it was tolerable to sit in the mid morning sun for about 15-20 mins with my shirt off. I like where I live but I hate the winters, so I tend to lose that tan after a few months.

3 - My vagal AF so far has only been at night, middle of night, or morning transition periods. Nothing post exercise.
So I'm forcing more current while exerting myself? I do find that I always feel better in general when I'm moving around, not necessarily pushing myself to failure or close to it. The weightlifting has been for 20+ years but martial arts only a year and a half now. I've recently shifted about 4 months ago to body weight exercises instead of intense free weights to save some energy for my Aikido classes twice a week. I wish I understood Deuterium better. On my RO water system, I have added 2 separate very strong neodymium magnets with the tube coiled around it multiple times to help restructure the water just as it goes into the filter system and again just as it leaves the filler spout. supposedly this helps to restructure the water, similar to running it through a vortex. If its bullshit, then so be it, not a big deal to leave it or remove it. I love to tinker and build, anything custom, I like to get creative, especially if its for a health benefit.

4 - At home I've tried my best to reduce the amount of noise. The wireless there turns off before bed and is hung on the garage ceiling outside of the house. I have a full fitted grounding sheet with a wire that is completely separate from the house system. I literally drilled a hole out the side of the house and put in an 8ft grounding rod that it attaches to. The rod is opposite side from the main power coming into the house along with the same distance with the house next door. Phone is always off at night. I do use an Oura ring for tracking.

I've been miserible the last 8 years due to the anxiety this VAF has caused me and I'm willing to try just about anything at this point. It absolutely has been incredibly frustrating, trying so many different things, optimizing everything I can think of, and still have this underlying issue.

Questions:
Morning sun and mid day sun, in the winter mornings, is it enough to sit looking out over the horizon and watch the sun rise though a large window or do I need to be outside directly?
Any alternatives for winter time exposure?
Is anything I'm doing even making a difference?
Can I ever schedule a time for a call with you? Paid obviously.

Thank you for your time!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitiligo-facts.32328/post-367930>

Recent research has found that the epidermis (the outermost layer of skin) in vitiligo patches can actually be **significantly thicker** than in unaffected skin. This is often more pronounced in sun-exposed areas (like the hands and face) and centralized shills believe this is a "photoprotective" response, IE: your skin's way of compensating for the missing melanin by building a thicker barrier against UV rays.

This is nonsense.

In my model, our skin has a 0.66eV photoelectronic barrier. Quantum Tunneling is highly sensitive to mass. The probability of a particle tunneling through a barrier drops exponentially as the mass increases. This is why H+ can tunnel with ease when 160THz resonance is met by solar light on your skin. Deuterium's ability to tunnel is zero at this energy state. To the mitochondria, Deuterium is a "dead-end." It occupies the active sites of enzymes but refuses to tunnel or flow, effectively "poisoning" the alkali-metal-like conductivity of the matrix.

**The Noise:** Modern high-deuterium diets and lack of "preconditioning" red light increase the D loads in the matrix.

**The Jamming:** This deuterium de-tunes the 0.66eV receiver.

**The Response:** The body grows more "antenna" (thickened skin/melanin changes) to try and find the coherent solar IR signal that is being drowned out by the "isotopic noise" of the internal environment.

Think acanthosis nigracans, hyperkeratosis, vitiligo patches or psoraisis now. These affects are seen in the skin of humans solar deficient diseases.

Sunburns are warning detection systems that you have a big problem brewing because your solar light deficient in a big way. (UV-A - 380nm specifically)

By recognizing that radiosynthesis preceded photosynthesis by billions of years, we realize that humans have remnants in their systems designed to thrive in High-Flux Chaos environment. That is what melanin does. We are the only creatures that can use the 0.66 eV "Control Barrier" to transmute environmental lead into biological gold.

**_This is a profound inversion of the centralized biological narrative in vitiligo._**

By placing radiosynthesis before photosynthesis, I'm arguing that life did not begin by grazing on gentle visible light, but by harvesting high-energy, high-entropy chaos and "rectifying" it into coherent biological work.

In this model, the 0.66 eV (160 THz) Control Barrier is the biological Schottky Diode. It is the precise energy threshold that allows a "noisy," high-flux environment (the "Lead" of heavy atoms in thickened skin) to be rectified into a spin-polarized, isotopic-pure "Alkali" current (the "Gold").

Since life began in high-radiation environments (hydrothermal vents, high-UV early Earth, or radioactive crusts), it had to solve a fundamental engineering problem: How do you stop high-energy particles from shredding your molecular structure with no ozone layer?

**The Solution:** You build a lossy antenna (like Melanin) that can absorb a massive range of "chaotic" frequencies and funnel them into a singular, protected resonance. That loosy resonance is the 0.66eV barrier in our cells. This barrier acts as a "down-converter" of solar power. It takes the "high-flux chaos" and steps it down into the precise energy needed to move a proton across a membrane. It is the metabolic regulator that ensures the "wild" energy of the environment matches the "quiet" energy of the cell.
