1\. The Chiral Folding of Tropoelastin
Elastin is formed from **Tropoelastin**, which possesses a highly repetitive, hydrophobic amino acid sequence that allows it to undergo **coacervation** (phase separation).

**The Quantum Spring**: This "folding" is a chiral process. In optimal health (M2 state), the elastin fiber is surrounded by a dense **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water shell**.

**Entropy-Driven Elasticity**: Elastin’s "snap-back" is actually an entropic force driven by the displacement and re-ordering of water molecules. Without **Solar IR** to maintain this EZ water shell, the "spring" loses its hydration and becomes brittle.

2\. The Elastin-Calcium "Crime Scene"
Elastin has a specific affinity for calcium, but in a "grounded" system, the **Zeta Potential** and **Melanin shield** prevent its binding.

**The Decoherence Shift**: When the **DC current** from the Melanin-Water battery fails, the **Piezo1 > NOX2** cascade begins. This creates an acidic, oxidative microenvironment.

**The Nucleation Site**: Degraded or "uncloaked" elastin fibers expose **anionic (negative) sites** that attract Ca2+ ions. This is why calcification often starts on the internal elastic lamina of our arteries when we are solar deficient. The elastin fiber stops being a "spring" and becomes a **template for hydroxyapatite crystals**.

3\. Aneurysm: The Failure of Topological Sovereignty
An aneurysm is a "blowout" of the arterial wall’s topological integrity.

**Elastolysis**: Chronic **pseudohypoxia** and the **sPLA2-IIA avalanche** activate **Matrix Metalloproteinases (MMPs)**, specifically MMP-2 and MMP-9, which "shred" the elastin lamellae.

**The Loss of the "Fold"**: As the elastin folds unravel due to a lack of **UV-UPE coherence inside the vessle**, the artery can no longer buffer the pulse wave. The mechanical stress (Piezo1) increases, but there is no "dielectric cushion" to absorb the energy.

**The Supernova**: The vessel wall thins and bulges. If the **BCL11A rheostat** is set to "sludge" (high viscosity/low flow), the shear stress at the site of elastin failure leads to a catastrophic rupture.

Elastin is the **vibrational dampener** of the vascular tree. When the **Deuterium "magnetic wrench"** ruins the IMJ alignment in the matrix, the resulting "chaotic noise" (UPEs) vibrates the elastin at its resonant frequency of destruction. It either **calcifies** to stop the noise or **ruptures** under the pressure of the mismatch.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-10T17:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-calcification.32362/post-368492>

65 million years after the KT event elastin shows up in vertebrate arteries. Elastin reminds me a lot of the pecten in therapod dinosaurs retinas. Modern birds still have these highly folded melanin geomtery (arrow below). Elastin is chiral like melanin is and this means it uses CISS to process electron and proton spins. While **melanin** derives its chirality from its monomeric precursors (like L-DOPA) and its complex supramolecular stacking, **elastin’s chirality** is more structural and hierarchical, manifesting at every level of its assembly.

Like all proteins, elastin is composed of **L-amino acids**, which are inherently chiral. However, the specific "vibrational frequency" I've described is driven by its high concentration of **Glycine, Valine, and Proline.** Proline is a unique, rigid chiral amino acid that prevents the formation of standard alpha-helices or beta-sheets. Instead, it forces tropoelastin into a **random coil** or "asymmetric coil" conformation. In optimal health, these repetitive sequences (like VGVAPG) form **chiral beta-spirals**. This creates the "folded" geometry that acts as a spring.

**Coacervation**: This is the "phase separation" process where tropoelastin monomers align before cross-linking. This is an **entropically-driven, chiral assembly** that requires a precise water-shell environment to ensure the fibers "twist" into the correct functional orientation.

**Chiral Inversion (The Failure)**: In cases of **calcification**, the "chiral spring" becomes physically jammed. As the **Zeta Potential** drops in a vessel flow becomes more viscous and the **Deuterium** embedded in the arterial wall and not the blood indices a "magnetic wrench" introduces vibrational jitter, the elastin loses its chiral coherence. It stops being a dynamic "resonator" and becomes a static, achiral "ash" of hydroxyapatite.
_**There is a direct biophysical affinity between the elastin and melanin that few know about:**_

**The Exit Pathway**: The NCC that make Melanin has an affinity for elastin fibers, often using them as a "highway" to exit the epidermis and enter the lymphatics.

**Photoprotective Synergy**: While melanin in the skin acts as the "solar panel," elastin acts as the "mechanical capacitor." Both rely on **chiral induced spin selectivity (CISS)** to filter the high-energy electrons generated by solar UV, preventing the **M1 "supernova"** in the vascular wall.

I consider **Melanin like** the cell's **photonic spectrometer**, then **Elastin** is its **vibrational tuning fork**. Both must be chiral to maintain the **Topological Sovereignty** of the tissue. When chirality is lost, either through "dirty" metal displacement or deuterium clogging, the "Cosmic Spring" fails, leading to the **aneurysm** or **calcification**.

Elastin, is the highly folded resilient partner to collagen lining blood vessels, emerged around 500 million years ago with the rise of vertebrates and closed circulatory systems, as detailed in evolutionary studies (Sage & Gray, 1979, pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov). Born from tropoelastin, a 65-kDa protein cross-linked by desmosines, elastin provided elasticity to tissues under cyclic stress, arteries, lungs, and skin, its hydrophobic domains coacervating into fibers that stretched and recoiled like a cosmic spring. Its evolution coincided with the appearance of closed circulatory systems in early jawed fish (gnathostomes), a biophysical necessity to handle pulsatile blood flow driven by oxygen-rich hearts.

Elastin’s structure, a network of elastic fibers, evolved to absorb mechanical energy, dissipating stress via vibrational modes enhanced by UV-driven water structuring in the ECM, a photo-bioelectric adaptation to maintain tissue resilience under light’s guidance. Elastin’s biophysical basis lies in its entropy-driven elasticity, its disordered, hydrophobic domains reduce entropy upon stretching, recoiling via hydrophobic hydration, a process amplified by light-structured water (Geiger, 2007, The liquid structure of elastin).

Quantum mechanically, elastin exhibits coherence through its disordered structure, acting as a quantum resonator for vibrational energy. The hydrophobic domains, rich in glycine and proline, support quantum tunneling of protons during recoil, while UV light induces electronic transitions in its peptide bonds, generating UPEs that synchronize with collagen’s photonic network, ensuring tissue-level quantum coherence.

This quantum elasticity minimizes entropy (folding geometry), aligning with the free energy principle, and protects tissues from mechanical and bioelectric stress in a light-driven world.Fibrillin, the orchestrator of elastic fiber assembly, evolved shortly after elastin, around 450 million years ago, as vertebrates developed complex tissues requiring structural precision (Keeley et al., 2002). Fibrillin-1, a 350-kDa glycoprotein, forms microfibrils that scaffold elastin deposition, creating extensible tissues like the aorta and dermis.

Its evolution was driven by the need for precise mechanical control in dynamic tissues, with fibrillin’s calcium-binding EGF-like domains providing tensile strength and flexibility (folding). Fibrillin’s biophysical basis lies in its role as a mechano-sensor, its microfibrils transduce mechanical stress into bioelectric signals, a photo-bioelectric adaptation enhanced by light-structured ECM water. Quantum mechanically, fibrillin’s periodic domains should entangle photons, contributing to UPE coherence, while its hydration shell supports proton tunneling, amplifying the quantum network of collagen and elastin, ensuring tissue resilience and coherence.

This evolutionary and quantum mapping of **Elastin** and **Fibrillin** provides a bio-"mechanical" layer to my **Topological Manifesto**. By situating their emergence at the dawn of **closed circulatory systems** (500 Mya), I’ve identified the hardware required to manage the newly formed **Pulsatile Pressure** of an oxygen-rich, high-redox vertebrate body.

1\. The "Cosmic Spring" as a Quantum Resonator

**The Entropy-Driven Recoil**: As my references (Geiger, 2007) suggest, Elastin is an **entropic engine**. When it stretches, it forces water into a high-order state; when it recoils, it releases that "stored" entropy.

**The UV-Amplifier**: In myframework, this recoil is not just mechanical. The **UV-driven water structuring** (EZ water in blood) around the hydrophobic domains acts as a lubricant and a dielectric buffer. This allows for **Proton Tunneling** during the recoil phase, ensuring that the energy dissipated is handled electrically, not just thermally.

2\. Fibrillin: The "Scaffold of Precision"

**The Mechano-Sensor**: Fibrillin-1 is the **scaffold** that ensures the "Cosmic Spring" of elastin is laid down with chiral geometric precision. Its **calcium-binding EGF-like domains** are the key.

**The Bioelectric Antenna**: Fibrillin microfibrils act as the **transducers** that turn mechanical pulse-waves into **bioelectric signals**. In a light-structured ECM, these periodic domains **entangle photons**, ensuring that the UPEs generated by the mitochondria are "broadcast" coherently through the vascular wall.

3\. The Failure: Aneurysm and Calcification
Now we can see exactly why the "wheels fall off":

**The Copper Connection (LOX)**: **Lysyl Oxidase (LOX)**, a copper-dependent enzyme, is the "weaver" that cross-links tropoelastin into the functional fiber. If the **Cu-Melanin-RAR** spectrometer is "blinded" by dirty metals or glyphosate, LOX fails. The result is "un-stitched" elastin, the structural precursor to **Aneurysm formation**.

**The Calcium Trap**: Fibrillin’s EGF-like domains **require calcium** for structural integrity. However, when the **Zeta Potential** drops and the **Piezo1 > NOX2** cascade begins, the "free" calcium in the "sludge" blood begins to bind to the _wrong_ sites on the elastin/fibrillin complex.

**The Frozen Spring**: This is **Aberrant Calcification**. The "Cosmic Spring" becomes petrified by hydroxyapatite because it lost the **UV-UPE coherence** and the **EZ water shield** that normally keeps calcium in a liquid, signaling state.

4\. The Vascular Pecten link
The **Elastin-Fibrillin-Collagen** triad is the **Vascular Pecten**. It is a folded, periodic, quantum-coherent antenna designed to handle the "Archean Chaos" of pressurized blood flow.

**Aneurysm** is the failure of the "Stitch" (Copper/LOX).

**Calcification** is the failure of the "Shield" (Melanin/UV/Water).

**Bifurcation Geometry**: Human anatomy is riddled with Y-junctions (carotids, iliacs) that are natural **Reynolds Number** traps. These high **Oscillatory Shear Index (OSI)** regions are "engineered" to experience disturbed flow and recirculation zones.

**The Design Trade-off**: To eliminate these OSI regions, we would need perfectly laminar, straight-line plumbing, which is anatomically impossible in a mobile, pressurized mammal.

The Convergence: Where Hydraulics Meets the D-Shell electronics
The "Mechanical" flaw (OSI) only becomes a "Pathological" crime scene when the "Rheological" shield (Zeta Potential) fails.

In the high-UV Archean world, Melanin didn't just "block" light; it **down-converted** high-energy VUV/UVC into "excitons" that were funneled into the D-shells of bound metals via chelation. This turned a lethal threat into a **rechargeable power supply**.

This is **Active Hydraulic Damping**. Zeta is no just about solar charging of the metals it is also about charge dissipation to the Earth.

**The Failure Point**: When the **Melanin-Water Battery** stops charging the **D-shell metals that melanin controls**, the blood's viscosity rises (Sludge). Now, the **Reynolds Number** at those bifurcations hits a critical threshold. The disturbed flow is no longer "buffered" by a dielectric cushion; it becomes a physical "hammering" of the wall. This is where **Piezo1** (the 2021 Nobel sensor) comes back in.

In OSI regions, the "Mechanical" shear stress physically deforms the membrane.

If the **Topological Insulator (Collagen)** is dehydrated (low UV-UPE flux), it cannot dissipate this mechanical energy as a DC current.

The **Piezo1** channel "trips," triggering the **NOX2 > Mitochondrial ROS > sPLA2-IIA** cascade we mapped above.

Why Aortic Calcification?
Calcification is the body’s "Engineering Workaround" for a structural failure.

When the **Elastin "Cosmic Spring"** in an OSI region can no longer handle the pulsatile stress due to **Deuterium jitter** and **Low Redox**, the wall begins to "fatigue" like metal.

To prevent an **Aneurysm** (rupture), the body precipitates **Calcium "Ash"** to stiffen the wall. It is a desperate, low-agency move to restore "Structural Integrity" at the cost of "Biological Coherence."

Its evolution coincided with the appearance of closed circulatory systems in early jawed fish (gnathostomes), a biophysical necessity to handle pulsatile blood flow driven by oxygen-rich hearts.
>
> Elastin’s structure, a network of elastic fibers, evolved to absorb mechanical energy, dissipating stress via vibrational modes enhanced by UV-driven water structuring in the ECM, a photo-bioelectric adaptation to maintain tissue resilience under light’s guidance. Elastin’s biophysical basis lies in its entropy-driven elasticity, its disordered, hydrophobic domains reduce entropy upon stretching, recoiling via hydrophobic hydration, a process amplified by light-structured water (Geiger, 2007, The liquid structure of elastin).
>
> Quantum mechanically, elastin exhibits coherence through its disordered structure, acting as a quantum resonator for vibrational energy. The hydrophobic domains, rich in glycine and proline, support quantum tunneling of protons during recoil, while UV light induces electronic transitions in its peptide bonds, generating UPEs that synchronize with collagen’s photonic network, ensuring tissue-level quantum coherence.
>
> This quantum elasticity minimizes entropy (folding geometry), aligning with the free energy principle, and protects tissues from mechanical and bioelectric stress in a light-driven world.Fibrillin, the orchestrator of elastic fiber assembly, evolved shortly after elastin, around 450 million years ago, as vertebrates developed complex tissues requiring structural precision (Keeley et al., 2002). Fibrillin-1, a 350-kDa glycoprotein, forms microfibrils that scaffold elastin deposition, creating extensible tissues like the aorta and dermis.
>
> Its evolution was driven by the need for precise mechanical control in dynamic tissues, with fibrillin’s calcium-binding EGF-like domains providing tensile strength and flexibility (folding). Fibrillin’s biophysical basis lies in its role as a mechano-sensor, its microfibrils transduce mechanical stress into bioelectric signals, a photo-bioelectric adaptation enhanced by light-structured ECM water. Quantum mechanically, fibrillin’s periodic domains should entangle photons, contributing to UPE coherence, while its hydration shell supports proton tunneling, amplifying the quantum network of collagen and elastin, ensuring tissue resilience and coherence.
>
> This evolutionary and quantum mapping of **Elastin** and **Fibrillin** provides a bio-"mechanical" layer to my **Topological Manifesto**. By situating their emergence at the dawn of **closed circulatory systems** (500 Mya), I’ve identified the hardware required to manage the newly formed **Pulsatile Pressure** of an oxygen-rich, high-redox vertebrate body.
>
> 1\. The "Cosmic Spring" as a Quantum Resonator
>
> **The Entropy-Driven Recoil**: As my references (Geiger, 2007) suggest, Elastin is an **entropic engine**. When it stretches, it forces water into a high-order state; when it recoils, it releases that "stored" entropy.

The **UV-driven water structuring** (EZ water in blood) around the hydrophobic domains acts as a lubricant and a dielectric buffer. ### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-10T16:51:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-elephants-have-large-brains-compared-to-a-rhinoceros-michel-crawford-shrinking-brain-book.32363/post-368487>

Does the elephant brain contain DHA?
Yes, like all mammalian brains, the elephant brain is rich in DHA. However, the way they acquire it differs from the marine-based theory Crawford applies to humans:

**Inefficient Conversion**: Crawford highlights that terrestrial plants only provide ALA (alpha-linolenic acid), which mammals convert to DHA very inefficiently.
**Scale and Quantity**: Elephants overcome this "inefficiency" through sheer volume. An adult
elephant consumes up to
**150–300 kg of vegetation daily**, providing enough precursor ALA to synthesize the DHA required for their 5 kg brain, even at low conversion rates.
**Specialized Metabolism**: Research suggests elephants and humans independently evolved similar adaptations in **aerobic energy metabolism genes** to fuel their "gas-guzzling" large brains. This is a centralized idea I do not ascribe to based on what I wrote in CPC#81.

**Why the Elephant vs. the Rhino?**
Rhinos (600g brain) and elephants (5000g brain) follow different evolutionary "strategies" despite sharing habitats:
**Gestation and Development**: You are correct that elephants are more "costly in time." They have a **22-month gestation period,** the longest of any land mammal, and significant **postnatal brain growth**. Their brains triple in weight after birth, a trait shared with humans but not rhinos.
**Social Complexity**: Unlike the more solitary rhino, elephants live in complex, matriarchal societies. This "social brain" requires massive storage for memory (to find water/food over decades) and communication.
**Trunk Dexterity**: Significant portions of the elephant brain (particularly the cerebellum and motor cortex) are dedicated to managing the **100,000+ muscles** in the trunk.
**Evolutionary Pulses**: Elephant brain size didn't just drift; it increased in specific pulses linked to climate shifts roughly 20–26 million years ago, suggesting they adapted to environmental instability through intelligence rather than just physical armor like the rhino.

In essence, while rhinos followed the "shrinking" path Crawford describes, prioritizing body mass and defense over neural tissue,
elephants invested in a high volume diet, long-term developmental strategy that allowed them to bypass the "DHA bottleneck" through massive consumption and extended growth windows.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-14T16:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-elephants-have-large-brains-compared-to-a-rhinoceros-michel-crawford-shrinking-brain-book.32363/post-368780>

While the elephant's CSF reservoir is an order of magnitude larger (roughly 7–10 times the volume) than a human's, this difference is proportional to their brain mass: Scaling: Allometric analysis indicates the elephant's 240 mL of ventricular volume is exactly what is expected for a mammal with its ~5 kg brain. Primate Outliers: Interestingly, research suggests that primates (including humans) actually have relatively small ventricular volumes compared to many other mammals when brain mass is taken into account.

| Feature | Human | Elephant |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Brain Weight** | ~1.4–1.5 kg | ~4.5–5.6 kg |
| **Total Neurons** | ~86 Billion | ~257 Billion |
| **Cerebral Cortex Neurons** | ~16.3 Billion | ~5.6 Billion |
| **Ventricular Volume** | ~20–35 mL | ~240 mL |

Encephalization: Humans have a much higher Encephalization Quotient (EQ) (~7.44) compared to elephants (~1.88), meaning human brains (and by extension, their CSF-holding structures) are much larger than would be expected for our body size.

This explains Neanderthals who had 125 grams more brain tissue but they went extinct as they headed away from the equator.

Nature is the way of seeing the truth. Only Nature penetrates human habit, pride, passion, and intelligence to reveal wisdom. Nature erects a boundary around us that tethers us to her. Sunrise and sunset are two such boundaries. If we stray too far from the laws in either, we pay a deep toll. The relationship between what we see and what we know is never settled. Each morning we see the sunrise. We know that the earth is turning toward the sun to create the picture we see. Yet the knowledge, the explanation, never quite fits the vision we experience and we never really understand how it renews us. That is due to a lack of awareness/dopamine that creates our need for transformation. This demand is built in by seasonal light changes.

The demand is not great at the equator but rises to extremes at higher latitudes. This is why life is sparse above 60 latitudes.Transformational change is a state of bold total annihilation or what we were to something we envision ourselves to be. It can not simply be a better version of the business-as-usual approach. Transformational learning is a shift in our context of reality and our point of view about ourselvesThe raw materials of nature are chaotic, but she uses her recipes simply to find harmony. We must find out about that melody in nature. Everything is linked. There is not even a small part in all nature that is a soloist, for every part is a relative substrate of one thing or force to another to create harmony and coherence of part in itself. This is how the sums of the parts, are greater than the whole.

Hi Anthony, yes you're right...I continued with my updates in my optimal journal, but perhaps I should have just continued with them here. I am doing well, though summer of 2025 ultrasound found a 1.5cm suspicious lesion in the same breast I'd had a lumpectomy in. I have been back in ES since November. I will need to do some imaging to determine if any changes have happened. The past month or so I have been experiencing swollen lymph nodes in the affected side with off and on aching/discomfort in the armpit/shoulder blade. I am hoping this is my immune system trying to move things out as everything else about my environment in ES is exponentially better than it had been back home. The only piece I truly struggle with is not having my partner with me for support but he is supporting from afar, and the above mentioned decades of emotional conflict with my parents that don't support me.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Ladies must get their breasts in the sun. Clear as day to avoid breast cancer. [https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/artic...Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast](https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/article-abstract/34/11/1963/766713/Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast)
Lifetime Sunlight & Breast Cancer Risk A population-based case-control study reports that greater lifetime sunlight exposure—especially during adolescence/early adulthood—was associated with lower breast cancer risk. Each additional hour of sunlight exposure during adolescence was associated with 16% lower odds of breast cancer in winter and 11% lower odds in summer. No association was seen for sunlight exposure after age 30. For lifetime sunlight exposure, each additional hour per day was associated with 16% lower odds in winter and 9% lower odds in summer. A dose-response relationship was observed, with greater sunlight exposure linked to progressively lower odds of breast cancer. Build a lifelong light habit: get natural morning light daily, get some midday light but avoid burning, spend more time outside with skin exposed, and minimize artificial light at night.

# Thread: 7th Day Adventist dietary advice......where does it come from?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7th-day-adventist-dietary-advice-where-does-it-come-from.32369/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-11T14:11:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7th-day-adventist-dietary-advice-where-does-it-come-from.32369/post-368572>

In 1837, a nine-year-old girl in Portland, Maine, was struck in the head by a rock thrown by a classmate. She spent three weeks unconscious. Her skull was permanently damaged. She emerged from it with seizures, visual disturbances, difficulty concentrating, and an inability to hold a pen steadily for years. She could not complete her formal education. She suffered neurological episodes for the rest of her life. She interpreted these episodes as divine visions. The visions, helpfully, were very specific. God was not merely offering general spiritual encouragement. God had views about diet. Specifically: meat was corrupting the faithful. It was inflaming their animal passions. It was producing lust, aggression, and physical debasement, and it was making the congregation difficult to keep properly pious. Ellen G. White published these revelations. She preached them. She built institutions around them. She co-founded the Seventh-day Adventist Church and became the single most influential figure in the history of organised Western vegetarianism. Her writings shaped an entire denomination's theology, which shaped an entire set of research institutions, which shaped the dietary guidelines that are currently framed on the wall of every NHS waiting room in the country. The woman whose neurological damage gave her hallucinations she attributed to God is, through a very direct and thoroughly documented line of institutional causation, the reason your doctor suggested you eat less red meat. She also, for the record, ate meat herself. Privately. Her own household records confirm this. Multiple contemporaries documented it. Her personal physician noted it. The church has never fully resolved the discrepancy. The visions, it turns out, were not binding at dinner. I am not mocking a woman who suffered a serious childhood injury. That would be unkind and it is not the point. The point is that the origin of modern mainstream nutritional dogma is not a randomised controlled trial. It is not a century of dispassionate scientific enquiry. It is a vision, from a head injury, in 1837, that told a woman meat was sinful, and that woman's followers built laboratories and professional associations and dietary guidelines, and here we are. Start there. Then work forward.

My Great Grandmother knew her and I remember hearing that Ellen would eat meat if that is what was served. My great grandmother also knew Kellogg. As her husband went to Battle Creek to learn how to build a Sanitarium like Kellogg and tried to build one in Fallon,NV at the turn of the century. It was a miserable failure. My guess is that Kellogg was the main driver of the health message and WHITE was just the messenger due to her “visions”.

**Insulator Interference**: Sphingolipids are highly insulating. Their massive accumulation in lysosomes throughout the vascular endothelium and heart disrupts the cell’s **Zeta Potential** and **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water**.

This leads to the **M1 Bunker Phenotype**, where the cell can no longer "frequency match" with the sun, causing the severe **neuropathic pain** and **hypohidrosis** (loss of sweating) characteristic of the disease.

These pigmented deposits suggest the **Melanin-Water battery** is attempting to "buffer" the excess charge at the RPE/SCN axis but is failing because the **D-shell metal batteries** (Cu/Fe) are "sludged" by the lipid buildup.

**Diagnostic "Optical" Shifts**: The fact that some female carriers are asymptomatic while others are severe (due to X-inactivation) proves that the **Topological Sovereignty** of the cell determines the clinical outcome more than the presence of the gene itself.

4\. Decentralized Management Strategies

**Grounding and CT**: Cold Thermogenesis (CT) is critical for Fabry’s patients to physically "compress" the lipid-crowded matrix and restore the **EZ water shield**.

"Survival of all exposed male groups was significantly greater… due to the effect of chronic progressive nephropathy in the kidney of sham control males"

### Rusty — 2026-03-27T11:12:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369737>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=369724)
>
> I have an iPhone and the code (\*3001#1234**5**#\*) results only show RSRP not ave or measured?
> What is RSRQ?
>
> Point of clarification, you say - “If you're below -100 dBm, Your phone is SCREAMING at the tower.”
> This is a minus number so is -90dBm above or below -100dBm?
> My RSRP at the time of writing jump between -112dBm and -115dBm

**RSRP** = _Reference Signal Received Power =_ how loud the tower is

**RSRQ** = _Reference Signal Received Quality =_ how clean that signal is

\-90 dBm is better than -100 dBm

Less negative = stronger signal

Closer to zero = stronger

> above -90 dBm → Strong, healthy signal
>
> \-90 to -100 dBm → Your phone starts working harder
>
> Below -100 dBm → Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation
>
> \-110 dBm or lower → Max transmit power, Max radiation

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-27T18:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369768>

> Rusty
>
> above -90 dBm → Strong, healthy signal
>
> \-90 to -100 dBm → Your phone starts working harder
>
> Below -100 dBm → Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation
>
> \-110 dBm or lower → Max transmit power, Max radiation

| dBm Value | Signal Quality |
| --- | --- |
| \-50 to -64 | Excellent signal |
| \-65 to -84 | Good signal |
| \-85 to -100 | Fair signal |
| \-100 to -110 | Poor signal |
| \-110 and below | Very weak signal |

A weak RSRP signal means more exposure from the phone when in use (mins/day) because it has to power up to connect BUT less nnEMF/RF exposure from the tower (24/7).

If I have an “excellent” RSRP signal my phone (when in use) connects at a lower power BUT I’m being zapped by the tower all the time.

Am I understanding this correctly?

### YaMan — 2026-03-28T14:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369792>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=369768)
>
> | dBm Value | Signal Quality |
> | --- | --- |
> | \-50 to -64 | Excellent signal |
> | \-65 to -84 | Good signal |
> | \-85 to -100 | Fair signal |
> | \-100 to -110 | Poor signal |
> | \-110 and below | Very weak signal |
>
> A weak RSRP signal means more exposure from the phone when in use (mins/day) because it has to power up to connect BUT less nnEMF/RF exposure from the tower (24/7).
>
> If I have an “excellent” RSRP signal my phone (when in use) connects at a lower power BUT I’m being zapped by the tower all the time.
>
> Am I understanding this correctly?

sounds good; these measurements are used by the device to ultimately determine and report the link‘s quality to the base station and the power with which the device will transmit to the base station when it has something (control or user data) to send. A weaker (stronger) link will require more (less) power from the device to bridge the distance and ensure detection at the base station. Both the base station (tower) and the device (your mobile phone) communicate in duplex mode multiple times per second to exchange control data (information necessary to keep the connection alive) and user data (whenever you are downloading or uploading data and/or voice). The base station also broadcasts signals to its coverage area for any phone to detect and connect to it. So basically you are being „zapped“ constantly whether you have a phone or not. With the phone active, zapping goes both ways.

> Rusty, I have a Cold Plunge that I made from a deep freeze. I have this outside in the garden and would like to ground the water in it.
> Would taking a wire from a grounding pad next to the plunge pool risk bringing in stray emf's?
> The wire should only be about 1.5 meters long. We have tons of trees around, but we are fairly close to a cell tower unfortunately.

Get an 8ft copper ground rod. Drive it into the dirt right next to the plunge, your garden soil near trees should have good moisture and conductivity. Run the shortest piece of solid copper wire you can from the rod to a copper plate sitting in the water.

A dedicated ground rod won't pull in stray EMFs, that's only a risk if you're grounding through your house wiring.

The cell tower is a separate issue though.. Grounding doesn't block RF.

Before you do this you need to understand the hazards if you do this in the wrong area... As this is different to being barefoot on the grass etc.. You are basically creating a dedicated low resistance path in water (which skin when wet/immersed drops in resistance further). If you do this in the wrong area you may be subject to fault/earth currents from nearby underground cables/appliances/electrical installations etc... E.g. if any earth potentials arise from nearby electricals you can become part of the circuit. To do it safely you need to measure certain things and know what is around you before you do this. I would not do this unless you understand thoroughly what to check before deciding to do this & understand the risk level based on the measurements.

### Rusty — 2026-04-16T19:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-371233>

**EMFs from Induction stove**

A Swiss research group tested 13 professional induction cooktops & found that most EXCEEDED even the already-high 1998 ICNIRP maximum exposure limits when a person stood close to the stove.

For the worst-case compliant device: current density in tissue exceeded ICNIRP limits by up to 24 dB..

A FACTOR OF 16.

The brain tissue of young children: overexposed by a factor of 2

The CNS of a fetus: can exceed general public limits entirely if the mother cooks at normal working distance.

And this happened on devices that had PASSED the official safety test.

Why?

Because the IEC 62233 compliance standard, the test that certifies your stove as "SAFE", measures the AC magnetic field at 300mm (1 foot) from the cabinet edge.

You don't cook from 1 foot away.

Your abdomen is at 0mm.
Your hands are at 50mm.
Your child's head is at cooktop height.

At 300mm: COMPLIANT

At 0mm: 16x OVER THE LIMIT

The test is designed to PASS not to protect.

And that's before you consider that the ICNIRP limits themselves are already deeply inadequate.

ICNIRP limits are set to prevent ACUTE effects.. enough induced current to directly stimulate a nerve.

They don't account for chronic, non-thermal biological effects.

Reba Goodman & Martin Blank showed magnetic field biological effects occur at 14 ORDERS OF MAGNITUDE below the thermal threshold ICNIRP uses as its baseline.

The WHO IARC classified ELF AC magnetic fields.. the exact type induction stoves emit at 20 kHz, as a Group 2B possible carcinogen in 2002.

ICNIRP's response in 2010 was to RELAX the reference levels.

So when the study says your stove exceeds limits by a factor of 16.. That's 16x over a number that's already orders of magnitude too permissive for chronic daily exposure.

You're not cooking on a stove.

You're standing in front of an industrial ELF AC magnetic field generator.

EVERY SINGLE DAY.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-04-16T21:05:00+0300

I have a NuWave induction cooker (in addition to propane).

No one leans against any cooker with hot pans on it . . . Get real.
@ 2ft away I get 5mG . . . BUT I very rarely even stand there. Where I may stand the reading is <0.5mG.
I set the temp and timer and walk away. They are great for soups, stews, keeping food warm etc. while doing something else.

### S Hudson — 2026-03-13T12:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bruxism-tinnitus-are-the-same-disease.32383/post-368695>

My X thread seems to presently be a bunch of muppets regaling the benefits of having MMA to cure their fecking bruxism. This got me to thinking what the decentralised dental philosophy on treating Bruxing/Clenching is, a condition that has increased in frequency in the western population base since COVID. Centralised philosophy is to concentrate on splint therapy and localised occlusal interferences, but that never gelled with me for I always held the belief that most bruxism is centrally mediated rather than created by the bodies reaction to wonky occlusion (it still perplexes me where that notion came from).

I'm wondering how much Jack's teachings come into the treatment of this, and i think the answer will be "A lot". For if we accept bruxism is centrally mediated, then surely it is just another chronic pain condition, which means it's all fundamentally a symptom of a low redox potential caused by mitochondrial dysfunction in this toxic world.

That also gets me thinking on how much Dr Sarno's theories (which seemed to me to be mostly about nullifying the nocebo impact of belief) on lower back pain still apply. The nnEMF environment now is much worse than back when Sarno was practising, and while the power of belief has been shown to impact mitochondrial function, I'm thinking that changing those harmful beliefs is now only a small part of the potential cure.

### digitald3 — 2026-03-13T15:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bruxism-tinnitus-are-the-same-disease.32383/post-368703>

There’s data that bruxism releases dopamine. So low redox and low dopamine makes sense as a cause of Bruxism. There’s also data that Bruxism happens as much during the day as it does at night. So the idea of making a “nightguard” is ridiculous. And often nightguards can make sleep worse. The corporate clinics use nightguards as up sells. Of late I have noticed that tongue posture an bruxism can be related. Those with low tongue posture tend to clench and brux more. So getting them to get their tongue up can be helpful. I believe low tongue posture is also a redox issue from birth. Often related to a tethered tongue but not always. The airway dentists believe people brux to open their airway. I suppose this is an issue in extreme OSA but it’s not the root cause so to speak.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-13T22:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-spots.32384/post-368740>

This from AI assist, obviously pushing the belief that all UV is bad, but the numbers are good to know, especially if your car windows are tinted. Being behind (almost) any glass is not the same as being OUTSIDE. Add to this the high nnEMF in most (ALL) modern vehicles.

Car windows provide varying levels of protection against UV radiation:

| WINDOW TYPE | UVA PROTECTION | UVB PROTECTION |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Windshield | 95-98% | 95-98% |
| Side/Rear Windows | 55-70% | 70-75% |

-   Windshields: Made of laminated glass, they block a significant amount of both UVA and UVB rays.
-   Side and Rear Windows: Typically made of tempered glass, they offer much less protection against UVA rays, which are more harmful and penetrate deeper into the skin.

### Infrared Protection​

Car windows are not specifically designed to filter infrared (IR) radiation. However, some window tints can reduce heat and glare by reflecting IR rays. The effectiveness of these tints varies based on the type of film used:

| TINT TYPE | IR REJECTION |
| --- | --- |
| Dyed | Moderate |
| Metallic | High |
| Ceramic | Very High |

-   Ceramic Tints: These provide the best overall protection against both UV and IR radiation, helping to keep the car cooler and protect the interior.

### Conclusion​

While windshields offer good UV protection, side windows do not block UVA rays effectively. For better protection against both UV and IR radiation, consider using high-quality window tints.

# Thread: Fasting and light

### tallweeds — 2026-03-13T16:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-and-light.32386/post-368710>

I found an interesting tidbit in the book, "The Science and Fine art of Fasting" by Herbert M. Shelton.

It is suggested that the "true mode of death" from starvation is from
want of heat, due, no doubt, to a lack of combustible material at the
end. Chossat found in starving animals, that when death seemed
imminent, restoration could be effected by the application of artificial
heat. The American Cyclopaedia (1873) recounts that M. Chossat
"deprived a number of animals (birds and small mammals) of all
sustenance and carefully observed the phenomena that followed, and
his experiments throw much light upon the subject of starvation. The
temperature in all the animals was maintained at nearly the normal
standard until the last day of life, when it began rapidly to fall. The
animals, previously restless, now became quiet, as if stupefied; they
fell over on their side, unable to stand; the breathing became slower
and slower, the pupils dilated, the insensibility grew more profound,
and death took place either quietly or attended with convulsions. If,
when these phenomena were fully developed, external warmth was
applied, the animals revived, their muscular force returned, they moved
or flew about the room, and took greedily the food that was presented
to them. If now they were again left to themselves, they speedily
perished; but if the external temperature was maintained until the
food taken was digested (and from the feeble condition of their digestive
organs this often took many hours), they recovered. The immediate
cause of death seemed to be cold rather than starvation"

### [The Science and Fine art of Fasting - Herbert M. Shelton : Free Download, Borrow, and Streaming : Internet Archive](https://archive.org/details/the-science-and-fine-art-of-fasting-herbert-m-shelton/mode/2up)

This is the PDF of The Science and Fine art of Fasting by Herbert Shelton in English language and script. The book is a comprehensive, medical masterpiece on...

archive.org

# Thread: Advice on Nocturnal Hypoxemia Without Sleep Apnea

### luisedtr07 — 2026-03-13T21:52:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-nocturnal-hypoxemia-without-sleep-apnea.32389/post-368739>

I recently had a sleep test and I discovered that 50% of the night my oxygen levels were below 90% with an avg at 89% and as low as 79%.

More info:
On this report, the oxygen section shows:

-   baseline O2 saturation **89%**
-   average SpO2 **90%**
-   minimum SpO2 **79%**
-   time spent **below 90% = 50.3%** of the night (**3 hours 42 minutes**)

At the same time, the respiratory event burden is low, with **AHI/RDI 1.5/hour**.

During the day my oxygen levels are fine.

Based on what I've been learning from the podcast, Dr. Jack says that sleep apnea is the body protecting itself.

Would my situation fall in this case?

My concern is that I technically don't have sleep apnea just low oxygen at night. My current doctors says it's most likely due to a tongue tie and my lower jaw being smaller than my upper jaw, therefore obstructing my airways due to having my wisdom teeth removed as a teenager and one of my tooth breaking in my late twenties. I'm 36 now.

I'm debating if this is something I should address with releasing the tongue tie and getting a platte extension or if the sunlight protocol will fix the issues without having to do these external interventions.

Thanks so much for the help!

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-14T03:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-nad-in-biophysical-terms.32390/post-368757>

When a photon of light hits the eye, the retinal chromophore inside opsin proteins changes shape in about 200 femtoseconds.
That’s 0.0000000000002 seconds.

To put that into perspective, a nerve impulse travelling along a neuron takes milliseconds. In other words, the very first step of vision happens around ten million times faster than an action potential in a nerve.

Before a neuron fires.....Before the brain interprets anything…Before hormones or metabolism change…,Light has already interacted and changed your biology. When you meet someone who thinks taking HRT can solve and environmental light story tell them this so they understand how clueless they are.

That tiny molecule retinal (derived from vitamin A) absorbs a photon and instantly flips shape.

That microscopic structural change triggers the cascade that eventually becomes a nerve signal and, ultimately, sight.

But what I find fascinating is that this isn’t just about vision.

Life evolved under the sun, and biology is full of molecules that respond instantly and directly to light:

• Opsins helping set our circadian rhythm
• Melanopsin signalling daytime and night to the brain via blue light variances
• Flavins responding to blue light
• Mitochondrial cytochromes absorbing red and near-infrared wavelengths

So in a very real sense, the first step of communication between the environment and biology happens at the speed of light......

Sometimes we think of ourselves as purely biochemical machines, but moments like this remind me that life is also deeply photonic.

The sun isn’t just warming the planet and allowing us to see durring the day

It’s constantly sending information into biology
And the molecules inside us are fast enough to listen.
Nature is pretty amazing like that.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-28T20:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-nad-in-biophysical-terms.32390/post-369831>

In 1995, a nurse broke hospital rules to place a newborn into her twin sister’s incubator. The baby was not expected to survive. Kyrie and Brielle Jackson were born 12 weeks early at a hospital in the United States. Each weighed roughly two pounds. They were placed in separate incubators, standard practice to prevent infection. Kyrie gained strength. Brielle did not. Three weeks after birth, Brielle went into critical condition. Her oxygen dropped. Her heart rate spiked. Her skin turned bluish-grey.

Nurse Gayle Kasparian tried everything. She held her. She had her father hold her. She wrapped her in a blanket. Nothing worked. Kasparian remembered hearing about a practice used in parts of Europe but never tried in American hospitals. She placed Brielle into Kyrie’s incubator. Their father described what happened next: “She snuggled up to Kyrie and she was just fine. It was immediate. It was absolutely immediate.” Within minutes, Brielle’s oxygen levels were the best they had been since she was born. As she slept, Kyrie stretched her left arm across her sister’s body and held her. Photographer Chris Christo captured the moment.

The image spread around the world and became known as “The Rescuing Hug.” Hospitals across multiple countries began placing premature twins together, a practice that had been resisted for decades. Both girls went home healthy. They are now 30.

**HOW DO YOU EXPLAIN THIS UNCLE JACK WITH YOUR THESIS?**

**1\. Disrupting the "Antenna"**
Hair isn't just dead keratin; in your model, it acts as a **macroscopic waveguide** for light and EMF.

**Melanin as the Receiver:** By using chemicals like **resorcinol and lawsone** (as shown in the study) or bleaches that suppress **MITF and Tyrosinase**, you are effectively "shaving the antenna" of its ability to process UV-UPE signals.

**2\. The "MITF" Sink**
The study confirms these dyes **downregulate MITF protein levels.

The Systemic Draw:** Because MITF is a transcription factor, its suppression in the scalp (one of the most vascular and nerve-dense areas of the body) could signal a global "battery drain" to the brain.

**Loss of Coherence:** If MITF levels drop, the **\-200mV EZ battery** and the **pi-electron resonance** in those cells collapse. This creates a "short-circuit" at the very site meant to dissipate external nnEMF.

**3\. The "Light-Charged Battery" Short-Circuit**
If we live under **LEDs** (which provide no 1878nm/IR "ignition") and then **bleach away the melanin** (the "soil/capacitor"), we are attacking the Lagrangian from both sides.

**The Result:** You have a high-energy blue-light environment with **zero "buffering" capacity.** This forces the brain’s **L5 pyramidal dendrites** to operate in a high-noise, incoherent state.

**4\. Societal "Brain Fog"**
The 100% sensitivity of the Yokohama study suggests that **AMPAR density** is a perfect tracker for cognitive decline. If a significant portion of the population is chronically suppressing their **MITF-melanin axis** through hair products while living in a "light desert," the resulting **"brain fog"** isn't a mystery, it becomes a predictable consequence of breaking the **photo-biological circuit. It evolved to thrive in high-UV environments (like Africa) by maintaining high metabolic "resistance". In the modern world of "light starvation" and artificial flicker, this high-resistance hardware causes a **catastrophic impedance mismatch that leads to dementia.**

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-16T04:08:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apo-e4-and-dementia-risk.32402/post-368920>

Might this Transdermal Neuro-Optic linking MITF-AMPAR be how haplotypes came to be as an unfolding due to photo-bio electric resistance? It would seem to me Familial hypercholestemia would be a step in the direction of alteration of coupling efficiency from high to low latitudes after ancestors left Africa only to return to the Tropics?

I'm describing **haplotypes** not as random mutations, but as **adaptive biological "impedance matchers"** designed to maintain the **MITF-AMPAR-melanin** coupling efficiency as humans moved across different solar "voltages" (latitudes).

If the brain is a quantum computer, the **haplotype is the "hardware revision"** required to keep the OS (metabolism) running on a changing power supply.

**1\. Haplotypes as "Photo-Bioelectric Resistance"**
In Africa (High Voltage/High UV), the system required "high resistance" (high melanin/low vitamin D synthesis) to prevent **photo-oxidation** of folate and neurotransmitters.

**The Transition:** As ancestors moved to high latitudes (Low Voltage), the "resistance" had to drop. Haplotypes emerged to **downregulate melanin**(freeing up UV for the MITF-SIRT1-NAD+ "ignition") and **upregulate the AMPAR "gain"** to capture the weaker metabolic signal.

**The Unfolding:** This "unfolding" isn't just about skin color; it's about the **Mitochondrial Haplotype (mtDNA)** adjusting the coupling efficiency of the **Electron Transport Chain (ETC)** to handle different IR/UV ratios.

**2\. Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH): The "Battery Leak"**
My link to **Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH)** is the "smoking gun" for this theory.

**Cholesterol as a UV-Resonator:** Cholesterol is the precursor to Vitamin D (via UV-B) and a critical component of the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water**interface in the IMM.

**The Adaptation:** FH (high LDL) may have been a **Northern Haplotype's** attempt to "buffer" more light or provide more substrate for Vitamin D/Hormone synthesis in a low-UV environment.

**The Tropical Return:** When an FH-haplotype (designed for low light/high resistance) returns to the Tropics (High UV), the "coupling efficiency" is mismatched. The high cholesterol "over-collects" UV energy, creating **thermal expansion (entropy)** instead of **coherence**. This "shorts out" the MITF-AMPAR loop. This stimulus wil push cholesterol levels down in the Tropics pretty fast. If it did not react fast this would lead to the **mitochondrial collapse** and premature cognitive decline (dementia) seen in mismatched populations.

**3\. Implications for Disease: The "Latitude Mismatch"

Dementia as an "Impedance Mismatch":** If a "Northern" brain (high-gain AMPARs) is placed in a "Southern" light environment (or vice versa), the **GDF-15 "stress flare"** is triggered because the internal "UV-UPE" circuit is receiving the wrong "frequency."

**Topological Short-Circuit:** This mismatch dissolves the **topological insulator** (the melanin-EZ interface). The cytoskeleton collapses not because it's "broken," but because the **biophotonic "instruction set"** from the haplotype doesn't match the ambient "solar voltage."

**4\. The "Transdermal" Cure**
This implies that we can’t treat "dementia" or "FH" as purely chemical issues. They are **frequency-matching issues**.

**Rewiring the Brain:** We can use the **skin's dermatomes** to provide the specific "missing" frequencies (like IR-A volume or 380nm ignition) to "re-tune" the haplotype's resistance.

**The Goal:** Restoring the **\-200mV EZ battery** by providing the exact "photo-bioelectric" load the specific haplotype evolved for.

This is why I think **ApoE4, which is** often called the "Alzheimer's gene", is actually just the ultimate **"ancestral high-voltage haplotype"** that now "short-circuits" when exposed to the flickering LEDs and nnEMF of the modern world.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-16T04:22:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apo-e4-and-dementia-risk.32402/post-368921>

**1\. The High-Voltage "Antenna" in a Low-Wattage World

AMPAR/NMDA Sequestration:** Unlike APOE3, which recycles receptors efficiently, APOE4 **traps AMPA and NMDA receptors** inside the cell. This effectively "muffles" the synaptic antenna, preventing the brain from "seeing" the internal metabolic light (UPEs).
**
The Gain Increase:** To compensate for this "darkness," the system likely upregulates receptor density (the high-gain state seen in the Yokohama study) to catch any remaining signal, leading to the **excitotoxicity and noise** of brain fog.

**2\. The "Short-Circuit" (Mitochondrial Collapse)

Delta Psi Loss:** APOE4 fragments directly interact with mitochondria, causing **collapsed cristae** and a significant drop in **mitochondrial membrane potential**.

**The NAD+/NADH Drain:** APOE4 neurons exhibit a **lower NAD+/NADH ratio** and lack "spare capacity" to handle high energy demands. In my model, this is the -200mV EZ battery losing its charge because the "ignition" frequency (380nm) is missing.
**
Lipid Peroxidation (UPE Overload):** APOE4 lacks the disulfide bridges found in E2/E3 that protect lipids from peroxidation. This results in a flood of **incoherent Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs)**, which act as "static" that destroys the topological insulator's coherence.

**3\. The Melanin/Melatonin Buffer Failure

Melatonin Deficiency:** Post-mortem data shows that **APOE4 carriers have significantly reduced melatonin** levels compared to others. Without this "nighttime buffer," the pi-electron flux from the previous day's metabolic activity cannot be restored or "grounded," leading to permanent structural damage.
**
Retinal Opacity:** APOE4 is linked to **retinal structural deficits** and neuroinflammation. If the eye which is the brain's main portal, and is "opaque" to natural light, the APOE4 brain becomes entirely dependent on the **transdermal "remote-start"** to survive.

**4\. Evolutionary Implication: The Latitude Trap**

APOE4 was likely a "high-performance" haplotype in high-UV environments where it could efficiently handle intense energy flux. However, when placed in the **"low-voltage"**light environment of modern buildings and flickering LEDs, the high-resistance APOE4 hardware **cannot generate the internal coherence** needed to maintain the cytoskeleton.

In high-UV, high-flux environments, tight coupling is an evolutionary masterstroke: it maximizes the **proton motive force (Delta Psi)** to drive the "melanin battery" and structural coherence with minimal leakage.

**5\. The High-Impedance Trap**

**The Short-Circuit:** Instead of generating a coherent flow of pi-electrons, the energy "clashes," creating heat (entropy) and **lipid peroxidation**. This produces the "static" of incoherent **UPEs** that dissolve the topological insulator (the melanin-cytoskeleton interface).

**6\. Why They Are Most Responsive to Transdermal Therapy**

**Recharging the -200mV Battery:** Providing **IR-A (600-1000nm)** to the skin expands the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water** systemically. For a tightly coupled system, this "volume" is the difference between a "seized engine" and a "super-site" of energy dissipation.

**The 380nm "Bypass":** Using UV-A on the skin triggers the **SIRT1/NAD+** pathway, providing the "high-voltage" start that the APOE4 brain is gasping for, even if the eyes are blocked by lenses or blue-light damage.

**7\. The Yokohama PET Confirmation**
The **100% sensitivity** of the Yokohama study likely captures this "high-gain" state in its most extreme form in APOE4 carriers. Their brain is **upregulating AMPARs** at a frantic rate, trying to "listen" for the coherent solar signal through a mountain of modern electronic noise.

**8\. The Path Forward: "Dermatomal Dosing"**
This suggests a radical shift in treating dementia:

**Haplotype-Specific Lighting:** APOE4 carriers shouldn't just get "some sun"; they likely need **specific "doses" of UV-A and IR-A** mapped to their ancestral latitude to satisfy their high-resistance hardware.

**Skin as the Primary Input:** Since their internal "light-sensing" (via the eye/retina) is often compromised by the haplotype's inflammatory response, the **skin (neuroectoderm)** becomes the most reliable way to "remote-start" the MITF-AMPAR loop.

How important is it to avoid artificial light before sun rise?

**The Missing Link to the tweet below and your understanding of humanity: AMPARs and Melanin**

There is emerging evidence that melanin (specifically neuromelanin) and AMPA receptors are biologically linked, particularly in how they regulate cell structure and survival: Direct Regulation: Research on human melanocytes shows they actually express functional AMPA and NMDA receptors. Blocking these AMPARs causes a rapid and reversible change in melanocyte morphology, disrupting the organization of actin and tubulin microfilaments. This links it to consciousness and human encephalization.

**Melanin Production in the brain:** AMPAR inhibition has been shown to drastically reduce the expression of MITF (Microphthalmia-associated transcription factor), which is the "master regulator" of melanin synthesis. This suggests that the glutamatergic system in our own brains and skin (via AMPARs) directly control the "battery" (melanin) levels in the cell. This is neuroectorderm driving its own evolution via electrical resistance to unfurl into new morphology via the symmetry equation buried in the Lagrangian. This means humans have a transdermal bidirectional loop between the skin and brain that controls melanin production inside their heads.

**1\. The Bicarb Loop as a Deuterium Exhaust**
In biophysics, the production of stomach acid (HCl) is half of a much larger cycle. For every proton (+) pumped into the stomach to reach a pH of 1.5, a bicarbonate molecule (HCO3−) is released into the bloodstream from pancreatic beta cells. This is known as the "alkaline tide."
**The Glucagon Exhaust:** Glucagon, the hormone of the fasted/scavenger state, stimulates the pancreas to release this bicarbonate into the duodenum to neutralize the acid.
**Isotope Selection:** The carbonic anhydrase enzymes that drive this loop are highly sensitive to the Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE). They preferentially move protium (1) over deuterium (2).
**The "Heavy" Sink:** By pumping massive amounts of D+ into the stomach, humans effectively "trap" deuterium in the gastric juice or exhaust it through the breath (as 2) and urine. A pH of 1.5 indicates a much more aggressive "pumping" mechanism than that of a Lion (2.0) or a Gorilla (4.5), making the human "deuterium exhaust" one of the most efficient in the mammal kingdom.

**2\. The p53 Connection: Protecting the Pump**
The production of this high acidity is metabolically expensive and "electrically hot." The cells of the stomach lining (parietal cells) have some of the highest mitochondrial densities in the body to power this pH 1.5 gradient. Because of this their genomes need protection from methyl groups that are deuterated. CH3- groups have 3 hydrogens that can replaced by a deuteron. One D+ affects the the movements of 96 other H+ atoms in the human lattice.
**The Guardian’s Role:** p53 (the "guardian of the genome") is required to monitor the DNA of these high-turnover cells. If deuterium (the "grit") leaks into the mitochondrial Cytochrome C Oxidase (CCO), the "pump" fails.
**Antenna Gain:** As we discussed, humans have a high-gain "skin antenna." This solar energy fuels the p53-mediated repair and the metabolic pathways needed to maintain the bicarb loop in the gut. This is controlled by the MITF-AMPAR. When this goes awry many people develop SIBO and "food allergies." The SIBO occurs because bacteria in the gut cannot handle deuterium well at all. There is no food allergies. The GALT becomes insufflated with deuterium and this gives humans the illusion of food allergies. The real problems is they have lost their exhaust system and this causes deuterium to back up in the system all the way back to the brain. (PANDAS = gut association. psychologic syndrome)

3\. The synthesis of biophysics and isotope geology in diabetics is lost on centralized medicine and this explains why diabetics have a higher risk of cancer due to the concentration of the D on CH3- when the alkaline tied is missing in action. I'm essentially describing the human stomach not just as a digestive organ, but as an isotopic **fractionation chamber**.

By maintaining a pH of 1.5, we aren't just breaking down proteins; we are creating a massive kinetic hurdle. If the proton pumps (H+/K+ ATPase) and carbonic anhydrase have a high enough **separation factor**, the stomach becomes a "deuterium sink." **A few points that align with my "Heavy Sink" hypothesis in my decentralized thesis:

The Energy Cost:** Pumping against a gradient to reach pH 1.5 is one of the most energetically expensive tasks in the body. If p53 is the guardian, it’s protecting the "circuit breakers" (mitochondria) in those parietal cells from being "gummed up" by the mass of a deuteron.

**SIBO and Isotopic Flux:** If the "exhaust" (acid production) fails, the gut lumen becomes more hospitable to deuterium-tolerant microbes. The "food allergy" then becomes a symptom of **isotopic toxicity** in the Gut-Associated Lymphoid Tissue (GALT) rather than a protein sensitivity.

**The Brain Link:** If the bicarb loop stalls, the "back pressure" of deuterium would naturally affect the highest-turnover areas first, the brain and the gut (the enteric nervous system). This provides decentralized clinicians a very clean biophysical explanation for the gut-brain axis issues like PANDAS.

**5\. FARTING OR FLOATERS IS WAY TO CLEAR DEUTERIUM**

Scientific research on stool buoyancy is sparse but definitive, with two landmark studies defining the field. The consensus is that whether a stool sinks or floats is primarily determined by its **trapped gas content** rather than its fat or water composition

Key Scientific Papers and Findings

-   **The Levitt and Duane Study (1972):** Published in the _New England Journal of Medicine_, this is the most cited work on the topic.
    -   **Method:** Researchers collected "floaters" from 13 subjects and subjected them to high-pressure degassing.
    -   **Discovery:** All floating stools immediately sank once the trapped gas was compressed or removed.
    -   **Conclusion:** Buoyancy in healthy individuals is almost exclusively due to trapped intestinal gases, specifically **methane**, which is less dense than water. Stool fat (steatorrhea) rarely reaches levels high enough to cause floating on its own without gas.
-   **The "Genesis of Fecal Floatation" Study (2022):** Published in _Scientific Reports_(Nature Portfolio), this study provided the first causal link between gut microbes and buoyancy.
    -   **Method:** Using the "Levô in Fimo Test" (LIFT), researchers compared germ-free mice to those colonized with gut bacteria.
    -   **Discovery:** Feces from germ-free mice consistently sank, while feces from colonized mice consistently floated.
    -   **Conclusion:** Stool buoyancy is a biomarker of **gut microbial colonization** and the activity of gas-producing (gasogenic) bacteria.

**Biophysical Factors of Buoyancy

Density:** Normal human stool has a density between **1.03 and 1.07 g/ml**, making it slightly denser than water (1.0 g/ml), which is why most healthy stools sink.

**Gas Composition:** Floating stools typically contain higher concentrations of methane, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide produced during the fermentation of dietary fibers.

**Methane Producers:** Approximately 10–15% of healthy people pass "floaters" consistently; these individuals are often categorized as high methane producers. Methane is one of the better biophysical ways to eliminate deuterium via the micriobiome to CH4.

I’ve just identified Methanogenesis as the microbiome's primary Deuterium "Vaporizer."

**5\. FARTING OR FLOATERS IS WAY TO CLEAR DEUTERIUM**

Scientific research on stool buoyancy is sparse but definitive, with two landmark studies defining the field. The consensus is that whether a stool sinks or floats is primarily determined by its **trapped gas content** rather than its fat or water composition

Key Scientific Papers and Findings

-   **The Levitt and Duane Study (1972):** Published in the _New England Journal of Medicine_, this is the most cited work on the topic.
    -   **Method:** Researchers collected "floaters" from 13 subjects and subjected them to high-pressure degassing.
    -   **Discovery:** All floating stools immediately sank once the trapped gas was compressed or removed.
    -   **Conclusion:** Buoyancy in healthy individuals is almost exclusively due to trapped intestinal gases, specifically **methane**, which is less dense than water. Stool fat (steatorrhea) rarely reaches levels high enough to cause floating on its own without gas.
-   **The "Genesis of Fecal Floatation" Study (2022):** Published in _Scientific Reports_(Nature Portfolio), this study provided the first causal link between gut microbes and buoyancy.
    -   **Method:** Using the "Levô in Fimo Test" (LIFT), researchers compared germ-free mice to those colonized with gut bacteria.
    -   **Discovery:** Feces from germ-free mice consistently sank, while feces from colonized mice consistently floated.
    -   **Conclusion:** Stool buoyancy is a biomarker of **gut microbial colonization** and the activity of gas-producing (gasogenic) bacteria.

**Biophysical Factors of Buoyancy

Density:** Normal human stool has a density between **1.03 and 1.07 g/ml**, making it slightly denser than water (1.0 g/ml), which is why most healthy stools sink.

**Gas Composition:** Floating stools typically contain higher concentrations of methane, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide produced during the fermentation of dietary fibers.

**Methane Producers:** Approximately 10–15% of healthy people pass "floaters" consistently; these individuals are often categorized as high methane producers. Methane is one of the better biophysical ways to eliminate deuterium via the micriobiome to CH4.

During this **Neoproterozoic** **period,** life evoles the heart which becomes the Z-axis of the GPS system. The heart evolved over 500–600 million years ago, with the first primitive, tubular heart-like organs appearing in ancestral bilaterians (early complex animals) to overcome diffusion constraints. Over hundreds of millions of years, this structure evolved from simple pulsating vessels into multi-chambered pumps, adapting from fish to tetrapods.

Key Stages in Heart Evolution:

-   ~600–700 Mya (Pre-Cambrian): Primitive blood vascular systems appeared.
-   ~500–520 Mya (Cambrian): The first heart-like organs, likely simple tubular vessels, evolved to pump blood. The oldest known fossilized heart, found in an arthropod (Fuxianhuia), dates to about 520 Mya.
-   ~400 Mya (Fish): Fish developed a two-chambered heart (one atrium, one ventricle).
-   ~350 Mya (Amphibians/Reptiles): As creatures moved to land, three-chambered hearts evolved.
-   ~200–300 Mya (Mammals/Birds): Four-chambered hearts evolved independently in the ancestors of mammals and reptiles/birds for high-pressure, efficient oxygen delivery.

The evolution of more chambers of the heart scales to more vortexing to control isotopic fractionation required by the more complex nervous system. This isn’t just a biological history of a pump; it is the **Mechanical Engineering of the Z-Axis** to scale the **Isotopic Centrifuge**.

The Bacterial Lagrangian (): Focused on Electroweak agility. It developed the first primitive metabolic pathways to dissipate energy in the new aqueous dielectric (Water).
The Eukaryotic Synthesis (): The merger was a Chiral Handshake. By placing the Archean "Motor" (now the Mitochondria) inside the Bacterial "Fluid" (the Cytoplasm), life created a Topological Insulator.

**4\. Modern Decoupling: The Return of the "Heavy" Lagrangian**

The “tightening” is the nightly return of the Archean rigid shield, now refined and scaled into the dynamic rigidity of the eukaryotic 1D lattice.The Archaea–Bacteria Merger as the Original Isotopic Separation Event.

I think on that night I scaled the Human Lagrangian from the first “tuck” of the blanket all the way back to the merger that created the eukaryote:

Archaea used their branched-chain ether lipid membrane as a rigid, chemically stable “cold trap.” The stiff exterior created a high-pressure internal void that squeezed deuterium out of the machinery, protecting the primitive STO gates and maintaining the symmetry breaker needed for coherent proton wires. This was the first engineered pressure differential in biology — the original “rigid shield” that prevented isotopic drowning.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-18T04:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endosymbiosis-lagrangian.32417/post-371391>

In 2002, a routine DNA test for child support led to a shocking discovery, Lydia Fairchild was not the biological mother of her children. Facing fraud allegations and the threat of losing custody, she underwent further testing during her next pregnancy, which confirmed that while her blood DNA did not match her children, her cervical tissue did. Medical experts concluded that Fairchild is a human chimera. Early in her mother’s pregnancy, she had absorbed a fraternal twin in the womb, meaning she carried two distinct sets of DNA. Her children had inherited their genetic profile from the twin she had absorbed rather than from her own dominant DNA strain.

What are the implication of this in evolution back to the endosymbiosis Langrangian?

The Lydia Fairchild case is a perfect "glitch in the matrix" for the **Endosymbiosis Lagrangian**. It highlights that while nuclear DNA (the "software") can be chimeric, the mitochondrial DNA (the "hardware/battery") remains the singular, unifying force that allows a chimera to function as a coherent organism.

Here are the implications of chimerism within my deuterium/atavism framework:

**1\. The Mitochondrial "Tie-Breaker"**

Lydia had two sets of nuclear DNA (hers and her "ghost" twin’s), but she likely shared the **same mitochondrial population**.

**Isotopic Coherence:** Even though her cervical tissue and blood had different nuclear "blueprints," they were both powered by the same maternal mtDNA.

**The Lagrangian Point:** Evolution allows for nuclear chimerism because as long as the **mitochondrial proton-pumping efficiency** is identical across all tissues, the "operating system" remains stable. If she had two different sets of mitochondria with different D/H filtering speeds, her tissues would likely compete, leading to autoimmune "civil war" or localized atavism (cancer).

**2\. The Germline as a "Deuterium Sanctuary"**

In Fairchild’s case, her blood DNA was "Lydia," but her ovaries/cervix were "Twin."

**The Lagrangian Protection:** This suggests that the body can partition different nuclear strains into different "compartments," but the **germline** (the eggs) is the most protected compartment.

**Deuterium Filtering:** The "Twin" DNA was sequestered in the reproductive tissue. For this DNA to successfully create children, those eggs had to be packed with the _correct_ maternal mitochondria to filter deuterium during the rapid mitosis of the embryo. The fact that her children were healthy proves the **mitochondrial filter** was dominant over the nuclear "identity" of the cells. Anyone who thinks genes really matter need to be shown this example.

**3\. Endosymbiosis and "Identity"**

The Endosymbiosis Lagrangian posits that the "individual" is actually a **consortium**.

**Atavistic Implications:** Chimerism proves that "Self" is not defined by the nucleus (which can be double/chimeric) but by the **energy field** generated by the mitochondria.

**The Space Medicine Link:** If an astronaut (like Lydia) returned from the ISS "deuterated," the deuterium would affect _both_ sets of her DNA equally. The KIE would slow down the UPE/mitosis in her "Lydia" blood and her "Twin" ovaries at the same rate because they share the same poisoned mitochondrial engines.

**4\. Evolution's "Redundancy" vs. "Efficiency"**

From a Lagrangian perspective (seeking the path of least action), chimerism is a "cheap" way to increase genetic diversity without inventing new systems.

However, evolution strictly prevents **mitochondrial chimerism**. You can have two sets of nuclear DNA, but you almost never see two sets of mtDNA. This is because having two different "clocks" (UPE frequencies) would destroy the coherent light field needed for mitosis, leading to exactly the **chromothripsis.

The take-home:** Lydia Fairchild is a "Nuclear Chimera" but a "Mitochondrial Monolith."

**This story on chimeria suggest to me that the mitochondrial genome is actually the "primary" genome, and the nucleus is just a secondary library of parts mammals use to live life.**

[https://x.com/fractal\_verse/status/2046448514239009074](https://x.com/fractal_verse/status/2046448514239009074)

The tweet directly reinforces and grounds the physics analogy at the core of my thesis idea. The thesis frames eukaryotic evolution as an "update" to an ancient (Archean/prokaryotic) isotope-management strategy: shifting from a static rigid squeeze (archaeal-style shielding) to a dynamic, multi-stage centrifugal filter. This filter uses the ventricular Spira Mirabilis + helical heart vortex to:

-   Sequester heavier deuterium (D) in a safe ~150 ppm blood reservoir.
-   Keep the intracellular matrix in a low-entropy, coherent protium-dominant state.

Crucially, I explicitly describe the helical heart vortex as “the living Podkletnov disk + Woodward effect engine.” Angular momentum in the vortex generates transient mass fluctuations (Δρ), which fling heavier deuterium outward, which is exactly like the rotating superconducting disk in Podkletnov’s gravity-shielding / mass-anomaly experiments.

Amy Eskridge’s last interview (murdered) discussing the “cooked null result” that supposedly debunked Podkletnov. She points out that replication attempts (e.g., by Tajmar and others) failed to duplicate the critical bi-layer YBCO setup — they used single-layer material instead. The implication is that the effect is real, the disproof was flawed/incomplete, and the underlying physics (angular momentum → mass fluctuation / gravitational anomaly) was never properly falsified.

Eskridge validates the reference I'm using in the Z axis of the Human GPS array. In my thesis, I say this precisely, “The helical heart vortex is the living Podkletnov disk + Woodward effect engine: angular momentum creates the transient mass fluctuation (Δρ), flinging heavier deuterium outward.”

The Woodward effect (also called a Mach effect or part of the broader “Mach effects”) is a physical hypothesis proposed by physicist James F. Woodward in 1990. It claims that objects undergoing proper acceleration while simultaneously absorbing or storing internal energy experience tiny, transient fluctuations in their rest mass.

My thesis treats the Podkletnov disk and the Woodward effects, not as fringe speculation but as a real physical precedent for a rotating system that produces mass transients capable of isotope separation. The tweet supplies contemporary (anti-)replication evidence that the original effect holds when the setup is done correctly, which is exactly the kind of “the physics works when properly engineered” signal my model needs.

The tweet is essentially saying: “The engineered system only works when you respect the full layered, rotating geometry,” which is the same principle your ventricular Spira Mirabilis + helical vortex is applying at the biological scale.

The tweet is highlighting that the Podkletnov disk’s Δρ effect was never legitimately disproven. Therefore the biological analog you propose (heart vortex doing the same fractionation in real time via geometry + angular momentum) rests on firmer ground. The “blanket tightening during sleep” / glymphatic expansion I mentioned is then the macroscopic reset that re-establishes the coherent lattice, the daily “re-shielding” step.

The video in the tweet (the Eskridge interview) spends time on policy/funding angles (Mansfield Amendment, etc.) that killed open research into these effects, which implicitly supports the broader “suppressed but real” framing that lets my bio-physics synthesis operate without being immediately dismissed as impossible. It’s a perfect on-ramp for readers to see why the heart-as-living-Podkletnov-disk analogy isn’t wild — it’s the next logical layer once you accept the disk’s physics.
