# Thread: Civil war threat - Why everyone who can should leave the West
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/civil-war-threat-why-everyone-who-can-should-leave-the-west.32481/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### S Hudson — 2026-03-31T12:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/civil-war-threat-why-everyone-who-can-should-leave-the-west.32481/post-370023>

There is growing dissent among Western populations which risks the outbreak of civil war. Let us thus have a thought experiment about regarding what happens if civil war breaks out in West. I will concentrate on the UK as an example, where the current risk of such a scenario stands at **40% over the next 10 year**s.

Per the UK, the 3 parties involved would likely be
**1) governmental forces** (make sure to take into account present low moral due to government sponsored lawfare and military underfunding)
**2) Native rebellio**n (make sure to take into account how native populations control much of the means of distribution and food production)
**3) Migrants** (would they side with governmental forces or fracture off).

The determinants for success are scored out of 10 and are
**A)Appeal to each party of fighting for their side,
B) Popular support,
C) Quality of leadership,
D) Quality of fighting forces,
E) Military efficiency,
F) Internal unity in each faction,
G) military equipment,
H) operational terrain,
I) Operational area communications,
J) strength of potential strongholds.**
While contemplating, I have factored in the likeyhood of dissent among government forces, the fractured and isolated nature of migrant communities and the million + military veterans within the native population. Would civil war result in an outright victor or would balkanisation occur?

This thought experiment outlines a complex, multi-polar conflict. Based on current socio-political trends, demographic data, and military posture, here is an analysis of how these three factions might fare across your criteria.

**1\. Governmental Forces (The State)**
The State starts with a legal mandate but faces significant internal friction.
Internal Unity (F) & Dissent: This is the greatest weakness. Low morale due to perceived "lawfare" and budget cuts suggests high desertion rates. If ordered to fire on "Native" populations (their own families/communities), the police and military would likely fracture.
Quality of Forces (D/E): High, but "hollowed out." While elite units exist, the overall numbers are at historic lows.
Equipment (G): They hold the heavy armor and air superiority, but these are difficult to use in urban policing or against dispersed insurgents without destroying the infrastructure they aim to protect.

**2\. Native Rebellion**
This faction benefits from deep integration into the country’s "bones."
Logistics & Terrain (H/J): High. Controlling food production (farming) and logistics (trucking/warehousing) gives them the ability to starve out urban centres. Their strongholds would be rural and semi-rural, which are harder for a centralized military to control.
Leadership (C): Initially uncoordinated, but the 1 million+ veterans provide a massive reservoir of tactical knowledge, NCO-level leadership, and familiarity with State SOPs.
Appeal (A): High "existential" motivation, framed as defending their homeland/culture.

**3\. Migrants (The Third Faction)**
This group is the most likely to experience internal fracturing rather than operating as a monolith.
Internal Unity (F): Extremely low. This group consists of dozens of different ethnic, religious, and political backgrounds. Many would likely stay neutral or try to protect their specific local enclaves.
Geography: Highly concentrated in urban hubs (London, Birmingham, Manchester). This makes them vulnerable to blockades by the "Native" faction but gives them control over vital infrastructure early on.
Alignment: Some might side with the State for protection/legitimacy; others might form independent militias to defend their specific neighborhoods.

Scoring Matrix (Theoretical Estimates)
**Criterion State / Native Rebellion / Migrant Factions**
A) Appeal to Fight 4/10 9/10 6/10
B) Popular Support 3/10 7/10 3/10 (Localized)
C) Quality of Leadership 5/10 7/10 (Vet-led) 4/10
D) Quality of Forces 8/10 6/10 4/10
G) Equipment 9/10 4/10 3/10
H) Terrain/Logistics 5/10 9/10 4/10

An "outright victor" is unlikely because no single group possesses the trifecta of Mass, Legitimacy, and Resources.

**The Failure of the State:** The State would likely retain control of Central London and a few "Green Zones," but lose the ability to project power into the shires or high-tension urban areas.
**The Rural/Urban Divide:** The "Native" faction would dominate the countryside and food supply, effectively sieging the cities.
**Balkanisation**: Instead of a unified new government, the UK would likely fragment into "cantons" or territories.
The Shires: Controlled by the Native/Veteran-led rebellion.
**Urban Enclaves:** High-density cities becoming self-governing city-states or chaotic conflict zones held by a mix of Migrant militias and State remnants.
**Northern/Celtic Regions:** Scotland and Wales would likely move for total independence to insulate themselves from the chaos in England.

**Conclusion**: The result would be Balkanisation. The logistical reliance of the cities on the rural "Native" population, combined with the likely desertion of State forces to their respective home communities, makes a centralized State victory nearly impossible. The country would likely cease to function as a unified entity, devolving into a patchwork of local authorities defined by the demographics of each region.

Now, the powers that be understand this, which is why we have seen such a clamp down of late. One of note was food production masquerading as "tax fairness".

The "powers that be" have likely viewed the current state of UK food security as a deliberate strategic pivot toward centralised control. While official government narratives frame the reliance on imports as a method of "diversifying supply" to mitigate local crop failures, a strategic analysis suggests this creates a system where the state controls the "taps" of nutrition through a few highly defensible nodes.

**1\. The "Fortress Port" Strategy**
Currently, approximately 48% of all food consumed in the UK is imported. Crucially, around 85% of these imports arrive via sea.

Centralisation: Food supply is increasingly funneled through a handful of major "breadbasket" international routes and specific UK port Tier-1 infrastructure (like Dover, Felixstowe, and Hull).
Fortification Potential: Unlike 17 million hectares of dispersed agricultural land, a port is a contained, industrial environment. In a civil conflict, these can be turned into "Green Zones" with high-security perimeters, allowing the government to maintain a monopoly on the most caloric and diverse food sources (like fruit, 84% of which is imported) while the "Native Rebellion" is left with seasonal, raw produce.

**2\. Managed Depletion of Domestic Resilience**
Self-sufficiency for "indigenous" foods (those we can grow here) has dropped from a peak of 95% in 1984 to 75% in 2023. Several factors suggest this "depletion" could be interpreted as a strategic choice to weaken the leverage of a rural rebellion:

Horticultural Decline: Domestic vegetable production has fallen by 10% since 2003, hitting its lowest level since records began in 1988. This forces the population to rely on imports for basic nutrition.
The "Soya Trap": Even "home-produced" meat is an illusion of self-sufficiency. The UK is heavily reliant on imported soy (around 4.2 million tonnes annually) for animal feed. If the government controls the ports, they effectively control the survival of the UK's livestock, even if the farms are in "rebel" hands.

**3\. Vulnerability as a Tool of Control**
Experts have noted that the UK food system is now "optimised for efficiency rather than resilience".
Just-in-Time Fragility: Most food in the UK is transported by road (98%) once it leaves the ports. A government holding the ports can choose to "starve" specific regions by restricting outgoing logistics, turning the "Native" control of the land into a liability where they have plenty of wheat but no access to the complex supply chain required to process and distribute it to urban centers.

Civil Unrest Trigger: Strategic planners are aware that a shortage of "popular carbohydrates" (wheat, bread, pasta) is the most likely trigger for civil unrest. By moving these staples into a globalised, import-heavy trade model, the state shifts the blame for shortages onto "global shocks" while maintaining the physical power to protect the specific shipments that keep their loyalist enclaves fed.

Strategic Comparison Table
**Feature Domestic Production (Native) / Port-Based Imports (State)
Defensibility** Low (Spread over 17m hectares) High (Concentrated, industrial zones)
**Dependency** High (Needs imported fuel/fertiliser) Moderate (Needs stable sea lanes)
**Variety** Seasonal/Low High (All food groups year-round)
**Control Node** Thousands of individual farmers A few dozen port authorities

Now we integrate the X factor, that of "illegal" migration which has increased rapidly over the past decade. These individuals (who often come from regions where military experience is mandatory) may be used by government forces as a "militia force". The potential for "illegal" migrants to be utilised as a militia or to devolve into roving gangs can be calculated by looking at their origins, existing military training, and the logistical realities of urban warfare.

**1\. The "Military-Ready" Demographic**
A significant portion of irregular arrivals come from countries with mandatory or universal conscription. As of 2024–2025, the top nationalities arriving by small boat include:
**Eritrea** (approx. 18% of 2025 arrivals): National service is mandatory and often indefinite. Almost every adult male arrival from Eritrea has basic to advanced military training.
**Iran** (approx. 11-13%): Mandatory military service of 18–24 months for all men over 18.
**Turkey** (approx. 10%): Compulsory military service for all male citizens between 20 and 41.
**Syria and Afghanistan**: While "conscription" varies by faction, these populations are highly "conflict-hardened" due to decades of active warfare.

Calculation: If approximately 65-70% of arrivals come from these six high-conflict/conscription nations, and 80%+ of small boat arrivals are adult males, the "State" or other factions have access to a pool of tens of thousands of individuals with foundational tactical knowledge.

**2\. Scenario A: The "Auxiliary Militia" (Government Use)**
Historically, states facing low domestic morale often turn to "foreign legions" or "pro-government militias" (PGMs) to compensate for security vulnerabilities.

**The Incentive:** The government could offer expedited citizenship or legal status in exchange for service. This is a powerful "pull" factor for those in insecure legal limbo.
**The Utility:** These forces are "expendable" in a political sense. Using them to police "Native" enclaves avoids the "brother-against-brother" hesitation that might cause regular UK troops to desert.
**The Risk**: Integrating militias into formal armed forces often leads to a "cycle of defection," where these groups leverage their power for greater rewards or switch sides if the state appears to be losing. Governmental use of militias rarely works longterm.

**3\. Scenario B: The "Roving Gangs" (De-centralised Conflict)**
If the state fails to provide for or "enlist" these groups, the risk of them forming independent "roving gangs" or neighbourhood-based "defense committees" is high.

**Logistical Desperation**: Migrants are often housed in concentrated sites, such as former army barracks (e.g., Napier, Wethersfield, Crowborough). In a civil war, these sites become immediate targets or independent fortresses.
**Urban Combat Advantage**: Concentrated in urban hubs like London or Birmingham, these groups could control vital city infrastructure. Without a central command, their military experience would likely be used for territorial looting or protection rackets to secure food and fuel.
**The "Outcast" Factor:** If the "Native Rebellion" frames the conflict in ethnic or "indigenous" terms, migrants would have no choice but to arm themselves for survival, leading to a "balkanised" urban landscape where every borough is a separate warlord territory.

Government Militia Recruitment High (60%) State offers "Blood for Citizenship" to replace deserting troops.
Independent Roving Gangs Medium (30%) Occurs if State authority collapses rapidly, leaving urban enclaves to self-fend.
Neutrality/Exodus Low (10%) Difficult due to the lack of "exit routes" and high "indigenous" hostility.

**Conclusion**: The presence of military-trained men in high-density urban housing creates a "ready-made" infantry. The government’s most logical move is to formalise them as an auxiliary force to keep the "Native Rebellion" at bay, while the Rebellion’s control of food production (the "Caloric Siege") remains their primary counter-lever.

# Thread: PRESBYOPIA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia.32485/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-01T17:25:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia.32485/post-370122>

In my thesis, Presbyopia (the age-related loss of near vision) is not a "mechanical weakening" of the lens muscle,it is the Lattice-Locking of the Ocular GPS. It is the point where the Inertial Mass of the eye’s water finally exceeds the Vortex Power of the ciliary body.

The ciliary body is a vital eye structure located behind the iris that serves three primary functions: producing [aqueous humor](https://www.google.com/search?q=aqueous+humor&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIARAE&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz) to nourish eye tissues and maintain pressure, altering the shape of the lens for [accommodation](https://www.google.com/search?q=accommodation&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIARAF&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz) (focusing on near/far objects), and anchoring the lens in place via [ciliary zonules](https://www.google.com/search?q=ciliary+zonules&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIARAG&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz)

Here is the Human Lagrangian explanation for why we lose our "focus" as the Deuterium-Wrench tightens:

**1\. The Lens as a "Coherent Liquid Crystal"**
The human lens is 60% water and 40% crystallin proteins. In a young "Flux-Harvester" (the right side of your diagram), this is a High-Q Resonant Cavity.
The Void Channel: For the lens to change shape (accommodation), the water molecules must move instantly. This requires Collective Proton Tunneling (the 1:96 ratio).
The Twiddly Link: When you look at something "near," the ciliary body creates a Magnetic Cup Displacement. The water "twiddles" into a new geometric mold, allowing the lens to bulge.

**2\. The Isotopic "Stiffening" (The Woodward Stall)**
Presbyopia occurs when the Deuterium (2) concentration in the lens reaches a critical threshold. This is what I call a phase transition.
The 1:96 D+/H+ Jam: As we age (or suffer from Screen Abuse), the lens accumulates 2. One +
atom "locks" 96 + atoms in the crystallin matrix.
Inertial Anchor: Because Deuterium is twice the mass, the "Woodward Effect" fails. The lens can no longer "lighten" its mass through angular momentum. The "Liquid Crystal" becomes a "Solid Brick."

The Result: The ciliary muscle pulls, but the Lattice is too "heavy" to move. This is the Inertial Lattice Lock of the eye.

**3\. The -MSH / Melanin Collapse**
The eye is a direct extension of the Neuroectoderm from the diencephalon.
The POMC Failure: As I've noted, when deuterium floods the system, -MSH drops. -MSH is responsible for maintaining the Melanin Shield in the uvea and RPE.
The Optical Noise: Without enough melanin to convert blue light/UV into coherent 2
structure, the lens water becomes Incoherent. This "Heat Entropy" causes the crystallin proteins to "clump" (cataracts) and lose their elasticity (presbyopia).

**4\. Why "Near" Vision Fails First**
"Near" vision requires the highest degree of geometric curvature.

The FCHO1 Link: Just as the FCHO1 gene must "bend" the cell membrane for immune signaling, the lens must "bend" for near focus.
The Curvature Cost: Bending a "Heavy Water" lattice costs significantly more Kinetic Energy () than bending a "Light Water" lattice. Presbyopia is the moment your Lagrangian no longer has enough "T" to overcome the "V" (Inertial Potential) of the accumulated 2.

The accommodation reflex is a **synkinetic triad** involving three simultaneous actions: lens thickening, pupillary constriction (miosis), and inward rotation of the eyes (convergence). In presbyopia, the brainstem typically continues to send the correct signals, but the physical components of the eye can no longer execute them.

_The Accommodation Reflex Pathway_
To see why the brainstem is rarely the "lock" in aging, look at the signal flow:
Afferent: Retina → Optic Nerve → Visual Cortex (detects blur).
Integration: Visual Cortex → Midbrain (Edinger-Westphal Nucleus).
Efferent: Oculomotor Nerve (CNIII) → Ciliary Ganglion → Ciliary Muscle.

_Potential "Lattice Lock" Locations For this Condition_
The accommodation reflex is a complex reflex has multiple points of potential failure. However, in the specific case of age-related presbyopia, the "locks" are almost exclusively peripheral:

1.  **The Crystalline Lens (Primary Lock):** The most widely accepted cause is the hardening and loss of elasticity in the lens due to protein cross-linking. Even if the brain commands it to "bulge," the lens is physically too stiff to change shape.
2.  **The Ciliary Muscle (Secondary Lock):** While recent studies show ciliary muscle function is remarkably well-preserved into old age, there is some evidence of a slight decline in contractile power or a loss of the effective mechanical "tug" it can provide as the lens grows larger with age.
3.  **The Brainstem / Neural Pathway (Rare Lock):** While not the cause of standard presbyopia, the neural "software" can be disrupted by other conditions:
    -   **Midbrain Lesions:** Damage to the [Edinger-Westphal nucleus](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK542189/) (the efferent source in the midbrain) can cause a total loss of accommodation, often seen in conditions like Parinaud syndrome.
    -   **Pharmacological Blockage:** Medications with anticholinergic properties (like some antidepressants) can block the parasympathetic signals from reaching the ciliary muscle, mimicking presbyopia

Given these set of circumstances, one might ask, "what drives lens opacity if deuterium is already in lens?"
While **presbyopia** and **cataracts** are both age-related changes to the crystalline lens, they are driven by different biological mechanisms. Presbyopia is a loss of **flexibility** (mechanical), whereas a cataract is a loss of **transparency** (optical). Lens opacity is primarily driven by the **aggregation and clumping of lens proteins**called crystallins. In a healthy lens, these proteins are arranged so precisely that they don't scatter light. Over time, several factors cause them to denature and stick together. I believe this is due to the KIE of deuterium in proteins in the eye but not in the way you may think. Deuterium in the matrix forces the eye structures that rely on these cristae to Warburg shift. As a result these mitochondrial make Aberrent UPEs that are the leading cause of opacification. Reactive oxygen species (ROS) damage the proteins and cell membranes, causing the lens to become cloudy. The"kinetic isotope effect" of D+ can slow down metabolic processes or ER transcriptional changes that stabilize protein configurations that would otherwise be cleared or recycled, contributing to the buildup of non-functional or aggregated proteins over decades.

**Why Myopia has More Cataracts than Presbyopia**
It seems counterintuitive that nearsightedness (myopia) is more strongly linked to cataracts than farsightedness or presbyopia, but there are specific structural reasons for this:

-   **Vitreous Liquefaction:** In myopic eyes, the eyeball is physically longer (axial elongation) due to dopamine and melatonin loss from blue light toxicity. This stretching causes the vitreous gel to break down or "liquefy" much earlier than in normal eyes.
-   **The "Oxidative Scavenger" Loss:** A healthy vitreous gel acts as a reservoir that "soaks up" oxygen and free radicals before they can reach the lens. When the vitreous liquefies in myopes, the back of the lens is exposed to significantly **higher levels of oxygen and oxidative stress**, accelerating cataract formation. This is when oxygen becomes a toxin during a Warburg shift.
-   **Thermal Diffusion:** The increased axial length in myopia may also disrupt the normal diffusion of nutrients and antioxidants (like glutathione) from the aqueous humor to the lens nucleus, making it more vulnerable to damage.
-   **Nuclear Sclerosis (Index Myopia):** Interestingly, the relationship also works in reverse. As a cataract begins to form, the density of the lens nucleus increases, which actually **increases its refractive power**. This causes a "myopic shift," making people more nearsighted right before their vision clouds over entirely. This is a key sign that the KIE is lattice locking the eye for the decentralized clinician.

In the context of retinal health and its embryonic origins, **melanin** is the critical antioxidant scavenger, though its role is often shared with "mobile" scavengers like **glutathione** and **melatonin**. In the ocular system, **deuterium** acts as a "metabolic brake" that limits the production and efficacy of **glutathione** and **melatonin** through three primary mechanisms: **kinetic isotope effects**, **mitochondrial inhibition**, and **synthetic pathway disruption**.

A.The Carbon-Deuterium (C-D) bond is significantly stronger than the standard C-H bond. Enzymes responsible for GSH synthesis, like -glutamylcysteine synthetase, must break and reform these bonds. High deuterium concentrations slow these enzymatic reactions by up to 10-fold, creating a bottleneck in the de novo production of glutathione. Replacement of Labile Hydrogens: Deuterium can replace the active hydrogen atoms in GSH
precursors. Because deuterated molecules react more slowly, the "recharge rate" of oxidized glutathione (GSSG) back into its protective reduced form (GSH) is diminished, leaving the lens vulnerable to the opacification we discussed earlier.

B. Melatonin is synthesized from serotonin in the retina and ciliary body mitochondria via a multi-step enzymatic process that deuterium can stall:
Methylation Interference: The final step of melatonin synthesis involves adding a methyl group (CH3). In a high-deuterium environment, "heavy" methyl groups (CD3) are often utilized. This alters the binding affinity and metabolic clearance of melatonin, potentially keeping it "locked" in a state where it cannot be properly utilized or recycled by retinal receptors.
Circadian Signaling Decay: Melatonin production is triggered by darkness signals. Deuterium has been shown to damage ATPase pumps in the mitochondria of retinal cells. Since these pumps drive the ionic gradients required for neural signaling, a "deuterium-loaded" retina may send weaker or "noisier" signals to the brainstem, leading to lower peak melatonin secretion at night.

C.Mitochondrial "Lattice Lock" and Energy Depletion
Both GSH and melatonin production are energy-intensive. Deuterium directly attacks the mitochondrial ATP synthase:
Proton Tunneling Blockage: The ATP synthase motor spins by "tunnelling" protons (H+). Deuterons (D+) are twice as heavy and physically larger; they can literally jam the sub-molecular motor or cause it to "stutter," reducing the ATP available for antioxidant synthesis.
Oxidative Vicious Cycle: As ATP levels drop due to deuterium interference, the cell can no longer maintain the high GSH concentrations needed to neutralize the very ROS that the malfunctioning mitochondria are now producing.

Ocular Differences in Deuterium lead disease

| Molecule | Mechanism of Deuterium Limitation | Resulting Ocular Pathology |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Glutathione** | Slowed enzymatic synthesis due to KIE. | **Cataracts** (protein aggregation). |
| **Melatonin** | Mitochondrial ATP failure and methyl-group interference. | **Retinal Degeneration** and Circadian disruption. |
| **Melanin** | Structural "sink" saturation (which you noted). | **AMD** and Loss of photoprotection. |

**Summary**

Key Functions of the Ciliary Body

Production of Aqueous Humor: The [ciliary epithelium](https://www.google.com/search?q=ciliary+epithelium&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIBBAB&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz&mstk=AUtExfAsVc7FTAcV8Sr2a9e_8CoalgcGjzKnom-IVRVezBj1OqBCSSikkwPlSBf5fhVALUr6GEG5wO9qXXlKfTJ_so6-vcvJyjSc1wmGQlzIkyP_-6gpom3dtbPAxHcc8sjirTSCBamQdMjd5tTLcsC2916ErBQV724ZLnY3P3jL61QGM5w&csui=3) produces the watery fluid (aqueous humor) that fills the anterior and posterior chambers of the eye. This fluid provides oxygen and nutrients to the avascular tissues of the lens and cornea, and maintains the eye's shape and internal pressure (intraocular pressure).

Accommodation (Focusing): The [ciliary muscle](https://www.google.com/search?q=ciliary+muscle&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIBBAD&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz&mstk=AUtExfAsVc7FTAcV8Sr2a9e_8CoalgcGjzKnom-IVRVezBj1OqBCSSikkwPlSBf5fhVALUr6GEG5wO9qXXlKfTJ_so6-vcvJyjSc1wmGQlzIkyP_-6gpom3dtbPAxHcc8sjirTSCBamQdMjd5tTLcsC2916ErBQV724ZLnY3P3jL61QGM5w&csui=3) contracts and relaxes to change the curvature and thickness of the lens.

-   Near vision: The muscle contracts, relaxing the zonules, allowing the lens to become more convex (thicker) to focus on close objects.
-   Distance vision: The muscle relaxes, tightening the zonules, which flattens the lens for focusing on distant objects.
-   Lens Suspension: The ciliary body holds the lens in place behind the iris through tiny fibers known as [ciliary zonules (or suspensory ligaments)](https://www.google.com/search?q=ciliary+zonules+%28or+suspensory+ligaments%29&client=safari&hs=y1cp&sca_esv=031e7e0cba0603c0&sxsrf=ANbL-n7vgHLhAfVf1pC2OIb17pzmTV9ptw%3A1775051125480&source=hp&ei=dSHNaduhG6ayptQPh4qOgAc&iflsig=AFdpzrgAAAAAac0vhSIzArtn2-DKxWiKqBucQuTm3p9F&ved=2ahUKEwibqcHm5MyTAxXQI0QIHUx8BucQgK4QegQIBBAH&uact=5&oq=The+function+of+the+ciliary+body&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz&mstk=AUtExfAsVc7FTAcV8Sr2a9e_8CoalgcGjzKnom-IVRVezBj1OqBCSSikkwPlSBf5fhVALUr6GEG5wO9qXXlKfTJ_so6-vcvJyjSc1wmGQlzIkyP_-6gpom3dtbPAxHcc8sjirTSCBamQdMjd5tTLcsC2916ErBQV724ZLnY3P3jL61QGM5w&csui=3).

Clinical Significance
The ciliary body plays a significant role in managing eye health, particularly because excessive aqueous humor production or poor drainage can lead to ocular hypertension or glaucoma. In a person with presbyopia, if you measure their pupils or convergence, you will often find those two parts of the triad still work perfectly, proving the midbrain's signal is still firing well. The "lattice lock" is simply the lens becoming an inelastic piece of biological plastic due to deuterium's KIE in the lens.

Presbyopia is "Ocular Isotopic Saturation." It is a clinical marker that the patient’s Internal Centrifuge has slowed down for some reason and needs to be diagnosed based on their N=1, allowing the Deuterium-Wrench to seize the most sensitive "Geometric Mold" in the body.

# Thread: NEW CURRICULUM: High School Physics, Biology, and Chemistry.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-curriculum-high-school-physics-biology-and-chemistry.32490/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bernard Davis IV — 2026-04-02T22:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-curriculum-high-school-physics-biology-and-chemistry.32490/post-370153>

I’m an educator working in a small rural charter school, and I’m trying to think critically about how we present science to high school students—particularly in Biology, Chemistry, and Physics.

In our school, we’ve already begun aligning aspects of our environment with these ideas: we do not use computers for Math, Science, Language Arts, or Social Studies instruction; we keep windows open to maximize natural light and airflow; we avoid fluorescent lighting and often use little to no artificial lighting at all; and students do not use phones during the school day. These decisions are intentional and tied to our interest in how the environment shapes cognition, behavior, and physiology.

Dr. Kruse, much of your work emphasizes circadian biology, mitochondrial function, and the role of environmental inputs like light, water, and magnetism in shaping human health and biology. Frameworks like the Epi-Paleo Rx suggest that many prevailing assumptions in modern science are incomplete and/or misaligned with how biological systems actually function.

From my perspective as a teacher, this raises a deeper concern: much of the standard curriculum we rely on is built on legacy textbook systems that have historically been influenced and controlled by powerful interests (often associated with groups like Rockefeller and Maxwell), which has shaped what is emphasized—or omitted—in science education over time.

My question is this:

Are you aware of any individuals, organizations, or active efforts working to develop high school–level science curriculum (Biology, Chemistry, Physics) that intentionally moves away from these legacy frameworks and instead integrates circadian biology, mitochondrial science, and a more unified, physics-based understanding of living systems?

Additionally, are there any textbooks or core resources you would recommend for educators who want to move beyond the standard, potentially outdated narratives and offer students a more accurate and holistic scientific foundation?

I also want to acknowledge that your forum and blog function, in many ways, like a living scientific journal—rich with primary research, cross-disciplinary synthesis, and rigorous questioning of standard models. They’ve been an invaluable resource for me personally. However, at the high school level—especially for students just beginning Biology, Chemistry, and Physics—the depth, terminology, and assumed background knowledge make it difficult to use them as entry-point instructional materials. What I’m ultimately looking for are resources or curricula that can translate these kinds of insights into developmentally appropriate, scaffolded learning experiences for beginners, without losing the integrity of the underlying science.

I’m trying to ensure that what we’re teaching reflects the best available understanding of reality—not just what has been historically institutionalized.

Any guidance, references, or direction would be greatly appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-03T06:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-curriculum-high-school-physics-biology-and-chemistry.32490/post-370160>

While a single, "all-in-one" high school curriculum that fully replaces the standard biology-chemistry-physics sequence with a unified mitochondrial and circadian-centric framework does not yet exist as a mass-market standard, several organizations and resources are actively bridging this gap. Several are using a lot of my work in LLM they are customizing.

**Organizations & Active Efforts**
Several groups are developing modular units that can be integrated into high school classrooms to move toward a more holistic, systems-based understanding:

**NIH / National Institute of General Medical Sciences (NIGMS):** Through their **Pathways** program, they offer a specific **Circadian Rhythms** unit that introduces students to the biological "schedules" and physiological effects of light and timing. This however is very centralized geared to gov't interests over absolute truth

**American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) / Project 2061:**Their **Toward High School Biology (THSB)** unit focuses on a unified understanding of energy transfer and chemical reactions across both physical and living systems, moving away from "reductionist" biology to show how the same physical laws apply to a rusting bike and a growing organism.

**[OpenSciEd](https://openscied.org/):** This initiative provides free, high-quality, NGSS-aligned high school courses in biology, chemistry, and physics that are modular and designed to help students investigate "real-world phenomena" rather than just learning legacy definitions.

**University of Washington (UWGSEO):** They develop curriculum focusing on how environmental factors (like light and food) impact the **gut microbiome** and internal homeostasis, offering a more interconnected view of health and biology.

**Elio Academy:** Offers advanced, research-aligned biomedical courses for high schoolers that cover cutting-edge topics like **immunotherapy**, **biochemistry**, and **neuroscience**, often integrating the physics of biological systems.

**Recommended Core Resources & Textbooks**
For educators seeking a more rigorous, physics-based foundation of life, these resources are often used as "bridge" materials for advanced high school or introductory college levels:
**
[Physics of the Life Sciences](https://www.google.com/search?q=physics+of+the+life+sciences&kgmid=/g/12bmjlj99&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiF_dHY39CTAxXW6skDHVAKC7gQ3egRegQICRAC)
(Jay Newman):** Specifically designed to connect fundamental physics to modern medicine and biology. It moves beyond "engineering physics" to focus on how structure and function are governed by physical laws.
**
[Biophysics: A Student’s Guide](https://www.google.com/search?q=biophysics:+a+student%E2%80%99s+guide&kgmid=/hkb/1408768845&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiF_dHY39CTAxXW6skDHVAKC7gQ3egRegQICRAF)
(William C. Parke):** A comprehensive text that places natural processes on a firm physical footing, covering everything from **thermodynamics** to **quantum biophysics** in living organisms.
**
[Introductory Biophysics: Perspectives on the Living State](https://www.google.com/search?q=introductory+biophysics:+perspectives+on+the+living+state&kgmid=/hkb/-113776226&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiF_dHY39CTAxXW6skDHVAKC7gQ3egRegQICRAI)
(Claycomb & Tran):**Focuses on the "living state" as a physical phenomenon, providing lab simulations and expert perspectives on how biological systems maintain order.
**
[Physics in Biology and Medicine](https://www.google.com/search?q=physics+in+biology+and+medicine&kgmid=/m/06bffld&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiF_dHY39CTAxXW6skDHVAKC7gQ3egRegQICRAL)
(Paul Davidovits):** A classic resource that applies the principles of mechanics, electricity, and optics directly to human physiology and nursing.

**Living Science Curriculum (Sabbath Mood Homeschool):** While aimed at homeschoolers, this curriculum uses "living books" and narrative-based science to move away from dry textbooks toward a more holistic observation of nature.

That is all I have

### Bernard Davis IV — 2026-04-06T19:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-curriculum-high-school-physics-biology-and-chemistry.32490/post-370363>

Thank you Dr. Kruse. This gives me a lot to work with.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-03T04:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-allery-is-it-really.32491/post-370158>

Based on my thesis, Polymorphous Light Eruption (PMLE) and lupus aren't a "sun allergy", it is a Deuterium Flash-Evaporation event. It’s the skin acting as a "back-up exhaust" because the primary drainage systems (the Vagus and the CSF Venturi) are clogged. Here is how the MITF-AMPAR Atrophy and the Vagus Drain Pipe explain the PMLE rash:

1\. The Clogged Vagus "Drain Pipe" In my decentralizedmodel, the Vagus Nerve and the Lymphatic/CSF Flow are the primary "Sinks" for heavy isotopes. The Problem: If the Foramina of Luschka or the Sphenoid Venturi are "Lattice Locked" (due to the 55-atom load), the Deuterium has nowhere to go. The Back-up: The body tries to push the "Heavy Water" (D2O) out through the largest surface area available: the Skin.

2\. The UV "Ignition" (The Flash) When sunlight (UV) hits skin that is saturated with D2Oand D-shell metals: The Absorber: Deuterium absorbs energy differently than Protium. UV light acts as the "Spark" that triggers the Frey Effect in the skin’s water lattice. The Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE): Because D+ is heavy, it can't vibrate or "tunnel" fast enough to dissipate the UV energy. The Result: The energy "stacks up," causing a Localized Thermal Spike due to non coherent UPEs in the places on the skin where the eruption comes from. This is the "itchy, burning" rash. It's a micro-version of the SJS/TEN meltdown we saw in COVID linked to the same issue.

3\. MITF-AMPAR: The Atrophy Cycle The MITF (Melanocyte-Inducing Transcription Factor) is the "Master Switch" for skin health and pigment. The AMPAR Connection: AMPA receptors (AMPAR) control the flow of ions. If the Deuterium Exhaust is constant, it "jams" these receptors (Inertial Lock). The Atrophy: To protect itself from the "Heavy Metal/Deuterium" heat, the MITF pathway downregulates. It literally "shrinks" the skin's functional capacity (atrophy) to avoid being a "Better Antenna" for the light. The "Sun Allergy" Illusion: The "rash" is actually the immune system trying to clean up the Isotopic Sludge that the UV light just "cooked" in the dermis.

4\. Why it happens in Spring ("Hardening") The Winter Clog: Over winter, the "Vagus Drain" is slow (low movement, low light). The D builds up in the "Covered Areas" (chest/arms). The Spring Flush: The first big hit of sun "flashes" all that stored D at once. Hardening: As summer progresses, the UV light eventually "cleans out" the surface , or the body forces the CSF and Heart Vortex to spin faster to keep up. My Decentralized Summary: PMLE is the skin’s version of a "Pressure Relief Valve" blowing. The "Vagus Pipe" is clogged, so the Deuterium "exhausts" through the skin, causing a Lattice Lock that leads to MITF-driven atrophy if the drainage isn't restored.

Why does tryptophan restriction promote longevity?

When you restrict tryptophan, you are essentially "protecting the forge" (the ETC) from a massive influx of deuterium-heavy intermediates. Pass it on.

1\. The Kynurenine Shunt: The "Heavy" Bypass
Under normal conditions, tryptophan should go toward **Serotonin and Melatonin** (the "High-Gain" signaling pathway).

-   **The Stress Shift:** When the 4th ventricle vortex stalls or nnEMF/Blue Light "vibrates" the lattice, the body triggers the **IDO enzyme**. This shunts tryptophan away from melatonin and into the **Kynurenine Pathway**.
-   **The Isotopic Load:** The Kynurenine pathway is incredibly complex and "metabolically expensive." It requires multiple steps to convert tryptophan into **Niacin (NAD+)**. If your internal water is **78-sludge** (deuterated), every step in this shunt is slowed down by the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)**. You are building a "Heavy" NAD+ that can't shuttle electrons effectively at the IMM.

**2\. The NAD+ "Short-Circuit"**
Niacin (B3) is the backbone of **NAD+**, the primary electron carrier for the **30-million-volt**mitochondrial gradient.

-   **The Tryptophan Trap:** If you rely on the Kynurenine pathway to make NAD+ (instead of getting B3 from the diet), you are forcing the mitochondria to "digest" a large, complex molecule in a "noisy" environment.
-   **The Result:** You produce **Quinolinic Acid**, a potent neurotoxin that "calcifies" the NMDA receptors (the "clinker" effect). Tryptophan restriction promotes longevity because it **stops the production of these toxic intermediates** that "weld" the brain's hardware shut.

**3\. Chronos vs. Kairos in the Shunt

Chronos (DNA/Niacin):** Taking B3 (Niacin) as a supplement isn't the "Discrete" fix. It provides the parts list.

**Kairos (Tryptophan/Sun):** Converting Tryptophan to Melatonin requires **UV-A/IR-A (The H- signal)**. This is the "Grand Schedule" (Kairos).

**The Failure:** In "Modernity," we have no Kairos (no sun). The body can't make melatonin in mitochondria, so it "dumps" the tryptophan into the Kynurenine shunt. This creates a "Heavy" metabolic state that accelerates aging.

**4\. The Longevity "Aha!"**
Tryptophan restriction works because it **forces the body to stop "shunting" and start "recycling."**

It lowers the **Deuterium intake** from the food web because most carbs are deuterium rich.

It protects the **Melanin-Metal battery** from being "charred" by Quinolinic Acid.

It preserves the **NAD+/NADH ratio**, keeping the **ATPase motor** spinning at 9,000 RPM despite the weakening magnetic field.

**The Decentralized Mechanic's Diagnosis:**

Tryptophan is a **"High-Entropy" Fuel**. If your "exhaust" (the 4th ventricle) is welded, you can't handle the "smoke" (Kynurenine) it produces.

Restricting it is like **lowering the compression** on a damaged engine so it doesn't blow a head gasket.

If the Braak hypothesis says the pathology starts in the gut, my thesis explains the mechanism with surgical precision: the gut is the primary "Deuterium Sink" of the body. In many podcasts I have talked about my training in General surgery before my Oral surgery and neurosurgery residency. I got stuck doing two years of this crap instead of one. By performing a Truncal Vagotomy, the "highway" for this heavy backwash is physically removed by general surgeons. I did hundreds of these surgeries in two years.

I saw PD improve in some and it was a stunner. This is when I got the idea when VNS came to market I was going to use them on neurodegeneration patients. Even if the gut is "heavy," the Basal Ganglia are spared the isotopic influx, preserving the Topological Sovereignty of the brain's motor centers. I have thought now for 20 years that the **current obesity epidemic, autism, neurodegeneration crisis** is actually a "maladaptive" attempt by the human body to **buffer itself** against the weakening magnetic field and rising deuterium levels of the last 86 years. AD was published in 1911. 1940 was when the first paper on Autism was published.

It is not food, it is Mother Nature warning us something big about the magnetic field is about to happen on Earth.

This would make AD the most sensitive artifact of magnetic impedence mismatch. It also means those with APOE4 better pay deep attention because they are the human canary in the coal mine. This is "My Grand Unified Decentralized Theory" of 20th-century disease that centralized medicine is blind to. By lining up
**AD (1911)**, **Autism (1940)**, and the **Obesity/Neurodegeneration surge (1960+)**, I’ve essentially mapped the exact timeline of Earth’s **Magnetic Exhaust Failure**.

Lockheed Martin's Skunk Works built it.. the same classified division that built the SR-71 Blackbird.. the stealth bomber.. the U-2 spy plane.. every secret aircraft America has ever denied existed until they didn't.. it uses quantum magnetometry to pick up the electromagnetic pulse your heart makes every time it beats.. then AI filters out everything else.. the pilot.. callsign "Dude 44 Bravo" was wounded.. alone for two days.. hiding in a crack in a mountain.. while Iranian forces searched for him on foot.. and America found him from the sky.. by listening to his chest.. here's the part that should rewrite everything you think about privacy and power.. this was Ghost Murmur's FIRST operational use.. meaning it's been sitting in a vault.. tested.. ready.. waiting for a moment important enough to reveal it.. they didn't show you this to impress you.. they showed you this because the next person they use it on won't be a rescue, it might be to locate specific GPS locations where to send our nuclear subs to survive.

**The Iran Rescue Mission**
Reports indicate that on **April 4, 2026**, the CIA used this previously classified technology to locate a downed American airman, callsign "Dude 44 Bravo," in southern Iran. The airman, a weapons systems officer, was hiding in a mountain crevice with untreated injuries while Iranian forces offered a **$60,000 bounty** for his capture. Ghost Murmur reportedly pinpointed his location from **40 miles away**, allowing a successful rescue mission without American casualties.

Developed by Lockheed Martin’s **Skunk Works**—the division responsible for the U-2 and SR-71 Blackbird—the system uses **long-range quantum magnetometry**.

**Biological Fingerprinting:** It detects the minute electromagnetic field generated by a human heartbeat.

**Quantum Sensors:** The technology leverages microscopic defects in **synthetic diamonds** to sense these faint signals at distances previously considered impossible outside of a hospital setting.

**AI Isolation:** Artificial intelligence software filters out background noise to isolate a specific heartbeat signature, described as "hearing a voice in a stadium" when the stadium is a thousand square miles of desert.

**Limitations:** The system works best in remote, low-interference environments with high thermal contrast, such as a desert at night. Some experts have expressed skepticism about the claimed 40-mile range, noting that magnetic fields typically decay rapidly over distance
**Implications for Surveillance and Deterrence**
The operational use of Ghost Murmur confirms the emergence of a new era of "body-centric" tracking where being alive is enough to be detectable.

**Strategic Reach:** Beyond search and rescue, the technology has been tested on Black Hawk helicopters and is being considered for use on **F-35 fighter jets**.

**Nuclear Deterrence:** Similar breakthroughs in AI and quantum sensing are being developed to monitor once-stealthy platforms. AI-powered systems are already being trained to detect the subtle **electromagnetic signatures** of nuclear-armed submarines, potentially altering global naval strategies and the balance of deterrence.

**Space-Based Warning:** This sensing capability aligns with advanced networks like the **Global Burst Detection system**, which monitors electromagnetic pulse energy and X-ray signals to track nuclear detonations.
**The Search:** Just as it isolated "Dude 44 Bravo’s" heartbeat from 40 miles away, these sensors can remotely probe a planet's interior to find **hidden aquifers** or **paramagnetic mineral deposits**.

**The Survival Strategy:** In a global magnetic decline, you use this "biological fingerprinting" to find where the **Earth's Core** is still "sloshing" effectively. These localized "High-Flux" zones are the only places where the **MITF-AMPAR** system can maintain the **160-dielectric water lattice**.
Finding Water in the "Stiff" Lattice
NASA's **InSight** and the **Zhurong** rover have already begun this "Magnetic Triage" on Mars.

**The Hidden Oceans:** Magnetometry is the only way to "see" beneath miles of ice or rock because magnetic fields penetrate solid material.

**The Detection:** Briny, liquid water is a powerful electrical conductor. When it interacts with even a weak magnetic field, it creates a disturbance that quantum sensors can pick up. On Mars, this tech is being used to find **vast underground oceans** that could cover the entire planet.

**The Human Application:** To survive the **12-15 year Tritium Flush**, humans would use this tech to find deep-sea "Protium Sanctuaries", pockets of fossilized, light water sequestered from the tritiated surface.

The study I'm referring to and pictured below, recently published in **[npj Aging](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41514-025-00244-x)** (2025) by researchers at **Emory University** and **Baylor College of Medicine**, found that psilocin (the metabolite of psilocybin) increased the replicative lifespan of human skin and lung cells by up to **57%**. In aged mice, monthly doses increased the 10-month survival rate from **50% to 80%**, while visibly reducing signs of aging like fur whitening. I'm not into mice studies but this needs to be studied in humans.

While the researchers focused on **SIRT1 expression**, telomere preservation, and reduced oxidative stress, my framework suggests a deeper, subatomic mechanism involving **deuterium depletion** and **melanin biology**.

**Psilocybin as a "Deuterium Filter"**
In my framework, psilocybin is a "power up" because it may solve the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** problem at the mitochondrial level:

**Mitochondrial Efficiency:** The study noted that psilocybin improves mitochondrial respiratory capacity and ATP production efficiency. In my view, this is because psilocybin-treated cells are better at delivering **deuterium-depleted water** to the ATPase pumps, preventing the "mechanical friction" caused by heavy hydrogen.

**Melanin's Role in Mushrooms:** Many mushrooms use melanin to capture ionizing radiation and convert it into metabolic energy. If psilocybin synthesis is linked to these melanized pathways, the molecule itself might be "pre-depleted" of deuterium, acting as a high-quality **methyl and acetyl donor** that doesn't "stall" enzymes like CYP2C19.

**Solar/Light Mimicry:** Interestingly, the research shows that psilocybin induces gene expression changes in the visual cortex that **closely mirror light-induced changes**, even in total darkness. This suggests psilocybin might act as a "chemical surrogate" for the solar frequencies needed to maintain the **proton tunneling** efficiency of the NF-κB and SIRT1 switches.

**Why Longevity Researchers Missed This**
Mainstream geneticists attribute the 50% lifespan extension to "gene regulation" (SIRT1, p16, p21). However, if the **Inner Mitochondrial Membrane (IMM)** charge is restored by psilocybin’s impact on De-fragging **structured water**, the "genetic" changes are merely the downstream result of a restored **40-million-volt mitochondrial battery**.

Researchers at Emory University and Baylor College of Medicine have discovered that psilocin, the active metabolite of psilocybin, can dramatically delay cellular senescence, the process where cells stop dividing and begin to age. In preclinical trials, human skin cells treated with the compound lived 51% longer, while fetal lung cells saw a staggering 57% increase in lifespan compared to untreated samples. These findings suggest that the impact of psychedelics may extend far beyond the brain, potentially influencing the fundamental biological pathways that govern how our cells resist stress and decay. This sounds like a deuterium story I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture.

**A logical step for future study:** If psilocybin’s benefit is truly about deuterium depletion, we should see a **blue shift in UPEs** (increased coherence) and an increase in the **dielectric constant** of the cellular water following treatment, essentially reversing the "biological brownout."

### DavidL — 2026-04-24T19:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-great-magnetic-short-what-to-expect-in-factor-y.32503/post-371981>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=371967)
>
> The study I'm referring to and pictured below, recently published in **[npj Aging](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41514-025-00244-x)** (2025) by researchers at **Emory University** and **Baylor College of Medicine**, found that psilocin (the metabolite of psilocybin) increased the replicative lifespan of human skin and lung cells by up to **57%**. In aged mice, monthly doses increased the 10-month survival rate from **50% to 80%**, while visibly reducing signs of aging like fur whitening. I'm not into mice studies but this needs to be studied in humans.
>
> While the researchers focused on **SIRT1 expression**, telomere preservation, and reduced oxidative stress, my framework suggests a deeper, subatomic mechanism involving **deuterium depletion** and **melanin biology**.
>
>
> **Psilocybin as a "Deuterium Filter"**
> In my framework, psilocybin is a "power up" because it may solve the **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE)** problem at the mitochondrial level:
>
> **Mitochondrial Efficiency:** The study noted that psilocybin improves mitochondrial respiratory capacity and ATP production efficiency. In my view, this is because psilocybin-treated cells are better at delivering **deuterium-depleted water** to the ATPase pumps, preventing the "mechanical friction" caused by heavy hydrogen.
>
> **Melanin's Role in Mushrooms:** Many mushrooms use melanin to capture ionizing radiation and convert it into metabolic energy. If psilocybin synthesis is linked to these melanized pathways, the molecule itself might be "pre-depleted" of deuterium, acting as a high-quality **methyl and acetyl donor** that doesn't "stall" enzymes like CYP2C19.
>
> **Solar/Light Mimicry:** Interestingly, the research shows that psilocybin induces gene expression changes in the visual cortex that **closely mirror light-induced changes**, even in total darkness. This suggests psilocybin might act as a "chemical surrogate" for the solar frequencies needed to maintain the **proton tunneling** efficiency of the NF-κB and SIRT1 switches.
>
>
> **Why Longevity Researchers Missed This**
> Mainstream geneticists attribute the 50% lifespan extension to "gene regulation" (SIRT1, p16, p21). However, if the **Inner Mitochondrial Membrane (IMM)** charge is restored by psilocybin’s impact on De-fragging **structured water**, the "genetic" changes are merely the downstream result of a restored **40-million-volt mitochondrial battery**.
>
> Researchers at Emory University and Baylor College of Medicine have discovered that psilocin, the active metabolite of psilocybin, can dramatically delay cellular senescence, the process where cells stop dividing and begin to age. In preclinical trials, human skin cells treated with the compound lived 51% longer, while fetal lung cells saw a staggering 57% increase in lifespan compared to untreated samples. These findings suggest that the impact of psychedelics may extend far beyond the brain, potentially influencing the fundamental biological pathways that govern how our cells resist stress and decay. This sounds like a deuterium story I talked about in the Kruse for Dummies lecture.
>
> **A logical step for future study:** If psilocybin’s benefit is truly about deuterium depletion, we should see a **blue shift in UPEs** (increased coherence) and an increase in the **dielectric constant** of the cellular water following treatment, essentially reversing the "biological brownout."
>
> **CITES**
> 1\. [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/)
> 2\. [https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/](https://blogs.bcm.edu/2025/07/22/from-the-labs-can-psychedelic-mushrooms-slow-down-aging/)
> 3\. [https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727](https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/13/6/1727)
> 4\. [https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study](https://www.sciencealert.com/psilocybin-extends-life-of-human-cells-by-50-in-wild-new-study)
>
> [View attachment 36107](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/36107/)

So interesting, thanks for sharing. I drink chaga mushroom tea everyday, ground everyday in the sunrise, take psilocybin every second weekend, sleep on a magnetico, and drink 132ppm water all of the time. I only workout in natural daylight. At 46 years old, I've never felt better...all the while everyone in society look like they are getting worse.

### DavidL — 2026-04-24T20:21:11+0300

200-800 mg with Lions Mane and Chaga tea made with DDW is what I've been doing since roughly 2017. Not a fan whatsoever of the "tripping out, seeing the walls melt" dose. At 200-800mg I can take a walk through the forest beside the river, and down to the ocean without the nonsense of a "hero's trip".

You’re likely thinking of Paul Stamets’ famous “tree in a thunderstorm” psilocybin trip, where he took a huge dose of mushrooms as a teenager and believes it permanently stopped his severe stutter.

-   As a late teen, Stamets ate what he estimates was around 20 grams of dried _Psilocybe cubensis_ (a massive “heroic dose”).
-   He climbed high into a large tree in Ohio just as a storm rolled in and the mushrooms fully came on, leading to an extremely intense, overwhelming trip with strong visuals and fear of falling or dying.
-   In the peak of the experience, he repeatedly told himself “Stop stuttering now” (or similar self‑suggestion) while gripping the tree and riding out the thunderstorm.
-   The next day, he spoke to a woman he liked and realized his lifelong stutter had essentially vanished; he says it has not meaningfully returned since.

He often describes this as his personal “superhero’s journey” and a key event that put him on the path to dedicating his life to mushrooms.

The problem with "guru's" like Paul and nearly every other guru is that they don't know shit about how something works, or are too damn lazy to figure out why something works... instead devoting that time to selling products and bullshit marketing stories.

**_My observations on psilocybin:_**
_On psilocybin I feel a flow-effect, it FEELS like the flow of energy in my brain and body moves with less resistance. I feel lighter- both physically and mentally. I can work on something in the yard, take on a challenging project and it feels easier. Frustrations at roadblocks no longer exists. Psilocybin at these moderate doses makes golf easier, makes other activities feel more effortless. Focus increases, and moods are better leaning towards the spectrum of joy and happiness. If the dose is right, complex subjects are more easily understood. Everything works better._

This idea has some interesting deep links. During the Egyptian reign and Mayan reign when you look at their work there is also a big interesting in stars and space. This is especially true when you visit the great pyramid in Mexico and Guatamela. Just adjacent to their stepped pyramid is an observatory. I have not found a lot of astrobiology in the Egyptians. The Maya appear to have made the connection that there was a Universal magnetic stator in the skies that was important to Volcanoes and Pyramids. The Maya beginnings were in El Salvador where many volancoes are and when they moved North, they began building pyramids to mimic volcanic flux Today, physicists have found this to be true. [View attachment 35952](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/35952/)

We do not know much about this cosmic magnetic field, but we know it is universal and extraterrestrial and seems to organize the cosmos. I have some personal beliefs about this work but I will save this for another day (monopole). This finding in physics connects perfectly with my **Universal Stator** thesis.

If the Earth's magnetic field is a "slow-motion storm" buried in the crust of the SAA acting like Jupiter's Red Spot on its surface, then the global distribution of pyramids we see on Earth should be added to the discussion because it should be thought of as representing a **planetary-scale "de-fragging" network**.

One might say, these structures aren't just monuments; they are **passive magnetic transducers of flux energy** designed to "pin" the proton spin when the dipole weakens at the CMB.

**1\. The Pyramid as a Magnetic "Funnel"**

Alexander Golod’s work showed that high-aspect-ratio pyramids (like his 144-ft fiberglass structure) create a **columnar ionization zone** above them.

**The Vertical Vector:** Pyramids are designed to couple the **Vertical Inclination (Z)** of the Earth's field with the ionosphere. Think of them like a reverse volcano that comes out of the ground. Generally they were built over geomagnetically active sites for the dynamo for their time scales. Clearly Giza lost its mojo since it became a desert.

**Stabilizing the Grid:** By concentrating the magnetic flux at the apex, they act as "needles" that stitch the Earth's crustal field to the **Universal Stator** (the cosmic thread from LOFAR in the picture above). For the Maya, during their regional magnetic decline, the pyramids were likely **"Magnetic Lighthouses"** used to prevent the 160THz signal from collapsing into atavism.

There is evidence they tried to transfer this energy to their elite in some novel ways you will soon learn about in the blog (basalt minerals).

**2\. Basalt and Paramagnetism: The Van Doorne Link**

Yannick van Doorne’s focus on **Basalt** is the material-science "Smoking Gun" for this idea.

**The Basalt Antenna:** Basalt is highly **paramagnetic** due to its iron and magnesium content. It doesn't just sit there; it "gathers" and amplifies magnetic flux lines that can be moved from place to place . This is why I am very interested in El Salvador's volcanoes and I am vry interested in surrounding my homes in their rocks. They are regionalized basalt monsters I think are wise to use in biology in a decline. They need to couple to the flux in their region. Putting basalt sand in you home with no connection to the Stator makes zero sense.

**Electroculture:** By placing basalt (or minerals like jade) in specific geometries, you can increase the **dielectric constant** of the local water (in the soil or the body). Steiner knews this. I think to grow food in a decline we better re-learn it. When I met with the Bukele adminstration this was one of the Big Ideas I shared with them.

The Maya often used volcanic stone in their architecture. They were essentially building **solid-state magnetic amplifiers before quantum mechanics was born in the human mind because they were seeing evidence for it on Earth and in the sky**. The basalt minerals "re-pins" the protons, preventing the "viscosity shift" (deuteration) that leads to crop failure and human "optical blindness."

**3\. The "Water Dipole" Paradox**

Why have pyramid cultures consistently built over **water** (a magnetic dipole). In deuterium biology, this is the most logical choice:

**The Hydraulic Ground:** Water is the "biological internet." By building a pyramid over a cenote or an aquifer, the structure can **"imprint" its magnetic coherence** onto the entire water table and this can be used in agriculture to sustain a popullation in a magnetic decline. This is where Steiner and Schauberger work becomes operational for this discussion

**Isotopic Centrifuging:** If a pyramid acts as a vortex for magnetic flux, it uses the water as a **dielectric buffer to transfer the energy to other bits of matter**. The magnetic "torque" of the pyramid forces the **Deuterium** to separate from the **Protium**. The pyramid essentially creates a "well" of deuterium-depleted water (DDW) for the community by focusing the magnetic flux toward the civilization's water supply. The interesting issue is in a magnetic decline, water become more rare because as magnetic declines persist they create deserts. Think about the Earth and Mars as two neighbors who have different experiences with Magnetic fields and the water table. It also should be shocking that most pyramid structures are built over very dry places by humans. This tells us that the ancients knew about the link between the dynamo, water, and oxygen at some level.

**The Vortex Anchor:** Water provides the "low-friction" medium necessary for the magnetic vortex in humans to spin. Building a pyramid on dry sand is like running a motor without oil; building it over water allows the **Universal Stator** to ground into the Earth’s "liquid lattice." So from this perspective, pyramids may be marking other epoch's SAA problems. The fact that pyramids show up in many cultures tells us water table shrinkage and flooding are somehow linked to periods of relative inclination and declination of the magnetic field.

**4\. Global De-Fragging: The "Grid"**
If the LIAA/SAA is a drifting "vortex of darkness," then the global network of pyramids acts as a **"Magnetic Firewall."

For example, a**s the magnetic anomaly moved over the Yucatán, their pyramids were the only thing keeping the **Obex** of the population "clear."

**The Collapse:** When the anomaly grew too large or the "Universal Stator" thread frayed too much, the pyramids could no longer "rectify" the signal. The water became viscous, the "their use of basalt to de-frag" failed, and the civilization fell into the **Warburg-shifted atavism** and dispersed.

**From this perspective, p**yramids are **Isotopic Rectifiers**. They use basalt's paramagnetism and water's dipolar nature to create a localized zone of **high-dielectric coherence**. This suggest that the **"Great Pyramid"** wasn't a tomb, but a **planetary-scale D-filter** designed to keep the Earth's "water table" from becoming too heavy for consciousness.

The Pacific ring of fire specializes in basalt volcanoes, and El Salvador is a country that is entirely paramagnetic. It is one of the reasons I chose it over Mexico 5 years ago. This brings up the black sand beaches of El Salvador which are basically basalt fields of paramagnetic crystals along the entire coastline. Might these beaches represent volcanic magnetic flux power source in a decline?

Second idea. Volcanic stone also has a memory of history of magnetic field buried with in it. It seems to me this rock was heavily favored for pyramid cultures. And it makes sense to me we should consider this as a modern hack for living in a magnetic decline.

I've just connected the **Pacific Ring of Fire** to the **Universal Stator** through the lens of material science.
El Salvador’s **black sand beaches** are not just "sand"; they are a massive, decentralized **paramagnetic array that can transfer the memory of its magnetic memory**. Because basalt is rich in magnetite and ilmenite, every grain of that black sand is a tiny dipole that "remembers" the magnetic field intensity of the eruption that birthed it.

**5\. El Salvador as a "Magnetic Battery" for the SAA decline**

The entire coastline of El Salvador acts as a **solid-state magnetic buffer**.

**The Coastal Antenna:** As the ocean (an electrolyte/dipole) crashes against the basaltic black sand (paramagnetic crystals), it creates a **motional induction field**.

**Flux Concentration:** This interaction likely creates a localized "high-pressure" magnetic zone that acts as a shield against the expanding SAA. This is clear even in the new maps and I believe this is also seen in the South African blob that is present as a boundary condition. The "volcanic memory" trapped in the basalt provides a stable **reference frame** (the Stator) that helps "re-pin" the protons of anyone standing on those shores, preventing the **dielectric collapse** we see in the Atlantic.

**7\. The Pyramid as an "Isotopic Sieve"**

I have linked this idea to **Steiner and Schauberger** work and I believe this is the operational key in a decline event.

**Vortex Imprinting:** Schauberger knew that water "matures" through vortexian motion. A pyramid built over an aquifer uses **magnetic torque** to "centrifuge" the water table isotopically. He never connected it to deuterium fractionation, but I have in my thesis.

**DDW Agriculture:** By Focusing the magnetic flux into the water, the pyramid creates a "well" of **Deuterium-Depleted Water (DDW)**. DDW creates 40% more food production for humans to sustain a civilization in a decline. This allows a civilization to grow high-energy, low-entropy food (Steiner’s biodynamics) even when the global field is failing.

**The Desert Connection:** Now consider the physics, as magnetism declines, the "dielectric brake" fails, and water loses its structure and evaporates more easily, leading to **deserts**.

Building pyramids in dry places (Giza, Teotihuacán) wasn't an accident; it was an **emergency "hydraulic" intervention** to keep the local water table from "going Martian," in my humble opinion.

**8\. Mars vs. Earth: The Magnetic Funeral**

Mars is the "Atavistic Ghost" of a planet that lost its dynamo. Mars is a lesson for humans, but humans do not see yet, what I am proposing.

**The Loss of Oxygen:** When the Martian dynamo failed, the magnetic "pin" for water and oxygen was lost. The water became "heavy" (deuterated) and eventually boiled off into space.

**Earth's Survival:** Pyramids are the "life support" systems humans built to prevent Earth from becoming Mars during magnetic excursions. They are **"Vortex Anchors"**that ground the Universal Stator into the "liquid lattice" of the water table.

**9\. The "Modern Mitohack": Living in the Volcanic Field**

Most people view volcanic stone as "dead rock," but I identifying it as **"frozen magnetic light." I have covered my homes in El Salvador with this rock. This is how much I believe that I am on to something big here.

Basalt Living:** Using basalt or its minerals in modern architecture isn't just aesthetic; it’s a **de-fragging tool for a coming declination**. It provides the local "memory" of a strong magnetic field to compensate for the current dipole collapse.

**The SAA Protection:** For someone in El Salvador, the "volcanic memory" in the ground and the "black sand" on the beach are providing a **continuous magnetic reboot** that protects the **Obex** and the **160THz signal** from the SAA’s "Darkness" of a potential loss of consciousness.

**My decentralized Synthesis on this topic?:** Pyramids are **archaeological "markers" of past SAA events.** They show up wherever the water table was at risk of "deuteration." The Maya didn't just build monuments; they built **Magnetic Rectifiers** to keep their "liquid internet" (water) from becoming a viscous, atavistic trap.

What are the implications I am drawing here? Might **"Bitcoin City"** project at the base of the Conchagua volcano actually be the first **"Magnetic Pyramid" of the 21st century?

Might we be using** geothermal flux to power a new digital stator. Now you know why I favor El Salvador. It uses geothermal power to produce 22% of its energy. **GROWING FOOD IN THE DECLINE?**

Question: So the biodynamic practices of vortexing soil preparations in water and also using things like horns( spiral structures) may actually have its foundations in science after all? Same for the " towers of powers" or field broadcasting towers. Could this help in some areas or would it be a complete waste of time if the magnetic field was already diminished there?

ANSWER: If the field is gone it becomes superfluous. The wise will pay attention to what happens to living things in the crust and adjust appropriately. There is a reason John Wesley Powell made the US government aware of the longitude line and water table in the USA buried in the Louisiana Purchase.

Powell identified the 100th meridian as the dividing line between the humid eastern US and the arid West, where rain is insufficient for farming. Back in 1860 one year after the Carrington Event he basically told us where the water table of the USA had a good dielectric constant.

I told [@dralexisjazmyn](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31523/) on her podcast that data is way out of data and the longitude we need to pay attention to is much further east today.

I believe this is likely due to the drying out from the SAA as it approaches the Eastern Seaboard of the Atlantic considering what has happened recently to AMOC based on the latest data.

The eastward shift is so pronounced that even Tornado Alley is moving. In 2025, a majority of confirmed tornadoes occurred east of the Mississippi River for the first time in US history, as the classic dryline of the Plains pushed further into the Southeast.

John Wesley Powell’s original warning about the 100th meridian was based on a static line. He had no way of knowing it was linked to the magnetic dynamo and its flux. Today, that line is dynamic, and the "heritage of conflict" he predicted is now arriving in the fertile Mississippi River valley whose water is more deuterated than it has ever been. That means foods there will be as well if it grows. Why? Deuterium decreases agriculture food growth by 40% I have warned @prospertarian of this but I do not think he see it clearly.
