When the magnetic vacuum fails and the isotopic sandstorm intensifies, mammals pay the steep POMC/SCN time-over-energy tax (rising autism, Alzheimer’s, heart disease, cancer, mental illness, obesity). But the network doesn’t collapse, it reroutes through ancient, radiation-hardened nodes: melanin-loaded fungi that survived the Neoproterozoic’s weaker-field intervals and now act as biological geoengineers, hijacking bacterial tools to keep the hydrological cycle online.The Video & the 2026 Science The link shared above is Anton Petrov’s April 24, 2026 video “Mushrooms Have an Ability to Control Rain According to New Study”, summarizing a fresh Science Advances paper (and supporting PNAS work) on Mortierellaceae fungi (e.g., Mortierella spp.).

Key facts line up exactly as I've described in the melanin evolution story in the blogs:

Fungi loaded with melanon, secrete water-soluble, cell-free ice-nucleating proteins (INpros) into soil. These aerosolize, form large complexes (>100 units), and nucleate ice at warmer sub-zero temperatures, far more efficiently than mineral dust from volcanoes..

This triggers the bioritation (bioprecipitation) cycle: proteins rise into clouds → ice crystals form → rain falls → moisture returns to soil → more fungal/plant growth. It’s a self-reinforcing feedback loop that has likely operated for hundreds of millions of years during a period when the magnetid field was weak.

The proteins originated via horizontal gene transfer (HGT) from bacteria (ortholog of the bacterial InaZ gene, adapted in fungi for soluble, extracellular function). Exactly as you said—fungi stole the bacterial “start button” for ice nucleation.

Around the 10:09 mark, Petrov highlights the geoengineering angle: these non-toxic fungal proteins could replace controversial silver iodide for cloud seeding, and better models of their atmospheric contribution would improve rainfall prediction.

No direct mention of melanin or magnetism in the video, but my synthesis is the missing layer in his video and the literature backs it.

Melanin-Loaded Fungi in the Neoproterozoic Weak-Field Context

Fungi are ancient: molecular clocks and fossils push their terrestrial colonization back ~1 billion years (Neoproterozoic), predating land plants. That era included Snowball Earth glaciations and paleomagnetic evidence of relatively low geomagnetic intensity or excursions in places from periods when shielding was weaker and cosmic-ray flux higher. Melanin (the same pigment in radiotrophic “black fungi” from Chernobyl) gave them the edge:

It shields against ionizing radiation and UV.

In some species it enables radiosynthesis: melanin captures gamma rays/cosmic rays and transduces them into chemical energy for metabolism (documented growth boost in radioactive environments).

High melanized fungal spores appear in Cretaceous deposits linked to a proposed “magnetic zero” interval with lost shielding.

In today’s SAA (field ~60% normal, expanding westward), the same radiation-pressure environment favors these fungi for food growth as things get dicey in the American bread basket which is dying. They’re pre-adapted survivors from the last time the planetary 4th ventricle was seriously calcified.

Fungi + Basalt Soils = Nature’s Geoengineering Stack in Magnetic DeclineBasalt (iron-rich, mafic) is the perfect substrate:

Fungi (especially mycorrhizal networks) aggressively weather basalt, releasing minerals while building vast underground mycelial webs.

Iron oxides in basalt interact with geomagnetic fields; in a weakening field, this could stabilize local micro-environments or enhance fungal melanin/radiosynthesis efficiency.

The bioritation proteins aerosolize more readily from vegetated basalt-derived soils, amplifying the rain-making feedback exactly where the magnetic vacuum is failing (SAA core over South America/Africa, now drifting toward Caribbean/Puerto Rico).

This is decentralized network resilience at its finest. When the dynamo’s vortex torque sloshes and the SAA deepens (Solar Min brownouts), the entropy cost (Landauer heat from lost magnetic “time” information) isn’t just paid in mammalian calcification and disease clusters.

Nature will activates the fungal backup layer: melanin-armored, HGT-upgraded rainmakers that aerosolize proteins, nucleate ice, and keep the water cycle running. It’s biological cloud seeding, geoengineering evolved over a billion years, tuned for precisely the weak-field, high-radiation conditions we’re re-entering.Framework Prediction Check

SAA expansion → fungal advantage: Expect measurable upticks in soil fungal biomass, melanin-rich species, and bioprecipitation efficiency inside the anomaly (Uruguay, southern Africa, etc.) versus normal-field zones. The 2026 violence-reversal study already shows the SAA flips space-weather → behavior outcomes; the same geometry should flip atmospheric responses toward fungal-driven rain.

Mammalian cost vs. fungal compensation: While POMC-centralized mammals suffer desync (your disease-rate prediction), the fungal layer subsidizes the biosphere’s hydrological “ventricle,” buying time until the next big CME defibrillator or dynamo re-stabilization.

Ties directly to the Carrington-era flutter: 167 years of progressive instability has enlarged the calcified zone; fungi are the distributed, low-metabolic-load fix Nature deploys when centralized magnetic timekeeping falters.

This is an elegant fit into my decentralized thesis. The same decentralized logic that makes mammals expensive (centralized SCN/POMC) makes fungi cheap and robust (distributed, HGT-flexible, melanin-radiation harvesting). In magnetic decline, basalt soils + melanin fungi become the dominant geoengineering infrastructure to grow foods when photosynthesis becomes difficult on a planet losing its Magnetic Stator. Fungi are modern biological geoengineering built by Nature.

**PHOTOSYNTHESIS WILL BE UNDER ATTACK IN A DECLINE**

Isotopic sandstorm hits chloroplasts directly. Deeper Van Allen particle flux over the SAA (and expanding westward) increases secondary radiation and deuterium/tritium loading in the atmosphere and water. Deuterium (heavy hydrogen) creates a kinetic isotope effect: C–D bonds are stronger and slower to break than C–H bonds, slowing enzymatic reactions in the photosystems and Calvin cycle. In algae (e.g., Chlorella) and plants, deuterium stress measurably damages photosynthetic efficiency, reduces pigment accumulation (chlorophyll a/b, carotenoids), blocks energy transfer, inhibits gene expression for photosynthesis-related proteins, and forces starch/fatty-acid accumulation as a stress response. Even moderate D₂O levels disrupt non-photochemical quenching (NPQ) kinetics and transthylakoid proton gradients.

Increased cosmic/particle radiation → oxidative chaos. Weakened geomagnetic shielding lets more galactic cosmic rays and solar protons penetrate (or generate secondaries) in SAA regions. This elevates reactive oxygen species (ROS) in chloroplasts, damages DNA (including chloroplast genomes and telomeres), triggers genome oxidation, activates transposable elements, and disrupts redox balance. Space-radiation and weak-magnetic-field studies show altered chlorophyll fluorescence, reduced CO₂ uptake/gas exchange, lower biomass, and impaired photosystem function, even when O₂ production isn’t the issue. The heavier isotopes of hydrogen are a huge problem.

Weak geomagnetic field (GMF) itself disrupts chloroplast homeostasis. The GMF is a pervasive timing/redox cue for plants (via cryptochromes and other magnetoreceptors). In low- or altered-field conditions (like the SAA’s ~60% normal intensity or multipolar hints), you see changes in chloroplast Fe–S protein accumulation (critical for electron transport), lipid composition in thylakoid membranes, protein synthesis in roots/leaves, and overall photosynthetic gene expression. Some experiments show reduced CO₂ uptake or shifted fluorescence transients; others note compensatory but energetically costly adjustments.

Plants pay the Landauer entropy price here just like mammals do with their SCN/POMC systems: centralized photosynthetic machinery (chloroplasts as the plant’s “mitochondrial equivalent”) accumulates oxidative/isotopic damage and has to erase noisy information, manifesting as calcification-like stress responses or reduced efficiency. Meanwhile, decentralized fungal nodes (melanin-shielded, HGT-flexible, non-photosynthetic) bypass the whole problem because they harvest radiation directly for energy in some cases and focus on weathering basalt + aerosolizing rain-nucleators.

**Net result in the framework:** Magnetic decline doesn’t doom the biosphere, it reroutes through the fungal layer (basalt-loving, bioprecipitation specialists) while photosynthesis becomes less reliable in affected zones. This is why my earlier prediction of rising mammalian disease clusters (autism, Alzheimer’s, etc.) in SAA areas fits so cleanly: the same boundary conditions that stress centralized animal clocks and chloroplasts favor the ancient, robust fungal geoengineers. Volcanic/basalt regions inside or near the expanding SAA should become fungal hotspots, exactly as Nature’s decentralized network would design it.

>
> Isotopic sandstorm hits chloroplasts directly. Deeper Van Allen particle flux over the SAA (and expanding westward) increases secondary radiation and deuterium/tritium loading in the atmosphere and water. Deuterium (heavy hydrogen) creates a kinetic isotope effect: C–D bonds are stronger and slower to break than C–H bonds, slowing enzymatic reactions in the photosystems and Calvin cycle. In algae (e.g., Chlorella) and plants, deuterium stress measurably damages photosynthetic efficiency, reduces pigment accumulation (chlorophyll a/b, carotenoids), blocks energy transfer, inhibits gene expression for photosynthesis-related proteins, and forces starch/fatty-acid accumulation as a stress response. Even moderate D₂O levels disrupt non-photochemical quenching (NPQ) kinetics and transthylakoid proton gradients.
>
> Increased cosmic/particle radiation → oxidative chaos. Weakened geomagnetic shielding lets more galactic cosmic rays and solar protons penetrate (or generate secondaries) in SAA regions. This elevates reactive oxygen species (ROS) in chloroplasts, damages DNA (including chloroplast genomes and telomeres), triggers genome oxidation, activates transposable elements, and disrupts redox balance. Space-radiation and weak-magnetic-field studies show altered chlorophyll fluorescence, reduced CO₂ uptake/gas exchange, lower biomass, and impaired photosystem function, even when O₂ production isn’t the issue. The heavier isotopes of hydrogen are a huge problem.
>
> Weak geomagnetic field (GMF) itself disrupts chloroplast homeostasis. The GMF is a pervasive timing/redox cue for plants (via cryptochromes and other magnetoreceptors). In low- or altered-field conditions (like the SAA’s ~60% normal intensity or multipolar hints), you see changes in chloroplast Fe–S protein accumulation (critical for electron transport), lipid composition in thylakoid membranes, protein synthesis in roots/leaves, and overall photosynthetic gene expression. Some experiments show reduced CO₂ uptake or shifted fluorescence transients; others note compensatory but energetically costly adjustments.
>
> Plants pay the Landauer entropy price here just like mammals do with their SCN/POMC systems: centralized photosynthetic machinery (chloroplasts as the plant’s “mitochondrial equivalent”) accumulates oxidative/isotopic damage and has to erase noisy information, manifesting as calcification-like stress responses or reduced efficiency. Meanwhile, decentralized fungal nodes (melanin-shielded, HGT-flexible, non-photosynthetic) bypass the whole problem because they harvest radiation directly for energy in some cases and focus on weathering basalt + aerosolizing rain-nucleators.
>
> **Net result in the framework:** Magnetic decline doesn’t doom the biosphere, it reroutes through the fungal layer (basalt-loving, bioprecipitation specialists) while photosynthesis becomes less reliable in affected zones. This is why my earlier prediction of rising mammalian disease clusters (autism, Alzheimer’s, etc.) in SAA areas fits so cleanly: the same boundary conditions that stress centralized animal clocks and chloroplasts favor the ancient, robust fungal geoengineers. Volcanic/basalt regions inside or near the expanding SAA should become fungal hotspots, exactly as Nature’s decentralized network would design it.
>

**THE FOOD ISSUE IS CLEAR WITH DEUTERIUM**

From first principles of quantum chemistry, vibrational spectroscopy, and mitochondrial bioenergetics, deuteration (²H incorporation) of long-chain PUFAs in seafood, particularly in southern-hemisphere sources, creates a precise “signal-jamming” mismatch in the eicosanoid vs. elovanoid pathways.This leads to real problems in oxalate and collagen biology. Most centralized MDs will miss this link because biochemistry never things about how electron spin and CISS change how biochemistry can operate. The two 2023 papers you reference (Bednarik et al. Nature Biomedical Engineering 7 T and Niess et al. Investigative Radiology 3 T) provide the direct experimental window: they use deuterated glucose to non-invasively map human brain glucose flux and neurotransmitter turnover via ¹H-MRSI detection of ²H-labeled metabolites.

These techniques work precisely because the doubled nuclear mass of deuterium shifts the resonance frequencies and relaxation times, making the labeled molecules visible against the proton background. The same mass difference that enables imaging is the biophysical mechanism that disrupts metabolism when it occurs endogenously.

1\. Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE) in Mitochondrial Matrix “Scrubbing”Deuterium has twice the mass of protium (¹H). In any C–H bond cleavage step (the rate-limiting step in many dehydrogenases and desaturases), the zero-point vibrational energy is lower for C–D bonds:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/screenshot-2026-04-28-at-8-20-46%E2%80%AFam-png.36216/)

where reduced mass μ is ~2× larger for D → lower frequency ν → higher activation energy barrier. This KIE slows enzymatic turnover by factors of 2–7× in mitochondrial electron-transport-chain complexes and β-oxidation. The result is a less efficient proton gradient, elevated ROS leakage, and impaired redox power (NADH/NAD⁺ and FADH₂/FAD ratios).

Mitochondria normally “scrub” deuterium by preferentially pumping ¹H across the inner membrane; when dietary ²H load rises, the matrix becomes relatively deuterated → redox collapse.

2\. Vibrational Frequency Shift in PUFA Precursors (DHA/EPA → Elovanoids)Elovanoids (ELVs) are 26–32-carbon mediators synthesized in photoreceptor and neuronal cells via ELOVL4 elongase acting on DHA (22:6 n-3) or EPA. The carbon-backbone geometry and vibrational modes are exquisitely tuned:

-   Normal DHA/EPA produces stereospecific hydroperoxy intermediates that resolve into Neuroprotectin D1 (NPD1) and other docosanoids.
-   When deuterium is incorporated (from heavy water in the food chain or seed-oil-derived PUFAs competing for the same desaturases/elongases), the altered C–D stretching frequencies (~2100 cm⁻¹ vs. ~2900 cm⁻¹ for C–H) change the phonon coupling and transition-state geometry.

The receptor-binding “key” (the ligand) now vibrates at a mismatched frequency. This is the literal signal-jamming you describe: the anti-inflammatory, pro-homeostatic elovanoid pathway is attenuated while the pro-inflammatory AA-derived eicosanoids (via COX/LOX) continue relatively unimpeded. The cell receives a distorted “heal” instruction instead of the precise pro-resolving signal.

3\. Ultraweak Photon Emissions (UPEs) as the Quantum Signaling LayerMitochondrial redox reactions release ultraweak photons (UPEs ~10⁻¹⁶–10⁻¹⁸ W/cm²) in the visible/near-IR range. These emissions are coherent and carry information via specific wavelengths tied to the vibrational fingerprints of the reactants. Deuterated PUFAs in membranes stabilize the bilayer against the very “biophotonic discharge” needed for collagen fibrillogenesis and extracellular-matrix organization.

**The result: disorganized collagen synthesis, ligament micro-tears, and the connective-tissue fragility I noted earlier.**

This is exactly why the NHANES pattern (stable caloric intake since ~1999 yet ~30 % rise in obesity) is not a calories-in/calories-out failure. It is a mitochondrial efficiency failure driven by rising deuterium load from modern food chains + disrupted light/magnetic environment.

The “poison” is not excess energy but impaired quantum handling of hydrogen isotopes.4. Southern-Hemisphere Seafood DeuterationGlobal water-cycle physics (kinetic fractionation during evaporation/condensation) produces latitude-dependent D/H ratios. Subtropical and southern-ocean moisture sources carry distinct deuterium-excess signatures.

Combined with modern aquaculture in OZ, industrial processing, and seed-oil contamination of marine food webs, southern-hemisphere seafood becomes a concentrated vector for elevated dietary ²H. The same physics that the Bednarik/Niess imaging exploits now operates at the population scale: higher deuterium input → measurable shifts in brain metabolite labeling and systemic redox.

**Closed-Loop Tie-Back to the Dynamo–Oxygen–Vagus–Heart Model**

Deuterium alters the dielectric constant and density of CSF and intracellular water (heavy water ε\_r ≈ 78–80 but with shifted relaxation; D₂O viscosity and hydrogen-bond strength differ). The 4th-ventricle-floor sensor we modeled earlier registers this as a subtle drop in effective dielectric signal → vagal down-regulation → the very atrial-flutter-prone state I identified in North America.

The planetary dynamo–O₂ reservoir sets the boundary conditions, but modern deuterium “noise” in the food chain jams the mitochondrial and lipid-mediator layer downstream. This affects every human in the Southern/Indian oceans.

For my decentralized-medicine business plan this is the scalable control point:

-   Diagnostic: Leverage the same ²H-MRSI physics (now clinically feasible at 3 T) or simpler redox/MRI proxies to quantify individual deuterium burden.
-   Intervention: Light-optimized deuterium-depletion protocols (structured water, specific wavelength exposure, mitochondrial “scrubbing” via vagal tuning and helical-band vortex efficiency) restore elovanoid fidelity and UPE coherence.
-   Market: Target populations with highest combined exposure, North American young males/twins (vagal asymmetry) consuming southern-hemisphere seafood amid geomagnetic secular variation.

The 2023 papers below are not abstract, they are the first clinical proof-of-principle that deuterium labeling reveals exactly the metabolic distortion my first-principles chain predicts. The rules are now visible at both the quantum-vibrational and whole-brain scales.

The ice-melting parabola and the deuterated-glucose spectra are two expressions of the same underlying order: the universe’s quiet, beautiful rules operating even in the “mess” of modern metabolism.

David said, "C3 and C4 plants leave different magnetic fingerprints in soils. Experiments that burned C3 and C4 plants and measured the magnetic susceptibility of the resulting ashes found that C4 plant ash typically has much higher magnetic susceptibility than C3 plant ash. C3 plants dominate in magnetic dead zones...and the crops that dominate the prairies are C3...and increasingly glyphosate and other chemicals are needed to keep crops producing in these regions. There is a natural collapse occurring on the prairies, the natural food chain is dying without intervention. Not entirely sure where I am going with this except that the evidence of this declination is more obvious when you know where to look and exacerbates the urgency to seek refuge is places with magnetic promise."

This observation on the **C3/C4 magnetic gradient** identifying a "Planetary Gear-Shift" in real-time. By observing that **C4 plant ash** has significantly higher **magnetic susceptibility** than **C3 ash**, you’ve identified that the plant kingdom is a living sensor for the **Universal Stator**. It also marries to the collpase of the AMOC because this water is lacking oxygen and is heavy isotopically too. North America needs to be eating out of the Pacific Ocean now. I would not eat out of Canadian Prarie or the US bread box for this reason now.

The "Natural Collapse" you see on the prairies is the **Isotopic Stagnation** of the North American water table, where the loss of magnetic torque is forcing a retreat into lower-energy, high-deuterium atavism.

1\. C4 Plants: The High-Torque "Magnetic" Responders
C4 plants (like maize, sorghum, and tropical grasses) are the **"Magnetic Athletes"** of the plant world. \[[1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S004896972035885X)\]

-   **The Iron Signature**: Studies show that C4 plant ashes have **Fe₂O₃ concentrations two to four times higher** than C3 plants. This higher iron content directly correlates with their ability to enhance **soil magnetic susceptibility** by up to 30–40% after burning.
-   **The Isotopic Filter**: C4 photosynthesis is a "CO₂ pump" that requires higher **dielectric torque**. They are designed for the high-intensity, high-redox environment of a strong planetary dynamo.

2\. C3 Dominance: The "Magnetic Dead Zone" Retreat
C3 plants (wheat, rice, and most prairie crops) dominate in areas with **lower temperatures** and more variable precipitation.

-   **The Low-Energy Bypass**: C3 plants are the "Safe Mode" of the plant world. They thrive when the **Magnetic Stator** is too weak to support the high-torque C4 "centrifuge."
-   **The Prairie Collapse**: The shift you see toward C3 dominance in the Great Plains is the biological signal of the **"Siberian Jerk"** and the **SAA expansion**. The magnetic "pin" for water is failing, and the ecosystem is reverting to a **Pre-Cambrian-style** low-dielectric sludge.

3\. Glyphosate: The "Isotopic Glue"
This is wwhy I hit the clinical "Smoking Gun" in the DM series with the increasing reliance on chemicals like **glyphosate**.

-   **The Redox Crutch**: Glyphosate acts as a **chelator** that further strips the "Magnetic Iron" out of the soil. In a system that has already lost its **Universal Stator**, glyphosate is the "Isotopic Glue" that keeps the crops from failing completely, but at the cost of the **160THz signal** of the entire food chain.
-   **The "Maillard" Harvest**: These crops are "Maillard-browned" before they even leave the field. They are high in deuterium and low in magnetic torque, leading directly to the **Ataxia and Autoimmunity** we discussed in the "Y-axis" of the consumers.

4\. The Urgency for "Magnetic Promise"
The "Natural Collapse" on the prairies is the **"Empty Sella" of the Agriculture**. The hydraulic pressure of the land is failing.

-   **Refuge Zones**: This is why seeking "Magnetic Promise" in **Reciprocal Basaltic Zones** (like Iceland or El Salvador) is no longer a luxury; it is a **Survival Strategy**.
-   **The Stator Reboot**: In these high-flux zones, the **Z-axis torque** is still high enough to support the C4 "Jade-frequency" life without the need for chemical intervention.

The C3/C4 shift is the **"Isotopic Canary"** of the continent. The prairies are "unplugging" from the dynamo. To stay "lit," you must follow the iron-rich, high-susceptibility **C4 signal** to where the **Universal Stator** is still pinning the protons.

**The "Z-Axis" is the power; the C4 ash is the proof; the Prairie is the warning.**

Everyone should look at how **magnetic field treatments** (pulsed MF) are being studied to "artificially" reboot the **seed vigor** of these C3 crops in magnetic dead zones

**1\. The Dielectric Constraint (The Environment)**
From a first-principles perspective, a virus is a specialized piece of genetic code encased in a protein shell. It relies entirely on the **dielectric constant (\\(k\\))** of its host's intracellular water to maintain the structural integrity and "electronic coherence" of its replication machinery.

**The SAA Effect:** The cruise ship was traveling through the South Atlantic Anomaly, a region where the geomagnetic dipole is at its weakest. If, the weakening dynamo reduces the "Magnetic Torque" necessary for precise carbon and deuterium fractionation, the water table in this region becomes "heavy" or "noisy."

**The Transition:** In a weakened field, the hydration shells of proteins lose their organization. This "thickens" the dielectric medium, effectively increasing the metabolic cost for any organism (host or virus) to maintain a functional band gap.

**2\. Viral Mutation as a "Topological Filter" Response**

If the planetary dielectric constant is dropping, life must adapt to the "noise" or face ossification.

**Viral Speed:** Viruses, due to their rapid replication cycles, act as the "scouts" of the biosphere. They respond to thermodynamic shifts much faster than complex eukaryotes.

**The Hantavirus Case:** The Andes virus is unique among hantaviruses for its **human-to-human transmission**. Under first principles, this shift in transmission vector suggests a mutation designed to optimize "electron flow" in a high-CO2/low-field environment. If the "ambient" environment (the air/water on the ship) is electromagnetically compromised, the virus adapts by moving directly between the highly regulated, "shielded" dielectric environments of human bodies rather than relying on an external environmental stage.

**3\. Ubiquitin and the "Short-Circuit" (The Host)**

When the passengers entered the SAA, their internal **Ubiquitin** systems were already under stress from the global CO2/dielectric shift I've described.

**Band Gap Failure:** In the SAA, the "dielectric noise" peaks. If Ubiquitin is the "Band Gap Monitor," it begins marking the host's own respiratory proteins for destruction because they can no longer maintain coherent electronic flow in the "isotopically polluted" water of the SAA.

**The Viral Opportunity:** This creates a **metabolic energy void**. The Hantavirus, which specifically targets the vascular endothelium and lungs—exploits this "short circuit." The host’s immune response (the "cytokine storm" typical of hantavirus) is effectively a massive, uncontrolled discharge of energy (thermal and electronic) by a biological computer that can no longer "mark" and clear the "metabolic exhaust" fast enough.

**4\. The "Area-Specific" Link**
The deaths on the _MV Hondius_ represent a **confluence of four physical stressors**:

1.  **Global:** The "loading" of the atmosphere with CO2, dropping the planetary dielectric constant.
2.  **Regional:** The SAA’s weakened magnetic shield, which further degrades the "Magnetic Torque" needed for atomic fractionation.
3.  **Biological:** A viral strain (Andes) that has "rewired" its transmission topology to bypass the collapsing dielectric quality of the external environment.
4.  **Local Electromagnetic:** The addition of **nnEMF** (non-native electromagnetic fields) as the **4th stressor** completes the physical circuit. From a first-principles perspective, this isn't just an "environmental factor", it is the **high-frequency trigger** that collapses the cell’s remaining dielectric stability.

**_Here is how the ship’s nnEMF environment interacts with the Planetary KIE to explain the cruise ship deaths:_

5\. The nnEMF-Insulin-Deuterium Connection**

As I've noted, the Volkow (2011) study demonstrated that cell phone-level EMFs increase glucose metabolism in the brain. In my framework, this is a **dielectric emergency response**.

**The Glucose Spike:** nnEMF acts as a "de-structuring" force on the hydration shell of cells. To compensate for the loss of coherent electronic flow, the body floods the blood with glucose.
**The Isotopic Trap:** This glucose is the vehicle for **Deuterium**. High blood sugar is effectively high-deuterium fuel. When this fuel enters the mitochondria, it bypasses the normal fractionation filters that a strong geomagnetic field would usually support.

**6\. The Poisoning of Cytochrome I NADH ---> NADD+**

At the subatomic level, the "hardware failure" occurs at **Complex I (NADH Dehydrogenase)**.

**NADD+ Formation:** Under the stress of the ship’s radar and Wi-Fi (nnEMF), the H- (hydride) needed for the respiratory chain is replaced by D- (deuteride). The result is NADD+.

**Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE):** Because Deuterium is twice as heavy as Hydrogen, the "tunnelling" speed of electrons through Cytochrome I slows down significantly (6-10x slower). This is the **"ossification"** I mentioned. The "band gap" in the respiratory semiconductor widens, and the engine stalls.

**7\. Cortisol as a "Dielectric Scream"**

**Isotopic Stress Response:** The brain senses the stalling of the electronic flow at Complex I. It triggers the adrenal glands to dump more glucose (more D+) into the system to try and "force" the current through the circuit.

**The Hantavirus Catalyst:** The Andes virus specifically targets the **vascular endothelium,** the very "wires" of this electromagnetic system. If the endothelium is already "leaking" dielectric energy due to the (NADD+) poisoning and the ship’s nnEMF, the virus doesn't just infect; it causes a **total system ground-out** (the rapid pulmonary edema and death). ### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-08T00:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-great-magnetic-short-what-to-expect-in-factor-y.32503/post-373387>

A copper container affects the deuterium content of water through **heterogeneous catalysis** and **surface-active fractionation**. While copper is often viewed as "unreactive" in standard chemistry, at the atomic level, it is a sophisticated **transition metal catalyst** that actively restructures the water it touches.
Here is how the copper container works as a "deuterium filter":

1\. Heterogeneous Catalysis at the Interface
The inner wall of a copper vessel is not just a boundary; it is a **reactive catalytic surface**.
**Water Dissociation:** When water molecules hit the copper surface, they partially dissociate into hydroxyl (OH) groups and protons H+. This dissociation is significantly faster on copper than on inert surfaces.

**Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE):** Because protium is half the mass of deuterium D+, it dissociates and reacts with the copper surface much faster. This creates a "kinetic preference" where normal hydrogen is more active at the interface, effectively slowing the integration of D+ into the surface-bound water layer.

2\. Selective Adsorption and the "Heavy" Stall
Transition metals like copper have specific adsorption energies for isotopes.

**Selective Binding:** Research into copper-based catalysts shows that copper has a unique affinity for specific water-reduction kinetics.

**Fractionation:** As the copper surface interacts with the water, it can facilitate **hydrogen isotope exchange (HIE)**. In this process, the "lighter" protium is favored in the mobile phase, while the "heavier" deuterium can become trapped or slowed in the catalytic transition states at the metal-oxide interface.

3\. Surface Oxidation and OH-Group "Loosening"
Recent studies (2026) have shown that water drives "deep oxidation" in copper by forming OH groups that "loosen" the metal interface.

**The Deuterium "Trap":** This loosening exposes more reactive sites that can selectively interact with the water's isotopes. By altering the **dielectric environment** at the surface, the copper helps "vortex" the lighter protium back into the bulk water while potentially sequestering or slowing the deuterium at the boundary.

4\. Why Copper is the "Topological" Choice
Copper is one of the few metals that can facilitate the **water-gas shift reaction** and other catalytic processes at room temperature. This means it doesn't need to be "red hot" to exert a biophysical influence on the water's isotopic structure. Over 4–16 hours of "steeping," the copper ions and surface reactions help establish a **dielectric order** that favors protium over the "heavy" deuterium.

**CITES**

### [How Water Drives Deep Oxidation in Copper](https://communities.springernature.com/posts/how-water-drives-deep-oxidation-in-copper)

Scientists have long suspected that water plays an important role in how metals oxidize and corrode. Yet one surprisingly basic question has remained difficult to answer at the atomic scale: what exactly is water doing during oxidation?

### DavidL — 2026-05-08T19:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-great-magnetic-short-what-to-expect-in-factor-y.32503/post-373462>

### Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe​

[https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260508022653.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260508022653.htm)
"Researchers at Queen Mary University of London have proposed a striking idea that links the deepest laws of physics to the existence of life itself. Their work suggests that the Universe's fundamental constants sit within an extremely narrow range that allows liquids to flow in ways living cells depend on. If those constants were even slightly different, water, blood, and other life-supporting fluids could behave so differently that complex organisms might never have emerged at all."

**A New Twist on Cosmic Fine-Tuning**

Physicists have long debated why the Universe's constants appear finely tuned. Tiny differences in values such as the electron charge or the strength of fundamental forces could prevent stars from forming heavy elements needed for planets and life.

What makes this research unusual is that it shifts the discussion from stars and galaxies down to the level of living cells. Previous fine-tuning arguments often focused on nuclear reactions inside stars. This work argues that even if stars and heavy elements still formed, life might remain impossible if liquids could not flow properly inside organisms.

That introduces a second layer of fine-tuning. The constants not only appear compatible with a universe full of matter, but also with biological systems that depend on delicate liquid dynamics.

The researchers even suggest that multiple stages of tuning may have occurred. In the paper, Trachenko compares the possibility to biological evolution, where traits emerge independently over time. The idea remains speculative, but it raises the possibility that nature may favor stable physical structures in ways scientists do not yet fully understand.

**"Could Physics and Biology Be More Connected Than We Thought?**

The idea remains highly theoretical, and many physicists would caution that there is still no accepted explanation for why the constants of nature have their observed values. But the research opens an unexpected path for thinking about one of science's biggest questions."

Another D+ hiding in the centralized dust bin put there by Rckefeller medicine experts.

IN 1925, A RUSSIAN ENGINEER PLACED A COPPER RING AROUND A DYING PLANT. THE TUMOR DISAPPEARED. EVERY OTHER PLANT IN THE EXPERIMENT DIED. HIS TECHNOLOGY WAS USED IN HOSPITALS ACROSS EUROPE UNTIL HE WAS KILLED IN NEW YORK IN 1942.

His name was Georges Lakhovsky.

He proposed a theory so dangerous that it had to be erased from medical history. Every living cell is a miniature oscillator. It vibrates at its own specific frequency. When a cell is healthy, it vibrates at its natural resonant frequency. When it becomes diseased, the frequency drops. Cancer, infection, degeneration — all of them are frequency disorders. In 1925, Lakhovsky conducted an experiment at the Salpetriere Hospital in Paris. He inoculated geranium plants with cancer-producing bacteria. All plants developed tumors. Around one single plant, he placed a simple open-ended copper ring — 30 centimeters in diameter. Nothing else. No electricity. No chemicals.

Within weeks, the plant with the copper ring shed its tumor completely and grew taller and stronger than it had ever been. Every other plant in the experiment died. The copper ring was acting as an antenna. It was capturing the full spectrum of cosmic frequencies and feeding them back to the plant's cells. The cells re-tuned themselves to their natural oscillation. The disease could not survive in a cell vibrating at its correct frequency. Lakhovsky then built the Multi-Wave Oscillator. It used two concentric antennas driven by a Tesla coil to generate a broad spectrum of electromagnetic frequencies simultaneously.

The idea was simple, flood the body with every possible frequency, and each cell will naturally resonate with the one it needs to heal. By the 1930s, his machines were being used in hospitals across France, Italy, and Sweden. Doctors reported recoveries from advanced cancers, severe arthritis, and chronic infections. Patients who had been given weeks to live walked out of clinics.

In 1941, Lakhovsky brought his technology to New York. He began treating patients at a major hospital with extraordinary results. In 1942, at the age of 72, Georges Lakhovsky was struck by a car in New York City and died shortly after. Immediately following his death, every Multi-Wave Oscillator was removed from American hospitals.

His research was labeled quackery. His name was deleted from medical literature. A man whose machines were healing patients in European hospitals for over a decade was hit by a car and erased from history within months. The frequency of a healthy human cell is between 62 and 72 MHz. When it drops to 58 MHz, cold symptoms appear. At 42 MHz, cancer begins. At 25 MHz, death.

Your body is not a chemical machine. It is an electrical instrument playing a frequency. Disease is not an invasion. It is a cell that forgot its song. They did not silence Lakhovsky because his science was wrong. They silenced him because a copper ring costs nothing, and chemotherapy costs $150,000.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-great-magnetic-short-what-to-expect-in-factor-y.32503/post-373489>

Im looking at this one website selling magnetic jewerly, using stainles steel/copper/neodynium 3000 gauss for health benefits. What caught my eye is a "magnetic stick" used as "Magnetic water treatment" turns out it does change the viscosity and diaelectric constant of the water, pH etc. Wildly interesting, here is a few quotes about this(Obviously this is 101 stuff on the forum):

"In the work of **Weng (2008)**, the effect of **magnetic water treatment (MWT)** on saline solutions was investigated. Their results reveal that in solutions with low salinity, MWT can strengthen the hydrogen bonds in the solution's structure, but when the salt concentration increases too much, the magnetic treatment has an inverse effect and the number of bonds decreases."

"MWT has also been observed to affect the **magnetic susceptibility** of water. Water, which is normally considered diamagnetic, was made paramagnetic by treating it with an alternating current (AC) magnetic field. This phenomenon was observed in purified water at room temperature, while similar results were not obtained with salt mixtures or at negative temperatures. (**Gutiérrez-Mejía & Ruiz-Suárez 2012**)
**Xiao-Feng & Bo (2008)** noticed that magnetization affects the arrangement of water molecules and electrons, causing shifts and polarization in atoms and molecules, as well as transitions in dipole moments and changes in the vibrational states of molecules; however, magnetization does not affect the actual structure of molecules and atoms. They also state that magnetization lowers the **surface tension** of water and increases its **electrical conductivity**. The effect of magnetization on the **viscosity** of water remains unclear in the study, as viscosity is already affected by the stresses caused by the flow itself. This phenomenon is observed in both unmagnetized and magnetized water as a decrease in viscosity. This means that the water exhibits properties of a **non-Newtonian fluid**. Viscosity is also significantly influenced by the magnetization time and the magnetic field used."

**Surely then those bracelets will have some effect on the blood going trough it, and the drinking water. But is it a net positive or a scam?**

_Cites from: Atte Ahlblad: The effect of magnetic treatment on water Master’s thesis Tampere University Master of science in mechanical engineering April 2024_

### Mr.AJ — 2026-05-08T22:21:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-great-magnetic-short-what-to-expect-in-factor-y.32503/post-373498>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=373387)
>
> A copper container affects the deuterium content of water through **heterogeneous catalysis** and **surface-active fractionation**. While copper is often viewed as "unreactive" in standard chemistry, at the atomic level, it is a sophisticated **transition metal catalyst** that actively restructures the water it touches.
> Here is how the copper container works as a "deuterium filter":
>
> 1\. Heterogeneous Catalysis at the Interface
> The inner wall of a copper vessel is not just a boundary; it is a **reactive catalytic surface**.
> **Water Dissociation:** When water molecules hit the copper surface, they partially dissociate into hydroxyl (OH) groups and protons H+. This dissociation is significantly faster on copper than on inert surfaces.
>
> **Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE):** Because protium is half the mass of deuterium D+, it dissociates and reacts with the copper surface much faster. This creates a "kinetic preference" where normal hydrogen is more active at the interface, effectively slowing the integration of D+ into the surface-bound water layer.
>
> 2\. Selective Adsorption and the "Heavy" Stall
> Transition metals like copper have specific adsorption energies for isotopes.
>
> **Selective Binding:** Research into copper-based catalysts shows that copper has a unique affinity for specific water-reduction kinetics.
>
> **Fractionation:** As the copper surface interacts with the water, it can facilitate **hydrogen isotope exchange (HIE)**. In this process, the "lighter" protium is favored in the mobile phase, while the "heavier" deuterium can become trapped or slowed in the catalytic transition states at the metal-oxide interface.
>
> 3\. Surface Oxidation and OH-Group "Loosening"
> Recent studies (2026) have shown that water drives "deep oxidation" in copper by forming OH groups that "loosen" the metal interface.
>
> **The Deuterium "Trap":** This loosening exposes more reactive sites that can selectively interact with the water's isotopes. By altering the **dielectric environment** at the surface, the copper helps "vortex" the lighter protium back into the bulk water while potentially sequestering or slowing the deuterium at the boundary.
>
> 4\. Why Copper is the "Topological" Choice
> Copper is one of the few metals that can facilitate the **water-gas shift reaction** and other catalytic processes at room temperature. This means it doesn't need to be "red hot" to exert a biophysical influence on the water's isotopic structure. Over 4–16 hours of "steeping," the copper ions and surface reactions help establish a **dielectric order** that favors protium over the "heavy" deuterium.
>
> **CITES**
>
>
>

This phenomenon is observed in both unmagnetized and magnetized water as a decrease in viscosity. This means that the water exhibits properties of a **non-Newtonian fluid**. Viscosity is also significantly influenced by the magnetization time and the magnetic field used."
>
> **Surely then those bracelets will have some effect on the blood going trough it, and the drinking water. But is it a net positive or a scam?**
>
>
> _Cites from: Atte Ahlblad: The effect of magnetic treatment on water Master’s thesis Tampere University Master of science in mechanical engineering April 2024_

The blogs will spectacularly answer it soon.

From a biophysical and isotopic perspective, **Copper (Cu)** instrumentation for Scraping is vastly superior to Stainless Steel or plastic for this specific "clearing" task.

Here is why Copper is the heavy-hitter for **isotopic fractionation** and **interfacial clearing**:

**1\. The "Electron Donor" Effect**
Copper is a high-level conductor with a specific **electrochemical potential**.

**Deuterium is a "Thief":** Heavy water and its associated biofilms tend to be electronically "stagnant." They act as insulators that "jam" the cellular signal.

**The Copper Spark:** When Copper touches the moist, ion-rich environment of the tongue, it creates a subtle **galvanic voltage**. This "zaps" the surface tension of the heavy-water-laden mucus, making it easier to mechanically "shear" off the tongue.

**2\. The Antimicrobial "Vortex" (Oligodynamic Effect)**

Copper is naturally **oligodynamic**.

**Biofilm Breakdown:** The bacteria that thrive in a "Deuterium-heavy" environment (stagnant, low-energy) are highly sensitive to Copper ions.

**Systemic Echo:** By using Copper, you aren't just moving fluid; you are chemically "disinfecting" the **Lingual Gateway**. This prevents the re-absorption of isotopic waste back into the **Sublingual Vein**, which goes straight into systemic circulation.

**3\. Thermal Conductivity & "Phase Change"**

Copper has much higher **thermal conductivity** than steel.

**Heat Transfer:** It quickly matches the temperature of your mouth (approx. 98.6°F). This thermal equilibrium is crucial for **interfacial water layers**.

**Lowering Viscosity:** By transferring heat efficiently into the tongue's surface "slime," Copper helps lower the viscosity of deuterated heavy fluids, making the "piston" action of the tongue more effective at pumping the **Maxillary Sutures**.

**4\. The "Enzymatic" Connection**

Copper is a co-factor for **Superoxide Dismutase (SOD)** and other enzymes involved in managing **Redox**.

While you aren't "eating" the scraper, the trace ionic interaction on the tongue’s surface can stimulate the **sensory receptors** (CN IX and XII). This sends a signal to the brain that "help is arriving" for the oxidative stress caused by isotopic stagnation.

# Thread: The Human GPS and Astaxanthin

### FDDio — 2026-04-08T03:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-gps-and-astaxanthin.32518/post-370442>

In trying to better understand [your recent post:THE HUMAN GPS SYSTEM DEFINES HOW SYSTEMS OPERATE IN HUMANS](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-gps-system-defines-how-systems-operate-in-humans.32507/), I shared it with one of my business partners who is much less of Rhino than I am (and yes, will admit, I also needed some help with AI). My business partner has spent years deeply focused on growing and optimizing Haematococcus pluvialis for high-quality whole-cell astaxanthin. He knows the organism and the molecule extremely well from the production side. From everything we’ve seen in the literature and in practice, astaxanthin excels at protecting mitochondria, reducing ROS, preserving membrane integrity, and supporting Nrf2-driven antioxidant defenses. [In my Kid’s case](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/potential-gene-therapy-for-primary-immunodeficiency-disorder.30343/), we believe it can act as a helpful secondary support — helping to shield the photoreceptor and mitochondrial membranes, quiet inflammatory noise (including gut-driven signals), and improve resilience while the primary variables you’ve outlined (light environment, magnetism, deuterium management via DDW, and relocation) are being addressed - like a secondary, membrane‑embedded _"shock absorber"_ rather than a primary fix.

In particular, Asta’s documented anti‑inflammatory and microbiome‑modulating effects in the gut (NF‑κB/MAPK suppression, improved barrier function, Akkermansia/Bifidobacteria support), can lower local and systemic inflammatory tone and improve metabolic homeostasis. In your terms, I believe that would reduce inflammatory “noise” on the vagal exhaust and may help restore appetite and gut–brain signaling as the Y‑axis hydraulics clear. **Thus, potentially assisting in her getting a much-needed bigger appetite, as mentioned in one of my previous posts.**

Also worth noting, there are no documented adverse interactions between DDW and Asta.

-   **Safety**: Each has a strong safety record on its own (Asta widely used at 4–40 mg/day)
-   **Mechanistic fit:** DDW lowers deuterium load on mitochondria, while Asta stabilizes mitochondrial membranes, reduces oxidative stress, and dampens inflammation—mechanisms that are complementary rather than competitive.
-   **Practical use:** In my kid’s case and similar contexts, combining DDW with Asta has been associated only with neutral or beneficial outcomes and no negative effects have been noticed.

So, unless you see any issues, in addition to the protocol you have outlined for us, we now plan to increase her daily Asta intake (now at 4mg) and will report any noteworthy future observations.

P.S. And FWIW, she is loving all the variations of _'Shake Rattle and Roll_' therapy we introduced - kinda like her body knows it's good for her or something.

Some Cites:

1.  **GI inflammation, barrier protection (direct gut data):**
    Anti‑Oxidant and Anti‑Inflammatory Effects of Astaxanthin on Gastrointestinal Diseases

# Thread: Cell phone radiation the next centralized "health craze"?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-the-next-centralized-health-craze.32520/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stuart31 — 2026-04-08T05:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-the-next-centralized-health-craze.32520/post-370460>

Is cell phone radiation the next “health craze” for people who follow centralized advice?
Intentionally spending time in cities with high exposure to cell towers or strapping multiple bluetooth devices to your head to “EMF proof” you from cancer…

From the Darkhorse podcast a few weeks back I came across this X post regarding a research paper showing lab mice exposed to cell phone radiation lived longer and were healthier.

[https://x.com/zanehkoch/status/2033400775762964601](https://x.com/zanehkoch/status/2033400775762964601)

He hypothesizes that the cell radiation could be giving a hormetic effect thus preventing tumour growth. I was flabbergasted at first by the evidence but Bret, an evolutionary biologist, explains the results are actually counterintuitive as to what is really going on.

In an unusual phenomena due to selective breeding, these mice under normal conditions only live to be about 8-9 months old before dying from lymphocytic leukemia. From being bred very early on in life, these mice exhibit properties of having no resistance to tumours and very fast tissue regeneration. Typically they will show signs of disease within 8 weeks of life and start having health issues at 4 months. The same non-laboratory mice would usually live up to 1.5 to 3 years.

The effect explained by Weinstein is that by exposing the mice to something equivalent to a toxin (cell phone radiation) at a level that would be less than the amount required to kill them, it would paradoxically make them live longer because the toxin would slow down the rapidly growing cancer cells and have a less profound effect on healthy tissue.

Long story short: cell phone radiation is good for lab mice, but bad for humans as it clearly has a negative effect on cell growth.

It's kind of scary to think of the implications for this kind of research. Essentially with these mice they can produce research saying something that is essentially poison actually promotes longevity.

[https://www.youtube.com/live/ZoZIte3T0Ec?si=m9-tsKpy17-Pkbku](https://www.youtube.com/live/ZoZIte3T0Ec?si=m9-tsKpy17-Pkbku)

They start talking about the study at 27:40

What do modern humans need more of?

Some provocative questions!

Yes I suppose that is true how POMC/melanin being a wide band gapped semiconductor can take a wider range of frequencies including nnEMF and convert them into more melanin and regenerative currents. The last blog tells how even people in Chernobyl can turn deadly levels of radiation into melanin on their surfaces. The origins of life were under the full spectrum radiation from the sun including “harmful” UVC.

The simplest way to answer without getting into specifics of different frequencies of 1-5g, quantum effects and electron spin which I'm still trying to wrap my head around. I'd say that nnEMF's diminish the EZ water required to store that usable energy produced by melanin.

Even if someone was in full sun doing all the “right things” but still getting hammered by nnEMF they **would** be getting beneficial infrared light to make ATP and metabolic water but are simultaneously dehydrating semiconductive surfaces and draining their cellular "batteries" via less EZ water. The result is dehyrdated tissues and deuterium being in places it shouldn't resulting in low redox potential, failing mitochondria and ultimately rising heteroplasmy. Certainly there's more negative effects than just that..

I’ve been deep-diving into the Quantum Biology of geographic location and the magnetic environment. We talk a lot about the dangers of non-native EMF (nnEMF) from tech, but I want to get your take on a massive **natural** electromagnetic event: **The Northern Lights (Aurora Borealis) and Geomagnetic Storms.**

Most people see a bucket-list photo op, but looking at it through a Kruse-lens, I’m seeing some potential red flags for our colonies:

-   **Magnetospheric Flux:** During these storms, the Earth’s magnetic field lines are vibrating and shaking. If our mitochondria rely on a stable geomagnetic flux to tune the ATPase, does a G4 or G5 storm essentially "jam" our internal signal?
-   **Light at Night (LAN):** The aurora emits specific frequencies (greens and purples) during a time when the SCN expects total darkness. Is this "natural" blue/green light just as disruptive to the melatonin cycle as a MacBook screen?
-   **The Polar Hole:** Since the magnetosphere is thinnest at the poles, are we getting hit with a "sub-particle soup" that lowers our Redox potential faster than we can recover it, especially since there’s no solar IR/UV during those long winter nights to fix the damage?

**The Question:** Living at these high latitudes, we’re already grinding in a **suboptimal climate** characterized by a massive deficit of solar UV/IR. Even though we’re aggressively using **Cold Thermogenesis (CT)** to generate our own internal **coherent light** and maintain the EZ in our biological water, how does the atmospheric turbulence of a K-index spike factor in?

Is the Aurora essentially a "leaky" atmospheric event that acts as a massive energy drain on our mitochondria?

Curious to hear if I should be pulling the black-out curtains when the skies start glowing.

Thanks

# Thread: Andrew and Kelly's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15

### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370595>

This is the start of Andrew and Kelly’s optimal journal–we’ve been together as a couple for the past 2 years and just moved in together. Our lifestyles are so intermingled we figured we’d do it together– also because Kelly has some stuff going on (she is not an active member on the site), and its a big impetus (as you’ll read) for my desire (and hers) to work on incorporating the protocols from Jack. She is willing and learning though!! Anyways, here's our story, situation and current POA…

### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370597>

Andrew (myself)
History
\-born and raised, and currently living in san diego, ca, currently 34 yo
\-active childhood, played a lot of sports, really liked being outside and active
\-fortunately had a mom who instilled “healthy” habits so I’ve always been interested in doing “healthy” things and wanted to be working in the “health and wellness” space
\-had lazy eye as a child, and had the corrective surgery for it when I was a child (4 yo), after which I wore glasses (transition glasses unfortunately) until age 15, which is when I switched to contacts, but ended up not wearing them because they would fly out playing high school football, and I never noticed a difference with them on/off so I stopped wearing them to this very day and see perfectly fine
\-did not have any mentionable health issues growing up, I was a pretty healthy kid
\-i went through a full vaccination schedule as a child (haven’t had one since age 17 including no covid vaxs)
\-I haven’t been sick since high school outside of two instances catching the covid sickness, otherwise I feel like im fairly resilient and high energy
\-wisdom teeth pulled age 17 for 2nd surgery
\-repaired pec tendon (torn bench pressing) age 24 for 3rd surgery, last time I went under.
\-my professional career has mostly been spent as a personal trainer, working inside gyms, but 3 years ago I became a massage therapist and do mostly fascial/body work now, my work is mobile so I go to clients and have the ability to get lots of sun breaks throughout the day.

Currently
\-overall I feel pretty good in terms of my current physical health-no major issues to speak of-primarily interested in continued overall optimization
\-the only issues I deal with are muscular aches/soreness in my body, and occasional gut (Bowel movement) issues (4-6 on bristol stool chart-i never get constipated) which has been on going since mid-20s-I think it’s more mental/emotional stress related (when i got my first grey hairs-but haven’t gotten more since) but soreness/aches is usually resolved though my own body work practices and I've come to learn how to resolve my gut issues with my sunlight protocol since learning jacks work.
\-I started implementing my current sun protocol last sept when I first found Jack's work, and was successful with the gut issues, like I said, but regressed in between daylight savings times with the lack of sunlight, but since then have been good when I get sunshine on my abdomen.
\-spend a lot of time at the beach or at parks in between clients (i have a lot of autonomy with my work schedule and flow) I also have always enjoyed being outside and active so im very motivated to be-its especially easy to do in san diego near the coast.
\-Diet is very consistent with a BAB that includes carbs (from sweet potato), afternoon snack is a tin of sardines, and a protein/fat based dinner.
\-I also like to surf, go out regularly 1-3xs/week (depending on swell and work schedule), and have been an avid cold plunger for 3 or 4 years now. Otherwise, lots of walking, a movement practice, and strength training maybe 1-2x/week.

Recent blood work (done in march):
I took a NMR lipid panel, omega panel, basic metabolic panel, vit D total/individual, CBC, homocysteine,and hscrp. The following are a list of some of the more significant numbers:
Vitamin d: 36ng/ml
Hscrp 0.2
Homocysteine 7
Total Cholesterol 273
HDL 50
LDL 208
Triglycerides 52
Omega 6/3 ratio 4.1
DHA 5.3H
Fasting glucose: 86

My vit D is obviously the biggest red flag (working on it), I used to think LDL was more of a concern but with some of the things I recently learned from the forum/blogs, I’m not as concerned, especially once we move to a new environment.

### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370598>

Kelly
History
\- currently 31 yo, she grew up in New Jersey, active, athlete, outdoors a lot, was also sick a lot as a kid,
\-started wearing contacts in 3rd grade, been wearing them ever since
\-had anxiety attacks and anemia in 2nd grade
\-had tonsils removed at 6 years old
\-all wisdom teeth are gone
\-had braces growing up, teeth are still crowded years later
\-in middle/high school, had a lot of gastrointestinal issues
\-had multiple retinal holes in both eyes at age 16 and had laser surgery to repair them (no symptoms - detected via an annual eye exam)
\-had the full vaccine schedule as a kid (no vax since 19 including covid)
\-got diagnosed with celiac when she was 20 (2013)
\-lost her period from 2016-2019 and wrecked her hormones trying to get into body building, this also led to cystic acne on her face which continues to today
\-has had constipation since college, which has actually gone away over the past two months
\-stopped body building and got her period back after working with a functional medicine doctor from 2019-2020 but still has the cystic acne
\-when she was 26 she moved to Scottsdale, AZ
\-first few months in AZ is where she started having debilitating menstrual cycles where she’d be in so much pain she couldn't open up her eyes, as the years went on she started having vomiting and diarrhea on day 1 of her cycle, happens on and off for past 6 years
\-when she was 28 she moved to san diego
\-Known family history: (all on father’s side) grandfather had T2 diabetes, grandmother had cataracts, father has CV disease, triple bypass surgery, father had retinal tears in both eyes in April 2025

Currently
\-she currently works remotely, virtual 1-1 work, spends a lot of time on the computer for work
\-5 months ago (Nov 2025) she noticed some small breast pain in the bottom of her left breast, where there was tenderness and a small lump
\-she also has been waking up between 1-4am with intense nausea and panic attacks, happens in luteal phase mainly
\-breast pain occurs more in ovulation phase
\-over the past 5 months that pain has spread into both breasts, her breast size has increased by 2 cups sizes since then, gained 8 lbs since then (she's a small woman at 5ft tall), she feels extremely inflamed
\-eats a BAB, fish in afternoon, and high protein/high fat dinner; predominantly organic/local
\-use reverse osmosis water that is remineralized via pristine hydro water system
\-wear yellow lens while on computer except for while doing 1-1 work (colored lens mess with ability to do 1-1 sessions)
\-in the process of getting rid of contacts and starting to wear glasses more often
\-One bright note is last week she accidentally ate some gluten (bulgar wheat in tabouli), and had no reaction

Main Blood work (done in Feb)
Kelly took a standard lipid panel, omega panel, basic metabolic panel, vit D total/individual, CBC,and hscrp
Some of her notable numbers are:
HSCRP: 1.0
total Vit D: 29ng/ml
Total Cholesterol 191
HDL 64
LDL 113
Triglycerides 47
Omega 6/3 ratio 8.9
DHA 3.5
Fasting glucose 82

Kelly’s HSCRP, vit D are our current biggest concerns (obvious). This combined with her current symptoms has really got us moving towards taking action on relocating sooner than later.

She has not gone to a doctor yet in regards to the breast issue, a friend (who's a nurse) rec’d a mammogram, but after I studied the forum, I suggested not to, and she’s on board with not doing it. There is a location here that does QT imaging, which apparently is “non-radiation breast scan” but 1: we don’t know if doing the scan (assuming it’s safe) will tell us anything we might not already know, and 2: if it’s worth spending $3500 on to do, when that $ could just be used to relocate– which would be the obvious move if it confirms what we might think it is. ### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-11T16:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/homocystine-levels.32531/post-370699>

Altered homocysteine levels are a warning shot for areas on Earth that are dangerous to mammals for the Z axis.

Elevated homocysteine levels are a **Biophysical Rosetta Stone** for cardiovascular markers. I use them when i am looking at risk factors for atraumatic splenic ruptures. The spleen turns over RBCs every 150 days, and this explands when the human is pregnant because women have to raise their RBC mass to support the pregnancy while they also hemodilute their blood by keeping more water in the blood. This happens because pregnancy requires a high dielectric constant to grow trilllions of cells from a sperm and egg. The issue with this is the RBC and water mass puts huge pressure on the spleen and if the blood is loaded with deuterium the spleen can rupture just from the atomic mass is has to handle. This is why I look at **Homocysteine** through the our Human "Vortex" lens in my patients, because honocysteine stops being a "methylation marker" and becomes a **Strain Gauge** for the Z-axis. (heart due to deuterium excess). The arrows mark the splenic rupture. Almost anyone who has a spleen rupture always has PAD somewhere in their system and their hearts are more susceptible to rhythm difficulties. This is why homocysteine is yoked to MI risk = loss of magentic flux.

Homocysteine levels rise precisely when the **Dielectric Constant of the Water Lattice (k)** collapses from **160 down to 78**, usually in response to the **Magnetic Declination I**’ve identified. That is you partners great risk now.

**1\. Homocysteine as a "Sulfur-Charge" Buffer**

Homocysteine is a sulfur-containing amino acid. Sulfur is a "Heavy" element that is critical for **Electron Transfer that I talked about in the melanin blogs in reference to ferrodoxin biology.

The Vortex Function:** In a high-flux, high-vortex environment, homocysteine is rapidly converted into **Cysteine** or **Methionine**. This process requires a high **Proton Motive Force** and efficient **Fractionation**. this means you need UV-NIR while grounded to a strong magnetic field. If any of these are weak homocysteine will rise. Most allopathic MDs have no idea homocysteine is an EXCESS deuterium measure in their tissues from their blood.

Centralized medicine has their head up their asses on this. None of them realizes how the blood is vortexed by the heart to push deuterium to the endothelial surface. It is amazing how ignorant these people are. You do not hate centralized medicine enough.

I'm exposing the "Big Pharma" shell game here folks. By focusing on ApoB and Lp(a) as "villains" to be drugged, they are treating the bandage as the cause of the wound. In my decentralized model, these aren't markers of a "bad diet", they are Dielectric Patch Kits sent to a vessel that is losing its Z-axis charge from the vortexing done by a 4 chambered heart losing its matrix power.

**2\. The "Mitochondrial Flare" (UPEs to nDNA)** The mitochondria are the Rulers of the Lattice. When they stall due to nnEMF or Deuterium loading, they can't communicate via the 9,000 RPM magnetic spin anymore.
The Distress Signal: Instead, they emit Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs). These are high-energy "flares" signaling mitochondrial "fire" (oxidative stress).
The Genetic Trigger: These UPEs hit the nucleus (nDNA) and act as a Photonic Switch, upregulating the production of ApoB and Lp(a). The nucleus isn't "broken"; it is responding to the light-signal that the power plant is failing.

**3\. ApoB/Lp(a) as the "Isotopic Sealant"** Why these specific proteins? Because they are designed to manage Interfacial Water.
The Leak: When nnEMF destroys the endothelial melanin and Deuterium leaches into the vessel wall, the dielectric constant () of the blood-water interface collapses from 160 back to 78.
The Repair: Lp(a) is highly "sticky" and contains disulfide bonds that act as a Charge-Capacitor. It is sent to the site of the "dielectric leak" to prevent the vessel from becoming a pro-oxidative swamp.
The Wallet Biopsy: Dr. Attia and "Centralized Science" see the Lp(a) "scab" and want to strip it off with a drug. But without the Lp(a), the vessel will simply short-circuit, leading to the MI "stall" we discussed in my work.

**4\. The "Elastin-Fibrillin" Deuterium Trap This is the "Smoking Gun" for patients:** Your vessel walls (Elastin) are supposed to be flexible springs. Deuterium replaces light hydrogen in these proteins, turning the "spring" into a "brittle stick." This is how PAD begins. The mechanical "Throb" of the heart now causes micro-fractures in the vessel because the "Lattice" is too heavy. The rise in ApoB is the body trying to "Grout"these fractures. If you look at the splenic rupture pics in here everyone of them had aortic PAD in them.

**5\. Reframing for the Patient:** The "Power Plant" Analogy To explain this to someone being "wallet biopsied": The House (Your Body): Your power plant (Mitochondria) is sparking and failing because you have "dirty electricity" (nnEMF) coming in. The Flare (UPEs): The plant sends up a flare to the main office (nDNA). The Repair Crew (ApoB/Lp(a)): The office sends out a repair crew with duct tape and grout to keep the walls from collapsing. Big Pharma's Solution: They want to shoot the repair crew so you don't see them on the security camera (your blood test), while the power plant continues to burn.

**The Z-axis Stall:** When the magnetic field fails (like in the **SAA** or the high latitudes during a decline), the **Lorentz Lift** on the blood vortex disappears. The "9,000 RP second" motor slows, and the system loses its ability to "spin" sulfur into its coherent, functional forms. Homocysteine "pools" in the blood like **Isotopic Sludge**.

**6\. The "Endothelial Sandpaper" Effect**

High homocysteine is toxic to the endothelium.

**My Thesis:** It isn't just "toxic"; it is **Abrasive**. In a low-vortex environment, the blood becomes **Viscous and Turbulent**.

**The Mechanical Damage:** The "Heavy" homocysteine molecules act like sandpaper against the vessel walls. This triggers the **UPE "Flares"** we discussed, which then call in the **ApoB and Lp(a) "Repair Crews."

The Z-axis Warning:** A high homocysteine level is the body's signal that the **X-axis Mastication** and **Z-axis Magnetic Lift** are insufficient to keep the "Lattice" from becoming a pro-oxidative swamp.

**7\. The 0.66 eV Connection**

The conversion of homocysteine requires **B12, Folate, and B6**.

**The Light Link:** These B-vitamins are **Chromophores**. They absorb specific frequencies of light to facilitate electron transitions.

**The Stall:** If the patient is "Light-Starved" (Fitzpatrick 1, living indoors, blocked Meckel's Cave), they can't provide the **0.66 eV Photonic Kick** needed for these enzymes to work. The Homocysteine rise is the "Check Engine" light for **Internal Tanning Failure**.

**8\. Zipcode Diagnostics: The "Dangerous" Earth**

I have proposed in the blogs that Homocysteine is a warning for **"Dangerous Places."

The Mapping:** If you superimpose a Homocysteine map over the **Magnetic Declination Map**, the highest levels will be in the "Stalls" (like the West/Midwest U.S. or the SAA).

**The 51st North Latitude:** This is where the "Lift" is lowest and the D20 loading is highest. But in the Southern Hemisphere that latitude is now 15 south due to the SAA. The rising Homocysteine is the evidence that the **Mammalian Vortex** can no longer handle the **Local Isotopic Viscosity**.

**My Decentralized Summary

Homocysteine is the "Viscosity Meter" of the blood.** It tells you exactly how much "Magnetic Drag" your system is experiencing. If it's high, your **Z-axis Gravity Well** in your heart is failing, and your "Lattice" is being shredded by its own internal friction. ApoB and Lp(a) are "Adaptive response" proteins to a loss of dielectric constant in the blood. They are the biophysical footprint of a system trying to maintain its Dielectric Integrity in a world of declining magnetic flux and rising electronic noise. This is a band gap collapse of elastin on the intima. Centralized MDs and PhDs people should drawn and quartered for posting BS.

It is the final "Warning Shot" before the **Spleen Rupture** or the **MI "Stall"** occurs. Pregnancy always exacerbates this and the baby is likely going to be born with mitochondrial maladaies tied to deuterium inside the matrix leading to many epigenetic and genetic errors.
